chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. Muhammad Bin Al Hassan Al Saffar said, 'It is narrated to me by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hassan Bin Zayd Bin Ali Bin Al Hussein, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim, and that Allah (a.z.w.j.) loves the seeker of knowledge**.'
arabicText: 1- مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَ طلب العلم فريضة على كُلِّ مُسْلِم أَلَا وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ بُعَادُ الْعِلْمِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. Muhammad Bin Al Hassan Al Saffar said, 'It is narrated to me by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hassan Bin Zayd Bin Ali Bin Al Hussein, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim, and that Allah (a.z.w.j.) loves the seeker of knowledge**.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Isa Bin Abdullah Al Umari, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation under all circumstances**.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٌّ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعُمَرِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Isa Bin Abdullah Al Umari, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation under all circumstances**.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim**.'
arabicText: 3- يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مسلم.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim**.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Isa Bin Abdullah Al Umari, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation from the Obligation of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعُمَرِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ مِنْ فَرَائِضِ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Isa Bin Abdullah Al Umari, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation from the Obligation of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Isa Bin Abdullah, from Ahmad Bin Umar son of Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.), he (a.s.) said: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation from the Obligations of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ع قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ مِنْ فَرَائِضَ الله.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Isa Bin Abdullah, from Ahmad Bin Umar son of Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.), he (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Seeking the knowledge is an obligation from the Obligations of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. He said, 'It was narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Najran and Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Amro Bin Aasim from Al Mufazzal Bin Salim, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Surely, the good teacher, there seek Forgiveness for him, animals of the earth, and fishes of the sea, and all with a soul in the air, and the entirety of the inhabitants of the sky and the earth**, and that the teacher and the student are equal in the Recompense. They will both come on the Day of Qiyamah like two pledged racing horses.'
arabicText: 1- قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي نَحْرَانَ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَاصِمٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص إِنَّ مُعَلَّمَ الْخَيْرِ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ دَوَابُ الْأَرْضِ وَ حِيتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَكُلُّ ذِي رُوحٍ في الهواء و جَمِيعُ أَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ وَ إِنَّ الْعَالِمَ وَ الْمُتَعَلَّمَ فِي الْأَجْرِ سَوَاءٌ يَأْتِيَانِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كَفَرَسَيْ رِهَانٍ يَزْدَحِمَانِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. He said, 'It was narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Najran and Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Amro Bin Aasim from Al Mufazzal Bin Salim, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Surely, the good teacher, there seek Forgiveness for him, animals of the earth, and fishes of the sea, and all with a soul in the air, and the entirety of the inhabitants of the sky and the earth**, and that the teacher and the student are equal in the Recompense. They will both come on the Day of Qiyamah like two pledged racing horses.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Al Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun Al Qaddah, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**One who travels a path in which he seeks knowledge, Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted would Cause him to travel a path to the Paradise**, and the Angels would place down their wings for a seeker of the knowledge being pleased with him, and there would seek Forgiveness for him, ones in the skies and ones in the earth, to the extent of the fishes in the sea. **And a merit of the scholars over the worshipper, is like a merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon**, and that the scholars are inheritors of the Prophets (a.s.). The Prophets (a.s.) do not leave for inheritance Dinars nor Dirhams, but rather they leave the knowledge as an inheritance.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ السَّعِيدِ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَيْمُونِ الْقَدَاحِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ مَنْ سَلَكَ طَرِيقاً يَطْلُبُ فِيهِ عِلْماً سَلَكَ اللهُ تَعَالَى بِهِ طَرِيقاً إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَضَعُ أَجْنِحَتَهَا لِطَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ رِضا بِهِ وَ إِنَّهُ لَيَسْتَغْفِرُ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ مَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ حَتَّى الْحُوتُ فِي الْبَحْرِ وَ فَضْلُ الْعَالِمِ عَلَى الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ عَلَى سَائِرِ النُّجُومِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْرِ وَ إِنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ لَوَرَثَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاء إِنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ لَمْ يُوَرِّثُوا دِينَاراً وَ لَا دِرْهَماً إِنَّمَا وَرَّثُوا الْعِلْمَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Al Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun Al Qaddah, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**One who travels a path in which he seeks knowledge, Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted would Cause him to travel a path to the Paradise**, and the Angels would place down their wings for a seeker of the knowledge being pleased with him, and there would seek Forgiveness for him, ones in the skies and ones in the earth, to the extent of the fishes in the sea. **And a merit of the scholars over the worshipper, is like a merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon**, and that the scholars are inheritors of the Prophets (a.s.). The Prophets (a.s.) do not leave for inheritance Dinars nor Dirhams, but rather they leave the knowledge as an inheritance.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**The seeker of knowledge, all things seek Forgiveness for him, and the fishes in the sea, and the birds in the atmosphere of the sky**.'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : طَالِبُ الْعِلْمِ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَ الْحِيتَانُ فِي الْبِحَارِ وَ الطَّيْرُ فِي حَوَ السَّمَاءِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The seeker of knowledge, all things seek Forgiveness for him, and the fishes in the sea, and the birds in the atmosphere of the sky**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Al Abbas Bin Aamir, from Fuzeyl Bin Usman, from Abu Ubeyda, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**The entirety of the animals of the earth send Salawaat upon the seeker of knowledge, to the extent of the fishes in the sea**.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيَّ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ جَمِيعَ دَوَابٌ الْأَرْضِ لَتُصَلِّي عَلَى طَالِبٍ الْعِلْمِ حَتَّى الْحِيتَانُ فِي الْبَحْرِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Al Abbas Bin Aamir, from Fuzeyl Bin Usman, from Abu Ubeyda, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The entirety of the animals of the earth send Salawaat upon the seeker of knowledge, to the extent of the fishes in the sea**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Amro Bin Shimr who said, 'Jabir narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**The good teacher, there tend to seek Forgiveness for him, animals of the earth, and fishes of the sea, and every small and large in the earth of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His sky**.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شَمْرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي جَابِرٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ مُعَلَّمَ الْخَيْرِ لَتَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ دَوَابٌ الْأَرْضِ وَ حِيتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَكُلُّ صَغِيرَة وَ كَبِيرَةٍ فِي أَرْضِ اللَّهِ وَ سَمَالِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Amro Bin Shimr who said, 'Jabir narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The good teacher, there tend to seek Forgiveness for him, animals of the earth, and fishes of the sea, and every small and large in the earth of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His sky**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Wahab Bin Saeed, from Al Hussein Bin Al Sabbah Al Nakhaie who said, 'It is narrated to me by Jareer Bin Abdullah Al Bajali, from the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) having said: 'Allah (a.z.w.j.) Revealed to me (s.a.w.a.): "**One who travels a way seeking the knowledge in it, I (s.a.w.a.) shall Ease for him a path to the Paradise**." '
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الصَّبَاحِ النَّخَعِي قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي حَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَحَلِيُّ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ص قَالَ: أَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ مَنْ سَلَكَ مَسْلَكاً يَطْلُبُ فِيهِ الْعِلْمَ سَهَّلْتُ لَهُ طَرِيقاً إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Wahab Bin Saeed, from Al Hussein Bin Al Sabbah Al Nakhaie who said, 'It is narrated to me by Jareer Bin Abdullah Al Bajali, from the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah (a.z.w.j.) Revealed to me (s.a.w.a.): "**One who travels a way seeking the knowledge in it, I (s.a.w.a.) shall Ease for him a path to the Paradise**." '

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Suleyman Bin Amro Al Nakhaie, form Abdullah Bin Al Hassan Bin Al Hassan Bin Ali, from his father, from Ali (a.s.) having said: '**The seeker of knowledge is escorted by a thousand Angels from the separate skies saying, 'Lord (a.z.w.j.)! Send Salawaat upon Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ عَمْرٍو النَّخَعِي عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَلِيَّ ع قَالَ: طَالِبُ الْعِلْمِ يُشَيِّعُهُ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ مِنْ مَفْرَقِ السَّمَاءِ يَقُولُونَ رَبِّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Al Hussein Bin Sayf, from his father, from Suleyman Bin Amro Al Nakhaie, form Abdullah Bin Al Hassan Bin Al Hassan Bin Ali, from his father, from Ali (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The seeker of knowledge is escorted by a thousand Angels from the separate skies saying, 'Lord (a.z.w.j.)! Send Salawaat upon Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Mahboub, from Amro Bin Abu Al Miqdam, from Jabir Bin Yazeed Al Jufy, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The teacher and the student are both participants in the Recompense. There are two Recompenses for the teacher, and for the student there is one Recompense**, and there is no good in (anything) besides that.'
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَبِي الْمِقْدَامِ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الْجُعْفِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ الْعَالِمُ وَ الْمُتَعَلَّمُ شَرِيكَانِ فِي الْأَجْرِ لِلْعَالِمِ أَجْرَانِ وَ لِلْمُتَعَلِّمِ أَجْرٌ وَ لَا خَيْرَ فِي سِوَى ذَلِكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Mahboub, from Amro Bin Abu Al Miqdam, from Jabir Bin Yazeed Al Jufy, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The teacher and the student are both participants in the Recompense. There are two Recompenses for the teacher, and for the student there is one Recompense**, and there is no good in (anything) besides that.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Amro Bin Usman, and al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, altogether from Jameel Bin Darraj, from Muhammad Bin Muslim, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**The one from you who learns the knowledge, there would be a Recompense for him similar to the one who taught it, and for him (teacher) would be a merit upon him (student)**. Learn the knowledge from the bearers of knowledge and teach it to your brethren just as the scholars taught you.'
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُثْمَانَ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَالِ جَمِيعاً عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقَالَ: إِنَّ الَّذِي تَعَلَّمَ الْعِلْمَ مِنْكُمْ لَهُ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ الَّذِي يُعَلِّمُهُ وَ لَهُ الْفَضْلُ عَلَيْهِ تَعَلَّمُوا الْعِلْمَ مِنْ حَمَلَةِ الْعِلْمِ وَ عَلَمُوهُ إِخْوَانَكُمْ كَمَا عَلَّمَكُمُ الْعُلَمَاءُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Amro Bin Usman, and al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, altogether from Jameel Bin Darraj, from Muhammad Bin Muslim, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The one from you who learns the knowledge, there would be a Recompense for him similar to the one who taught it, and for him (teacher) would be a merit upon him (student)**. Learn the knowledge from the bearers of knowledge and teach it to your brethren just as the scholars taught you.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ali Bin Asbat, from one of his companion, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said: '**The Momin, the scholar is of a greater Recompense than the Fasting one, the one standing (for Salat), the fighter in the Way of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**, and when he dies, such a crack is cracked in Al-Islam, nothing can fill it up to the Day of Qiyamah.'
arabicText: 10 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ بَعْضٍ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْعَالِمُ أَعْظَمُ أَجْراً مِنَ الصَّائِمِ الْقَائِمِ الْغَازِي فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَ إِذَا مَاتَ تُلِمَ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ ثُلْمَةٌ لَا يَسُدُّهَا شَيْءٌ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ali Bin Asbat, from one of his companion, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Momin, the scholar is of a greater Recompense than the Fasting one, the one standing (for Salat), the fighter in the Way of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**, and when he dies, such a crack is cracked in Al-Islam, nothing can fill it up to the Day of Qiyamah.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**One who teaches a good, for him would be a Recompense similar to the one who acts with it**.' I said, 'Supposing if he were to teach someone else, would that (also) flow for him?' He (a.s.) said: 'If he teaches the people, all of them, it would flow for him (the original teacher).' I said, 'Supposing he has died?' He (a.s.) said: '**And even if he has died**.'
arabicText: 11 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَم عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ مَنْ عَلَّمَ خَيْراً فَلَهُ مِثْلُ أَجْرٍ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهِ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ عَلَّمَهُ غَيْرَهُ يَجْرِي ذَلِكَ لَهُ قَالَ إِنْ عَلَّمَهُ النَّاسَ كُلُّهُمْ جَرَى لَهُ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ مَاتَ قَالَ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**One who teaches a good, for him would be a Recompense similar to the one who acts with it**.' I said, 'Supposing if he were to teach someone else, would that (also) flow for him?' He (a.s.) said: 'If he teaches the people, all of them, it would flow for him (the original teacher).' I said, 'Supposing he has died?' He (a.s.) said: '**And even if he has died**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Fuzeyl Bin Usman, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**The animals of the earth tend to send Salawaat upon the seeker of knowledge, to the extent of the fishes in the sea**.'
arabicText: 12- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ دَوَاتِ الْأَرْضِ لَتُصَلِّي عَلَى طَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ حَتَّى الحيتَانُ فِي الْمَاءِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Fuzeyl Bin Usman, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The animals of the earth tend to send Salawaat upon the seeker of knowledge, to the extent of the fishes in the sea**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Barqy, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from All Bin Yaqteen, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**One who teaches a good, for him would be his Recompense**.' I said, 'Supposing he (learner) were to teach that to someone else?' He (s.a.w.a.) said: 'It would flow for him (teacher), and even if he were to teach the people, all of them.' And one of them has an addition in it: 'I said, 'And if he (teacher) has died?' He (a.s.) said: '**And even if he has died**.'
arabicText: 13 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَقْطِينٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ ع يَقُولُ مَنْ عَلَّمَ خَيْراً فَلَهُ أَجْرُهُ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ عَلَّمَ ذَلِكَ غَيْرَهُ قَالَ يُجْرِي لَهُ وَ إِنْ عَلَّمَهُ النَّاسَ كُلَّهُمْ وَ زَادَ فِيهِ بَعْضُهُمْ قُلْتُ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ قَالَ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Barqy, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from All Bin Yaqteen, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**One who teaches a good, for him would be his Recompense**.' I said, 'Supposing he (learner) were to teach that to someone else?' He (s.a.w.a.) said: 'It would flow for him (teacher), and even if he were to teach the people, all of them.' And one of them has an addition in it: 'I said, 'And if he (teacher) has died?' He (a.s.) said: '**And even if he has died**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Al Hussein Bin Ali Bin Yusuf, from Muqatil Bin Muqatil, from Al Rabie Bin Muhammad Al Muslimy, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**There is none from a servant who moves in seeking the knowledge and strives, except he is splashed from the Mercy with a splashing**.'
arabicText: 14- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يُوسُفَ عَنْ مُقَاتِلِ بْنِ مُقَاتِلٍ عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ الْمُسْلِمِيِّ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يَعْدُو فِي طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ وَ يَرُوحُ إِلَّا خَاصَ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ حَوْضَاً.
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Al Hussein Bin Ali Bin Yusuf, from Muqatil Bin Muqatil, from Al Rabie Bin Muhammad Al Muslimy, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**There is none from a servant who moves in seeking the knowledge and strives, except he is splashed from the Mercy with a splashing**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Barqy, from Suleyman Al Ja'fari, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**The teacher and the student are equal in the Recompense**.'
arabicText: 15 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ الْجَعْفَرِي عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : الْعَالِمُ وَ الْمُتَعَلَّمُ فِي الْأَجْرِ سَوَاءٌ.
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Barqy, from Suleyman Al Ja'fari, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The teacher and the student are equal in the Recompense**.'

chapter: Chapter 2: REWARDS OF THE TEACHER AND THE STUDENT
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Al Hammad Al Harisy, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'A man would come on the Day of Qiyamah and for him would be of the good deeds like the layered clouds, or like the lofty mountains. He will say, 'O Lord (a.z.w.j.)! Where is for me and I did not do these?' He (a.z.w.j.) will Say: "**This is your knowledge which you taught, the people acted with it from after you**."'
arabicText: 16 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَمَّادِ الْحَارِثِي عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ يَجِيءُ الرَّجُلُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ لَهُ مِنَ الْحَسَنَاتِ كَالسَّحَابِ الرِّكَامِ أَوْ كَالْجِبَالِ الرَّوَاسِي فَيَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ أَنَّى لِي هَذَا وَ لَمْ أَعْمَلْهَا فَيَقُولُ هَذَا عِلْمُكَ الَّذِي عَلَّمْتَهُ النَّاسَ يُعْمَلُ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Al Hammad Al Harisy, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'A man would come on the Day of Qiyamah and for him would be of the good deeds like the layered clouds, or like the lofty mountains. He will say, 'O Lord (a.z.w.j.)! Where is for me and I did not do these?' He (a.z.w.j.) will Say: "**This is your knowledge which you taught, the people acted with it from after you**."'

chapter: Chapter 3: RECOGNITION OF THE SCHOLAR WHO, ONE WHO RECOGNISES HIM RECOGNISES ALLAH, AND ONE WHO DENIES HIM DENIES ALLAH THE EXALTED, AND REASON DUE TO WHICH ONE IS HARMONISED TO RECOGNISING HIM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Al Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Sagheer, from the one who narrated it, from Rabie Bin Abdullah, [cite: 3007] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Allah Refused for the things to flow except by the means (medium), so He Made an explanation to be for every medium, and Made a knowledge to be for every explanation, and Made a speaking door to be for every knowledge.** [cite: 3008] One who recognises him recognises him, and one who is ignorant of him, is ignorant of him. [cite: 3009] **That is Rasool-Allah and us.**' [cite: 3010]
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ السَّعِيدِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ صَغِيرٍ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ رِبْعِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ: أَبي اللهُ أَنْ يُجْرِيَ الْأَشْيَاءَ إِلَّا بِالْأَسْبَابِ فَجَعَلَ لِكُلِّ سَبَبٍ شَرْحاً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَرْحٍ عِلْماً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ عِلْمٍ بَاباً ناطقاً عَرَفَهُ مَنْ عَرَفَهُ وَ جَهِلَهُ مَنْ جَهِلَهُ ذلِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص وَ نَحْنُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Al Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Sagheer, from the one who narrated it, from Rabie Bin Abdullah, [cite: 3007]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Allah Refused for the things to flow except by the means (medium), so He Made an explanation to be for every medium, and Made a knowledge to be for every explanation, and Made a speaking door to be for every knowledge.** [cite: 3008] One who recognises him recognises him, and one who is ignorant of him, is ignorant of him. [cite: 3009] **That is Rasool-Allah and us.**' [cite: 3010]

chapter: Chapter 3: RECOGNITION OF THE SCHOLAR WHO, ONE WHO RECOGNISES HIM RECOGNISES ALLAH, AND ONE WHO DENIES HIM DENIES ALLAH THE EXALTED, AND REASON DUE TO WHICH ONE IS HARMONISED TO RECOGNISING HIM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Muhammad Al Qashani, from Muhammad Bin Isa Al Ubeydi, raising it, said, [cite: 3012] 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**Allah Refused the things to flow except by the means, so He Made a means to be for every thing, and Made an explanation to be for every means, and Made a key to be for every explanation, and Made a flag to be for every key, and Made a speaking door to be for every knowledge.** [cite: 3013] One who recognises him recognises Allah, and one who denies him denies Allah. **That is Rasool-Allah and us.**' [cite: 3014]
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْقَاشَانِي عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى الْعُبَيْدِي يَرْفَعُهُ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللهِ ع أَبَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يُجْرِيَ الْأَشْيَاءَ إِلَّا بِالْأَسْبَابِ فَجَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ سَبَباً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ سَبَبٍ شَرْحاً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَرْحِ مِفْتَاحاً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ مِفْتَاحٍ عِلْماً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ عِلْمٍ بَاباً نَاطِقَاً مَنْ عَرَفَهُ عَرَفَ اللَّهَ وَ مَنْ أَنْكَرَهُ أنكر اللَّهَ ذَلِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَ نَحْنُ
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Muhammad Al Qashani, from Muhammad Bin Isa Al Ubeydi, raising it, said, [cite: 3012]
thaqalaynMatn: 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**Allah Refused the things to flow except by the means, so He Made a means to be for every thing, and Made an explanation to be for every means, and Made a key to be for every explanation, and Made a flag to be for every key, and Made a speaking door to be for every knowledge.** [cite: 3013] One who recognises him recognises Allah, and one who denies him denies Allah. **That is Rasool-Allah and us.**' [cite: 3014]

chapter: Chapter 3: RECOGNITION OF THE SCHOLAR WHO, ONE WHO RECOGNISES HIM RECOGNISES ALLAH, AND ONE WHO DENIES HIM DENIES ALLAH THE EXALTED, AND REASON DUE TO WHICH ONE IS HARMONISED TO RECOGNISING HIM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It was narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hussein Bin Al Munzir, from Umar Bin Qays Al Masir, [cite: 3016] 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**Allah did not Leave anything the community could be needy to up to the Day of Qiyamah except He Revealed it in His Book, and Explained it to His Rasool, and Made a limit to be for all things, and Made pointer to point to it.**' [cite: 3017]
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَيْسِ الْمَاصِرِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَدَعْ شَيْئاً يَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهِ الْأُمَّةُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ إِلَّا أَنْزَلَهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ وَ بَيْنَهُ لِرَسُولِهِ وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَداً وَ جَعَلَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا يَدُلُّ عَلَيْهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It was narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hussein Bin Al Munzir, from Umar Bin Qays Al Masir, [cite: 3016]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**Allah did not Leave anything the community could be needy to up to the Day of Qiyamah except He Revealed it in His Book, and Explained it to His Rasool, and Made a limit to be for all things, and Made pointer to point to it.**' [cite: 3017]

chapter: Chapter 3: RECOGNITION OF THE SCHOLAR WHO, ONE WHO RECOGNISES HIM RECOGNISES ALLAH, AND ONE WHO DENIES HIM DENIES ALLAH THE EXALTED, AND REASON DUE TO WHICH ONE IS HARMONISED TO RECOGNISING HIM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. And it is reported by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Yahya Bin Abu Imran, from Yunus, from Al Hussein Bin Munzir, from Umar Bin Qays, [cite: 3028] 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) - similar to that.' [cite: 3029]
arabicText: 4 وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنَ مُنْذِرٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. And it is reported by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Yahya Bin Abu Imran, from Yunus, from Al Hussein Bin Munzir, from Umar Bin Qays, [cite: 3028]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) - similar to that.' [cite: 3029]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, and Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, [cite: 3043] 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**A scholar benefiting (others) with his knowledge is superior to worship of seventy thousand years by a worshipper.**' [cite: 3044]
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ وَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: عَالِمٌ يُنْتَفَعُ بِعِلْمِهِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ عِبَادَةِ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ عَابِدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, and Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, [cite: 3043]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**A scholar benefiting (others) with his knowledge is superior to worship of seventy thousand years by a worshipper.**' [cite: 3044]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun, [cite: 3046] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon.**'' [cite: 3047]
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص فَضْلُ العَالِمِ عَلَى الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ عَلَى سَائِرِ النُّجُومِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْرِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun, [cite: 3046]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**Merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon.**'' [cite: 3047]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. And from him, by this chain, said, '**Merit of the scholar is more beloved to me than merit of the worship.**' [cite: 3049]
arabicText: 3- وَ عَنْهُ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ : فَضْلُ الْعَالِم أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ فَضْلِ الْعِبَادَةِ
thaqalaynSanad: 3. And from him, by this chain, said, [cite: 3049]
thaqalaynMatn: '**Merit of the scholar is more beloved to me than merit of the worship.**' [cite: 3049]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, and Zayd, from Al Rawandy, [cite: 3051] 'From Ja'far Bin Muhammad (a.s.) having said: '**The knowledge would come in front of the worshipper at a hill of travel distance of five hundred years.**' [cite: 3052]
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ وَ زَيْدٌ عَنِ الرَّاوَنْدِي عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ع قَالَ : يَأْتِي صَاحِبُ الْعِلْمِ قُدَّامَ الْعَابِدِ بِرَبِّوَةٍ مَسِيرَةً خَمْسٍ مِائَةِ عَامٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hassan, and Zayd, from Al Rawandy, [cite: 3051]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Ja'far Bin Muhammad (a.s.) having said: '**The knowledge would come in front of the worshipper at a hill of travel distance of five hundred years.**' [cite: 3052]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, [cite: 3054] 'From Ali Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.), or from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**One pondering in the Religion is severer upon the Satan than worship of a thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3066]
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةً عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ أَوْ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مُتَفَقَّهُ فِي الدِّينِ أَشَدُّ عَلَى الشَّيْطَانِ مِنْ عِبَادَةِ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, [cite: 3054]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Ali Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.), or from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**One pondering in the Religion is severer upon the Satan than worship of a thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3066]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Sa'dan Bin Muslim, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said, [cite: 3068] 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), 'A man reporting your Ahadeeth, sending that to the people, and confining it in the hearts of your Shias, and maybe there is a worshipper from your Shia who hasn't this narrating, which of the two is superior?' [cite: 3069] He (a.s.) said: '**The narrator of our Ahadeeth sending among the people and confining it in the hearts of our Shias is superior than a thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3071]
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ سَعْدَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَ رَجُلٌ رَاوِيَةٌ لِحَدِيثِكُمْ يَبْتُ ذلِكَ إِلَى النَّاسِ وَ يُسَدِّدُهُ فِي قُلُوبِ شِيعَتِكُمْ وَ لَعَلَّ عَابِداً مِنْ شِيعَتِكُمْ لَيْسَتْ لَهُ هَذِهِ الرِّوَايَةُ أَيُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ قَالَ الرَّاوِيَةُ حَدِيثًا يَبْتُ فِي النَّاسِ وَ يُسَدِّدُهُ فِي قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِنَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Sa'dan Bin Muslim, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said, [cite: 3068]
thaqalaynMatn: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), 'A man reporting your Ahadeeth, sending that to the people, and confining it in the hearts of your Shias, and maybe there is a worshipper from your Shia who hasn't this narrating, which of the two is superior?' [cite: 3069] He (a.s.) said: '**The narrator of our Ahadeeth sending among the people and confining it in the hearts of our Shias is superior than a thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3071]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa Bin Yunus Bin Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, [cite: 3073] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'When it will be the Day of Qiyamah, Allah Mighty and Majestic will Resurrect the scholar and the worshipper. [cite: 3074] When they stand in front of Allah, Allah will Say to the worshipper: "**Go to the Paradise!**" [cite: 3075] And He will Say to the scholar: "**Intercede for the people due to your beautiful educating to them!**"' [cite: 3076]
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى بْنِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَمَّنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيَامَةِ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ الْعَالِمَ وَ الْعَابِدَ فَإِذَا وَقَفَا بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ قَالَ لِلْعَابِدِ الْطَلِقَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ قِيلَ لِلْعَالِم فَاشْفَعْ لِلنَّاسِ بِحُسْنِ تَأْدِيبِكَ هُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa Bin Yunus Bin Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, [cite: 3073]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'When it will be the Day of Qiyamah, Allah Mighty and Majestic will Resurrect the scholar and the worshipper. [cite: 3074] When they stand in front of Allah, Allah will Say to the worshipper: "**Go to the Paradise!**" [cite: 3075] And He will Say to the scholar: "**Intercede for the people due to your beautiful educating to them!**"' [cite: 3076]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Umar Bin Musa, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas'ada Bin Ziyad, [cite: 3078] 'From Ja'far (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) that the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The superiority of the scholar over the worshipper is like the superiority of the sun over the stars**, and superiority of the worshipper over the non-worshippers is like the superiority of the moon over the stars.' [cite: 3079]
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَةَ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ص قَالَ: إِنَّ فَضْلَ الْعَالِمِ عَلَى الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الشَّمْسِ عَلَى الْكَوَاكِبِ وَ فَضْلَ الْعَابِدِ عَلَى غَيْرِ الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ عَلَى الْكَوَاكِبِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Umar Bin Musa, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas'ada Bin Ziyad, [cite: 3078]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Ja'far (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) that the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The superiority of the scholar over the worshipper is like the superiority of the sun over the stars**, and superiority of the worshipper over the non-worshippers is like the superiority of the moon over the stars.' [cite: 3079]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from the one who mentioned it, [cite: 3081] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**A scholar is superior than a thousand worshippers, and a thousand ascetics**.' [cite: 3093] And he (a.s.) said: '**A scholar benefitting (others) by his knowledge is superior than worship of seventy thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3095]
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَمَّنْ ذَكَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: عَالِمٌ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ وَ أَلْفِ زَاهِدٍ و قَالَ ع عَالِمٌ يُنْتَفَعُ بِعِلْمِهِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ عِبَادَةٍ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ عَابِدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from the one who mentioned it, [cite: 3081]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**A scholar is superior than a thousand worshippers, and a thousand ascetics**.' [cite: 3093] And he (a.s.) said: '**A scholar benefitting (others) by his knowledge is superior than worship of seventy thousand worshippers.**' [cite: 3095]

chapter: Chapter 4: MERITS OF THE SCHOLAR OVER THE WORSHIPPER
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Mahboub, from Muawiya Bin Waheyb who said, [cite: 3097] 'I asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) about two men, one of them an understanding narrator of Ahadeeth, and the other a worshipper, there isn't for him like his narrations'. [cite: 3098] He (a.s.) said: '**The narrator of the Ahadeeth, the one pondering in the Religion is superior than a thousand worshippers, there neither being any understanding for him nor narrating.**' [cite: 3100]
arabicText: 10 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ رَجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَا فَقِيةٌ رَاوِيَةٌ لِلْحَدِيثِ وَ الْآخِرُ عَابِدٌ لَيْسَ لَهُ مِثْلُ رِوَايَتِهِ فَقَالَ الرَّاوِيَةُ لِلْحَدِيثِ الْمُتَفَقَّهُ فِي الدِّينِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ لَا فِقْهَ لَهُ وَ لَا رِوَايَةَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Mahboub, from Muawiya Bin Waheyb who said, [cite: 3097]
thaqalaynMatn: 'I asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) about two men, one of them an understanding narrator of Ahadeeth, and the other a worshipper, there isn't for him like his narrations'. [cite: 3098] He (a.s.) said: '**The narrator of the Ahadeeth, the one pondering in the Religion is superior than a thousand worshippers, there neither being any understanding for him nor narrating.**' [cite: 3100]

chapter: Chapter 5: THE PEOPLE ARE CATEGORISED UPON THREE A SCHOLAR, AND A STUDENT, AND SCUM, AND THAT THE IMAMS FROM PROGENY OF MUHAMMAD, THEY ARE THE SCHOLAR, AND THEIR SHIAS ARE THE STUDENTS, AND REST OF THE PEOPLE ARE SCUM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Yahya Bin Abu Imran, from Yunus, from Jameel who said, [cite: 3110] 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: 'The people are categorised upon three types a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3111] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3112]
arabicText: -1- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ جَمِيلٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ يَعْدُو النَّاسُ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةِ صُنُوفٍ عَالِم وَ مُتَعَلِّم وَ عُنَاءٍ فَنَحْنُ الْعُلَمَاءُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا الْمُتَعَلِّمُونَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ غُثَاءُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Yahya Bin Abu Imran, from Yunus, from Jameel who said, [cite: 3110]
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: 'The people are categorised upon three types a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3111] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3112]

chapter: Chapter 5: THE PEOPLE ARE CATEGORISED UPON THREE A SCHOLAR, AND A STUDENT, AND SCUM, AND THAT THE IMAMS FROM PROGENY OF MUHAMMAD, THEY ARE THE SCHOLAR, AND THEIR SHIAS ARE THE STUDENTS, AND REST OF THE PEOPLE ARE SCUM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to me by Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Al Abbas Bin Aalmir, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir, [cite: 3114] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are two (types of) men a scholar and a strudent, and rest of the people are scum. [cite: 3115] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3116]
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنِي الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيَّ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَهُ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ النَّاسَ رَجُلَانِ عَالِمٌ وَ مُتَعَلَّمُ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ غُنَاء فَنَحْنُ الْعُلَمَاءُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا الْمُتَعَلِّمُونَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ غُنَاء.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to me by Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Al Abbas Bin Aalmir, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir, [cite: 3114]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are two (types of) men a scholar and a strudent, and rest of the people are scum. [cite: 3115] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3116]

chapter: Chapter 5: THE PEOPLE ARE CATEGORISED UPON THREE A SCHOLAR, AND A STUDENT, AND SCUM, AND THAT THE IMAMS FROM PROGENY OF MUHAMMAD, THEY ARE THE SCHOLAR, AND THEIR SHIAS ARE THE STUDENTS, AND REST OF THE PEOPLE ARE SCUM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim, from Salim, [cite: 3118] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are categorised upon three - a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3119] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3120]
arabicText: -3- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ عَنْ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : النَّاسُ يَعْدُونَ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةٍ عَالِم وَ مُتَعَلَّمٍ وَ عُنَاءٍ فَنَحْنُ الْعُلَمَاءُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا الْمُتَعَلِّمُونَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ غُثَاءٌ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim, from Salim, [cite: 3118]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are categorised upon three - a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3119] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3120]

chapter: Chapter 5: THE PEOPLE ARE CATEGORISED UPON THREE A SCHOLAR, AND A STUDENT, AND SCUM, AND THAT THE IMAMS FROM PROGENY OF MUHAMMAD, THEY ARE THE SCHOLAR, AND THEIR SHIAS ARE THE STUDENTS, AND REST OF THE PEOPLE ARE SCUM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Abdul Hameed, from Sayf Bin Ameyra who said, [cite: 3122] 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: 'The people are categorised upon three a scholar, and a student, and scum'. [cite: 3133] They asked him (a.s.) about that, so he (a.s.) said: '**We are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3134]
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةً قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو سَلَمَةَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ يَعْدُو النَّاسُ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةٍ عَالِم وَ مُتَعَلَّمٍ وَ غُنَاء فَسَأَلُوهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ نَحْنُ الْعُلَمَاءُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا الْمُتَعَلِّمُونَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ غُنَاء.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Abdul Hameed, from Sayf Bin Ameyra who said, [cite: 3122]
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: 'The people are categorised upon three a scholar, and a student, and scum'. [cite: 3133] They asked him (a.s.) about that, so he (a.s.) said: '**We are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3134]

chapter: Chapter 5: THE PEOPLE ARE CATEGORISED UPON THREE A SCHOLAR, AND A STUDENT, AND SCUM, AND THAT THE IMAMS FROM PROGENY OF MUHAMMAD, THEY ARE THE SCHOLAR, AND THEIR SHIAS ARE THE STUDENTS, AND REST OF THE PEOPLE ARE SCUM
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Ahmad Bin Aiz, from Abu Khadeeja, [cite: 3136] 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are called upon three a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3137] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3138]
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ الْوَشَاءِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَائِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي خَدِيجَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ النَّاسَ يَعْدُونَ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةٍ عَالِم وَ مُتَعَلَّم وَ غُنَاء فَنَحْنُ الْعُلَمَاءُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا الْمُتَعَلِّمُونَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ عُثَاءٌ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Ahmad Bin Aiz, from Abu Khadeeja, [cite: 3136]
thaqalaynMatn: 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'The people are called upon three a scholar, and a student, and scum. [cite: 3137] **So, we are the scholars, and our Shias are the students, and rest of the people are scum.**' [cite: 3138]

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to me by Al-Sindy Bin Muhammad, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abdullah Suleyman who said, 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and there was a man from the people of Al-Basra with him (a.s.) called 'Usman the blind', and he was saying, 'Al-Hassan Al-Basry alleges that the ones who conceal the knowledge, the stench from their bellies would even harm the inhabitants of the Fire'. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: '**Then the believer of the people of Pharaoh is destroyed! And the knowledge has never ceased to be concealed since Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Noah (a.s.). Let Al-Hassan go right and left, for by Allah (a.z.w.j.), he will not find the knowledge except over here**.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي السَّنْدِي بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ سُلَيْمَانَ قَالَ : سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ عِنْدَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْبَصْرَةِ يُقَالُ لَهُ عُثْمَانُ الْأَعْمَى وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَ الْبَصْرِيَّ يَزْعُمُ أَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ الْعِلْمَ يُؤْذِي رِيحُ بُطُونِهِمْ أَهْلَ النَّارِ فقالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع فَهَلَكَ إِذَا مُؤْمِنُ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَ مَا زَالَ الْعِلْمُ مَكْتُوماً منذ بعث الله نُوحاً ع فَلْيَذْهَبِ الحَسَنُ يميناً وَ شِمَالًا فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا يُوجَدُ الْعِلْمُ إِلَّا هاهنا.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to me by Al-Sindy Bin Muhammad, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abdullah Suleyman who said, 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and there was a man from the people of Al-Basra with him (a.s.) called 'Usman the blind', and he was saying, 'Al-Hassan Al-Basry alleges that the ones who conceal the knowledge, the stench from their bellies would even harm the inhabitants of the Fire'. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Then the believer of the people of Pharaoh is destroyed! And the knowledge has never ceased to be concealed since Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Noah (a.s.). Let Al-Hassan go right and left, for by Allah (a.z.w.j.), he will not find the knowledge except over here**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to me by Abu Ja'far Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Saeed, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Bin Al Halby, from Moalla Bin Abu Usman, from Abu Baseer, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said to me: '**Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba is from the ones Allah (a.z.w.j.) Said: And from the people there are ones who are saying: We believe in Allah and in the Last Day; and they are not at all Believers [2:8]. So, let Al-Hakam go to the east and to the west, but by Allah (a.z.w.j.), he will not achieve the knowledge except from People (a.s.) of the Household, Jibraeel (a.s.) descended unto them**.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّضْرِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ مُعَلَّى بْنِ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع قَالَ: قَالَ لِي إِنَّ الحَكَمَ بْن عُتَيْبَةَ مِمَّنْ قَالَ اللهُ وَ مِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَ بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَ ما هُم بِمُؤْمِنِينَ فَلْيُشَرِّقِ الْحَكَمُ وَ لِّيُعَرِّبُ أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ لَا يُصِيبُ الْعِلْمَ إِلَّا مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ نَزَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ جَبْرَئِيل ..
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to me by Abu Ja'far Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Saeed, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Bin Al Halby, from Moalla Bin Abu Usman, from Abu Baseer, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said to me:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba is from the ones Allah (a.z.w.j.) Said: And from the people there are ones who are saying: We believe in Allah and in the Last Day; and they are not at all Believers [2:8]. So, let Al-Hakam go to the east and to the west, but by Allah (a.z.w.j.), he will not achieve the knowledge except from People (a.s.) of the Household, Jibraeel (a.s.) descended unto them**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to me by Al Sindy Bin Muhammad, and Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about the testimony of a child of adultery (bastard), 'Is it allowed?' He (a.s.) said: 'No'. I said, 'Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba claims that it is allowed'. He (a.s.) said: '**O Allah (a.z.w.j.)! Do not Forgive his sins for him. What did Allah (a.z.w.j.) Say for Al-Hakam: And it is a Zikr for you and for your people, and soon you shall be Questioned [43:44]. So, let Al-Hakam go right and left. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! He will not find the knowledge except from People (a.s.) of a Household, Jibraeel (a.s.) descended unto them**.'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنِي السَّنْدِي بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَ عَنْ شَهَادَةٍ وَلَدِ الزناء تَجُوزُ قَالَ لَا فَقُلْتُ إِنَّ الحَكَمَ بْنَ عُتَيْبَةَ يَزْعُمُ أَنَّهَا تَجُورُ فَقَالَ اللَّهُمَّ لَا تَغْفِرْ لَهُ ذَنْبَهُ مَا قَالَ اللهُ لِلْحَكَمِ إِنَّهُ لَذِكْرٌ لَكَ وَ لِقَوْمِكَ وَ سَوْفَ تُسْأَلُونَ فَلْيَذْهَبِ الْحَكَمُ يميناً وَ شِمَالًا فَوَ اللَّهِ لَا يُوجَدُ الْعِلْمُ إِلَّا مِنْ أهْلِ بَيْتٍ نَزَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ جَبْرَئِيل.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to me by Al Sindy Bin Muhammad, and Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abu Baseer who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about the testimony of a child of adultery (bastard), 'Is it allowed?' He (a.s.) said: 'No'. I said, 'Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba claims that it is allowed'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O Allah (a.z.w.j.)! Do not Forgive his sins for him. What did Allah (a.z.w.j.) Say for Al-Hakam: And it is a Zikr for you and for your people, and soon you shall be Questioned [43:44]. So, let Al-Hakam go right and left. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! He will not find the knowledge except from People (a.s.) of a Household, Jibraeel (a.s.) descended unto them**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Ali, from Abu Is'haq Sa'alba, from Abu Maryam who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to Salama Bin Koheyl and Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba: '**Whether both of you (go) east and west, you will not find correct knowledge except something emerging from us (a.s.), People (a.s.) of the Household**.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ثَعْلَبَةَ عَنْ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع لِسَلَمَةَ بْنِ كَهَيْلٍ وَ الْحَكَمِ بْنِ عُتَيْبَةَ شَرِّقَا وَ غَرْبًا لَنْ تَحِدًا عِلْماً صَحِيحاً إِلَّا شَيْئاً يَخْرُجُ مِنْ عِنْدِنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Ali, from Abu Is'haq Sa'alba, from Abu Maryam who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to Salama Bin Koheyl and Al-Hakam Bin Uteyba: '**Whether both of you (go) east and west, you will not find correct knowledge except something emerging from us (a.s.), People (a.s.) of the Household**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Al Fazl, from Musa Bin Al Qasim, from Imad Bin Isa, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said, 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying, and a man from the people of Al-Basra had asked him (a.s.) saying, 'Usman the blind is reporting from Al-Hassan (Al-Basry) that those who are concealing the knowledge, the stench of their bellies would hurt (even) the inhabitants of the Fire'. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: '**Then the believer of the People of Pharaoh is destroyed. They are lying. That (stench) is from the private parts of the adulteresses, and the knowledge has not ceased to be concealed after the son of Adam (a.s.) was killed. So, let Al-Hassan go right and left, he will not find the knowledge except with People (a.s.) of the knowledge, those Jibraeel (a.s.) descended upon**.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ عِمَادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ وَ سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْبَصْرَةِ فَقَالَ إِنَّ عُثْمَانَ الْأَعْمَى يَرْوِي عَنْ الْحَسَنِ أَنْ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ الْعِلْمَ تُؤْذِي رِيحُ بُطُونِهِمْ أَهْلَ النَّارِ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع فَهَلَكَ إِذا مُؤْمِنُ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ كَذَبُوا إِنَّ ذَلِكَ مِنْ فُرُوحِ الزُّنَاةِ وَ مَا زَالَ الْعِلْمُ مَكْتُوماً قَبْلَ قَتْلِ ابْنِ آدَمَ فَلْيَذْهَبِ الْحَسَنُ يَمِيناً وَ شِمَالًا لَا يُوجَدُ الْعِلْمُ إِلَّا عِنْدَ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ الَّذِينَ نَزَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ جَبْرَئِيلُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Al Fazl, from Musa Bin Al Qasim, from Imad Bin Isa, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said, 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying, and a man from the people of Al-Basra had asked him (a.s.) saying, 'Usman the blind is reporting from Al-Hassan (Al-Basry) that those who are concealing the knowledge, the stench of their bellies would hurt (even) the inhabitants of the Fire'. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Then the believer of the People of Pharaoh is destroyed. They are lying. That (stench) is from the private parts of the adulteresses, and the knowledge has not ceased to be concealed after the son of Adam (a.s.) was killed. So, let Al-Hassan go right and left, he will not find the knowledge except with People (a.s.) of the knowledge, those Jibraeel (a.s.) descended upon**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Al Hussein Bin Usman, from Yahya Bin Al Halby, from his father, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: 'A man said, and I was with him (a.s.), 'Al-Hassan Al-Basry is reporting that Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'One who conceals knowledge would come on the Day of Qiyamah reined with reins of fire'. He (a.s.) said: '**He lied, woe be unto him! So where are the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): And a Momin man from the people of Pharaoh who had concealed his Eman said, 'Will you kill a man who is saying, 'My Lord is Allah', [40:28]?'** Then Abu Ja'far (a.s.) extended his voice with it and said: '**Let them go wherever they so desire to. But, by Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They will not be finding the knowledge except over here!**' Then he (a.s.) was silent for a while, then Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: '**With Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَالِ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: قَالَ رَجُلٌ وَ أَنَا عِنْدَهُ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَ الْبَصْرِيَّ يَرْوِي أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص قَالَ مَنْ كَتَمَ عِلْماً جَاءَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مُلْحَما بِلِجَامٍ مِنَ النَّارِ قَالَ كَذَبَ وَيْحَهُ فَأَيْنَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ قَالَ رَجُلٌ مُؤْمِنٌ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إِيمَانَهُ أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلًا أَنْ يَقُولَ رَبِّيَ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ مَدَّ بِمَا أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع صَوْتَهُ فَقَالَ لِيَذْهَبُوا حَيْثُ شَاءُوا أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ لَا يَجِدُونَ الْعِلْمَ إِلَّا هاهنا ثُمَّ سَكَتَ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع عِنْدَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Al Hussein Bin Usman, from Yahya Bin Al Halby, from his father, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: 'A man said, and I was with him (a.s.), 'Al-Hassan Al-Basry is reporting that Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'One who conceals knowledge would come on the Day of Qiyamah reined with reins of fire'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**He lied, woe be unto him! So where are the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): And a Momin man from the people of Pharaoh who had concealed his Eman said, 'Will you kill a man who is saying, 'My Lord is Allah', [40:28]?'** Then Abu Ja'far (a.s.) extended his voice with it and said: '**Let them go wherever they so desire to. But, by Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They will not be finding the knowledge except over here!**' Then he (a.s.) was silent for a while, then Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: '**With Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What the people have been commanded with seeking the knowledge from its extractors, and its extractors are Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. (Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to me by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Abu Al Bakhtari and Sindi Bin Muhammad, from Abu Al Bakhtari, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.): '**The scholars are inheritors of the Prophets (a.s.), and that is because the Prophets do not leave for inheritance, neither gold nor Dinar, and rather they leave for inheritance, Ahadeeth from their Ahadeeth. The one who takes anything from it, so he has taken an abundant share. Therefore, look at this knowledge of yours, who have you taken it from, regarding us (a.s.), People (a.s.) of the Household. In every replacement (Imam a.s.) there would be an equaliser, negating from it the alterations of the exaggerators, and plagiarism of the falsifiers, and interpretations of the ignoramuses**.'
arabicText: نادر من الباب: 1- حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْبَحْتَرِي وَ سِنْدِي بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْبَحْتَرِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ وَرَثَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ ذلِكَ أَنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ لَمْ يُوَرَثُوا دِرْهَماً وَ لَا دِينَاراً وَ إِنَّمَا وَرَّثُوا أَحَادِيثَ مِنْ أَحَادِيثِهِمْ فَمَنْ أَحَدَ شَيْئًا مِنْهَا فَقَدْ أَخَذَ حَظًّا وَافِراً فَانْظُرُوا عِلْمَكُمْ هَذَا عَمَّنْ تَأْخُذُونَهُ فَإِنَّ فِينَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فِي كُلِّ خَلَفٍ عُدُولًا يَنْقُونَ عَنْهُ تَحْرِيفَ الْغَالِينَ وَ انْتِحَالَ الْمُبْطِلِينَ وَ تَأْوِيلَ الْجَاهِلِينَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. (Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to me by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Abu Al Bakhtari and Sindi Bin Muhammad, from Abu Al Bakhtari, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.):
thaqalaynMatn: '**The scholars are inheritors of the Prophets (a.s.), and that is because the Prophets do not leave for inheritance, neither gold nor Dinar, and rather they leave for inheritance, Ahadeeth from their Ahadeeth. The one who takes anything from it, so he has taken an abundant share. Therefore, look at this knowledge of yours, who have you taken it from, regarding us (a.s.), People (a.s.) of the Household. In every replacement (Imam a.s.) there would be an equaliser, negating from it the alterations of the exaggerators, and plagiarism of the falsifiers, and interpretations of the ignoramuses**.'

chapter: Chapter 7: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.). The knowledge is to be drawn from them (a.s.), and they (a.s.) are scholars neither being unjust nor ignorant
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Sabbah Al Muzany, from Al Haris Bin Haseyra Al Hakam Bin Uteyba who said, 'A man met Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) at Al-Talbiya, and he (a.s.) was intending Karbala. He entered to see him (a.s.) and greeted to him (a.s.). Al-Hussein (a.s.) said to him: 'Which city are you from?' He said, 'From the people of Al-Kufa'. He (a.s.) said: '**O brother from the people of Al-Kufa! But, by Allah (a.z.w.j.), if you had met me (a.s.) at Al-Medina, I (a.s.) would have shown you the traces of Jibraeel (a.s.) from our (a.s.) house and his (a.s.) descent upon my (a.s.) grandfather (s.a.w.a.) with the Revelation. O brother from the people of Al-Kufa! The knowledge is drawn from our (a.s.) possession. Are they (people) knowing and we (a.s.) are ignorant? This is what cannot happen to be!**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ صَبَّاحِ الْمُزَنِي عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ حَصِيرَةً عَنِ الْحَكَمِ بْنِ عُتَيْبَةَ قَالَ: لَقِيَ رَجُلٌ الْحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيَّ عِ بِالثَّعْلَبِيَّةِ وَ هُوَ يُرِيدُ كَرْبَلَاء فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْحُسَيْنُ ع مِنْ أَيِّ الْبُلْدَانِ أَنْتَ فَقَالَ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ قَالَ يَا أَخَا أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ لَوْ لَقِيتُكَ بِالْمَدِينَةِ لَأَرَيْتُكَ أَثَرَ جَبْرَئِيلَ مِنْ دَارِنَا وَ نُزُولِهِ عَلَى جَدِّي بِالْوَحْيِ يَا أَخَا أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ مُسْتَقَى الْعِلْمِ مِنْ عِنْدِنَا أَفَعَلِمُوا وَ جَهِلْنَا هَذَا مَا لَا يَكُونُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Sabbah Al Muzany, from Al Haris Bin Haseyra Al Hakam Bin Uteyba who said, 'A man met Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) at Al-Talbiya, and he (a.s.) was intending Karbala. He entered to see him (a.s.) and greeted to him (a.s.). Al-Hussein (a.s.) said to him: 'Which city are you from?' He said, 'From the people of Al-Kufa'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O brother from the people of Al-Kufa! But, by Allah (a.z.w.j.), if you had met me (a.s.) at Al-Medina, I (a.s.) would have shown you the traces of Jibraeel (a.s.) from our (a.s.) house and his (a.s.) descent upon my (a.s.) grandfather (s.a.w.a.) with the Revelation. O brother from the people of Al-Kufa! The knowledge is drawn from our (a.s.) possession. Are they (people) knowing and we (a.s.) are ignorant? This is what cannot happen to be!**'

chapter: Chapter 7: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.). The knowledge is to be drawn from them (a.s.), and they (a.s.) are scholars neither being unjust nor ignorant
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Al Haysam Al Kufy, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Ibn Harasa Al Shaybani, from a sheikh from the people of Al Kufa who said, 'I saw Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.) at Mina. He (a.s.) said: 'From where is the man?' I said, 'A man from the people Al-Iraq'. He (a.s.) said to me: '**O brother from the people of Al-Iraq! But, had you been with us (a.s.) at Al-Medina, I (a.s.) would have shown you the places of Jibraeel (a.s.) from our (a.s.) house. The people have learned the knowledge from us (a.s.), and you see them as knowing and us (a.s.) as ignorant?**'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا الهَيْثَمُ النَّهْدِي الْكُوفِي عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ عَنِ ابْنِ هَرَاسَةَ الشَّيْبَانِي عَنْ شَيْرٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَلِيَّ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ عَ مِنِّى فَقَالَ مِمَّنِ الرَّجُلُ فَقُلْتُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ فَقَالَ لِي يَا أَحَا أَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ أَمَا لَوْ كُنْتَ عِنْدَنَا بِالْمَدِينَةِ لَأَرَيْنَاكَ مَوَاطِنَ جَبْرَئِيلَ مِنْ دُوَيْرِنَا اسْتَقَانَا النَّاسُ الْعِلْمَ فَتَرَاهُمْ عَلِمُوا وَ جَهِلْنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Al Haysam Al Kufy, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Ibn Harasa Al Shaybani, from a sheikh from the people of Al Kufa who said, 'I saw Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.) at Mina. He (a.s.) said: 'From where is the man?' I said, 'A man from the people Al-Iraq'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said to me: '**O brother from the people of Al-Iraq! But, had you been with us (a.s.) at Al-Medina, I (a.s.) would have shown you the places of Jibraeel (a.s.) from our (a.s.) house. The people have learned the knowledge from us (a.s.), and you see them as knowing and us (a.s.) as ignorant?**'

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Al Qasim Bin Suleyman, from Al Moalla Bin Khuneys, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]: '**Meaning one who takes his opinions as his religion without guidance from Imams (a.s.) from the Imams (a.s.) of guidance**.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّضْرِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنِ الْمُعَلَّى بْنِ حُنَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ حَلَ وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَواهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ يَعْنِي مَنْ يَتَّخِذُ دِينَهُ رَأْيَهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدَى أَئِمَّةٍ مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَى.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Al Qasim Bin Suleyman, from Al Moalla Bin Khuneys, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Meaning one who takes his opinions as his religion without guidance from Imams (a.s.) from the Imams (a.s.) of guidance**.'

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. And from him, from Al Hussein, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, 'From Abu Al-Hassanasws regarding Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]: 'Meaning one who **takes his opinions as his religion, without guidance from the Imamsasws of guidance**'.
arabicText: 2- وَ عَنْهُ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَل وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ يَعْنِي مَنِ اتَّخَذَ دِينَهُ رَأْيَهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَى.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from him, from Al Hussein, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, 'From Abu Al-Hassanasws regarding Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Meaning one who **takes his opinions as his religion, without guidance from the Imamsasws of guidance**'.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Nazr Bin Shuayb, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazl, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'farasws about Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]. Heasws said: 'Allahazwj Means by it **one who takes his opinions as his religion from without an Imamasws from the Imamsasws of Guidance**'.
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ نَضْرِ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِي قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ قَالَ عَلَى اللَّهُ بِمَا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ دِينَهُ رَأْيَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ إِمَامٍ مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَى.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Nazr Bin Shuayb, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazl, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'farasws about Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50].
thaqalaynMatn: Heasws said: 'Allahazwj Means by it **one who takes his opinions as his religion from without an Imamasws from the Imamsasws of Guidance**'.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Al Hajjal, from Ghalib Al Nahwy, 'From Abu Abdullahasws regarding Words of Allahazwj the Exalted: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]. Heasws said: '**He takes his opinions as his religion**'.
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنِ الْحَجَّالِ عَنْ غَالِبِ النَّحْوِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ قَالَ اتَّخَذَ رَأْيَهُ دِيناً.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Al Hajjal, from Ghalib Al Nahwy, 'From Abu Abdullahasws regarding Words of Allahazwj the Exalted: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50].
thaqalaynMatn: Heasws said: '**He takes his opinions as his religion**'.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Muhammad Bin Fuzeyl, 'From Abu Al-Hassanasws regarding Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]: 'It means **he takes his whims (personal opinions) as his religion without guidance from the Imamsasws of guidance**'.
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ فُضَيْلٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَل وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدَى مِنَ اللَّهِ يَعْنِي اتَّخَذَ دِينَهُ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الهُدَى.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Muhammad Bin Fuzeyl, 'From Abu Al-Hassanasws regarding Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]:
thaqalaynMatn: 'It means **he takes his whims (personal opinions) as his religion without guidance from the Imamsasws of guidance**'.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6.
