(Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ishaq Bin Ammar, from Ahmad Bin Al Nazr, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir Bin Yazeed, 'From Abu Ja'farasws having said: '**One who makes a religion without hearing from a truthful oneasws, Allahazwi would Necessitate the straying for him up to the Day of Qiyamah**'.
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ النَّضْرِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شَمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ: مَنْ دَانَ اللَّهَ بِغَيْرِ سَمَاع عَنْ صَادِقٍ أَلْزَمَهُ اللَّهُ الْبَتَّةَ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. (Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ishaq Bin Ammar, from Ahmad Bin Al Nazr, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir Bin Yazeed, 'From Abu Ja'farasws having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**One who makes a religion without hearing from a truthful oneasws, Allahazwi would Necessitate the straying for him up to the Day of Qiyamah**'.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the strayers, those who strayed away from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth, and took the religion of opinions without guidance from the Imams (a.s.) of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. (Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to us by Al Hussein Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Sayyari, from Ali Bin Abdullah who said, 'A man asked himasws about Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50]. Heasws said: '**One who says being with the Imamsasws, and follows theirasws orders, and does not allow theirasws obedience**'.
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُعَلَّى بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ السَّيَّارِي عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ : سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَل فَمَنِ اتَّبَعَ هدايَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَ لا يَشْقَى قَالَ مَنْ قَالَ بِالْأَئِمَّةِ وَ اتَّبَعَ أَمْرَهُمْ وَ لَمْ يَجُزَ طَاعَتَهُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. (Rare from the Chapter) It is narrated to us by Al Hussein Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Sayyari, from Ali Bin Abdullah who said, 'A man asked himasws about Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic: And who is more straying than the one who follows his own whims without a Guidance from Allah? [28:50].
thaqalaynMatn: Heasws said: '**One who says being with the Imamsasws, and follows theirasws orders, and does not allow theirasws obedience**'.

chapter: Chapter 9: IN IT IS CREATION OF THE BODIES OF THE IMAMSasws AND THEIRasws HEARTS, AND BODIES OF THE SHIAS AND THEIR HEARTS, LEST THE EXAGGERATION ENTERS THE PEOPLE REGARDING THE WONDERS OF THEIR SWS KNOWLEDGE
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Bishr Bin Abu Uqba, 'From Abu Ja'farasws and Abu Abdullahasws having said: '**Allahazwj Created Muhammad saww from clay from essence beneath the Throne... maturing. It was mounted as clay of Amir Al-Momineenasws from the mature clay of Rasool-Allah saww... maturing. Ourasws clay was mounted from remnant of clay of Amir Al-Momineenasws... maturing. The clay of ourasws Shias was mounted from the mature of ourasws clay. Thus, their hearts incline towards usasws and ourasws hearts are kind towards them, kinder than the father is upon the son**...'.
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنْ بِشْرِ بْنِ أَبِي عُقْبَة عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً ص مِنْ طِينَةٍ مِنْ جَوْهَرَةٍ تَحْتَ الْعَرْشِ وَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ لِطِينَتِهِ نَضْجٌ فَحَبَلَ طِينَةَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع مِنْ نَضْجِ طِينَةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ص وَ كَانَ لِطِينَتِهِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَحَبَل طيتَنَا مِنْ فَضْلِ طِينَة أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع وَ كَانَتْ لِطِيتِنَا نَصْجٌ فَجَبَلَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ نَضْحِ طِينَتِنَا فَقُلُوبُهُمْ تَحِنُ إِلَيْنَا وَ قُلُوبُنَا تَعْطِفُ عَلَيْهِمْ تَعَطَّفَ الْوَالِدِ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ وَ نَحْنُ خَيْرٌ لَهُمْ وَ هُمْ خَيْرٌ لَنَا وَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لَنَا خَيْرٌ وَ نَحْنُ لَهُ خَيْرٌ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Bishr Bin Abu Uqba, 'From Abu Ja'farasws and Abu Abdullahasws having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allahazwj Created Muhammad saww from clay from essence beneath the Throne... maturing. It was mounted as clay of Amir Al-Momineenasws from the mature clay of Rasool-Allah saww... maturing. Ourasws clay was mounted from remnant of clay of Amir Al-Momineenasws... maturing. The clay of ourasws Shias was mounted from the mature of ourasws clay. Thus, their hearts incline towards usasws and ourasws hearts are kind towards them, kinder than the father is upon the son**...'.

chapter: Chapter 9: IN IT IS CREATION OF THE BODIES OF THE IMAMSasws AND THEIRasws HEARTS, AND BODIES OF THE SHIAS AND THEIR HEARTS, LEST THE EXAGGERATION ENTERS THE PEOPLE REGARDING THE WONDERS OF THEIR SWS KNOWLEDGE
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin isa, from Bu Al Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'farasws said to me: '**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! Allahazwj Created Muhammad saww and Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww from clay of Illiyeen, and Created their sws hearts from clay above that, and Created ourasws Shias from clay below Illiyeen, and Created their hearts from clay of Illiyeen. Thus, the hearts of ourasws Shias are from bodies of Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww... And Allahazwi Created enemies of Progenyasws of Muhammad saww from clay of Sijjeen... Thus, their hearts are from the bodies of those, and every hearts tends to incline to its body**'.
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي الحَجَّاجِ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع يَا أَبَا الْحَجَّاجِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً وَ آل مُحَمَّدٍ صَ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ فَوْقِ ذَلِكَ وَخَلَقَ شِيعَتَنَا مِنْ طِينَةِ دُونِ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ فَقُلُوبُ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ أَبْدَانِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ عَدُوَّ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص مِنْ طِينِ سِحِينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ أَحْبَتْ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَخَلَقَ شِيعَتَهُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ دُونَ طِينٍ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينِ سِجِّينٍ فَقُلُوبُهُمْ مِنْ أَبْدَانِ أُولَئِكَ وَكُلُّ قَلْبٍ يَحِنُ إِلَى بَدَيْهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin isa, from Bu Al Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'farasws said to me:
thaqalaynMatn: '**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! Allahazwj Created Muhammad saww and Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww from clay of Illiyeen, and Created their sws hearts from clay above that, and Created ourasws Shias from clay below Illiyeen, and Created their hearts from clay of Illiyeen. Thus, the hearts of ourasws Shias are from bodies of Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww... And Allahazwi Created enemies of Progenyasws of Muhammad saww from clay of Sijjeen... Thus, their hearts are from the bodies of those, and every hearts tends to incline to its body**'.

chapter: Chapter 9: IN IT IS CREATION OF THE BODIES OF THE IMAMSasws AND THEIRasws HEARTS, AND BODIES OF THE SHIAS AND THEIR HEARTS, LEST THE EXAGGERATION ENTERS THE PEOPLE REGARDING THE WONDERS OF THEIR SWS KNOWLEDGE
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. And it is narrate to me by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad Al Barqy, from Abu Nahshal, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Sumaly who said, 'I hear Abu Ja'farasws saying: '**Allahazwj Created usasws from the high Illiyeen, and Created the hearts of ourasws Shias from it... So, their hearts inline towards them because these have been Created from what they had been Created from**'. Then heasws recited this Verse: **Never! Surely the Book of the righteous would be in Illiyeen** [83:18]'.
arabicText: 3- وَ حَدَّثَنِي ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي نَهْشَلٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنِ الثَّمَالِيِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقْنَا مِنْ أَعْلَى عليِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِنَا بِما خَلَقْنَا مِنْهُ وَ خَلَقَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَقُلُوبُهُمْ تَهْوِي إِلَيْنَا لِأَنَّهَا خُلِقَتْ بِمَا خُلِقْنَا مِنْهُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الْأَبْرَارِ لَفِي عِليِّينَ وَ ما أدراك ما عليون كِتَابٌ مَرْقُومٌ يَشْهَدُهُ الْمُقْرَبُونَ وَ خَلَقَ عَدُونَا مِنْ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِمَّا خَلَقَهُمْ مِنْهُ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَقُلُوبُهُمْ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ لِأَنَّهَا خُلِقَتْ بِمَا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتابَ الفُخَارِ لَفِي سِجِّينٍ وَ ما أَدْراكَ مَا سِحِينٌ كِتَابٌ مَرْقُومٌ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. And it is narrate to me by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad Al Barqy, from Abu Nahshal, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Sumaly who said, 'I hear Abu Ja'farasws saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allahazwj Created usasws from the high Illiyeen, and Created the hearts of ourasws Shias from it... So, their hearts inline towards them because these have been Created from what they had been Created from**. Then heasws recited this Verse: **Never! Surely the Book of the righteous would be in Illiyeen** [83:18]'.

chapter: Chapter 9: IN IT IS CREATION OF THE BODIES OF THE IMAMSasws AND THEIRasws HEARTS, AND BODIES OF THE SHIAS AND THEIR HEARTS, LEST THE EXAGGERATION ENTERS THE PEOPLE REGARDING THE WONDERS OF THEIR SWS KNOWLEDGE
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. And it is narrated to me by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad al Barqy, from Fazalat, from Al Batainy, from Abu Baseer, 'From Abu Ja'farasws having said: '**Weasws and ourasws Shias are Created from one clay, and ourasws enemies are Created from clay of pus, from toxic fumes**'.
arabicText: 4- وَ حَدَّثَنِي ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ عَنِ الْبَطَائِنِي عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّا وَ شِيعَتَنَا خُلِقْنَا مِنْ طِينَةِ وَاحِدَةٍ وَ خُلِقَ عَدُونَا مِنْ طِينَةِ حَبَالٍ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. And it is narrated to me by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad al Barqy, from Fazalat, from Al Batainy, from Abu Baseer, 'From Abu Ja'farasws having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Weasws and ourasws Shias are Created from one clay, and ourasws enemies are Created from clay of pus, from toxic fumes**'.

chapter: Chapter 9: IN IT IS CREATION OF THE BODIES OF THE IMAMSasws AND THEIRasws HEARTS, AND BODIES OF THE SHIAS AND THEIR HEARTS, LEST THE EXAGGERATION ENTERS THE PEOPLE REGARDING THE WONDERS OF THEIR SWS KNOWLEDGE
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to me by Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Rabie, 'From Aliasws Bin Al-Hussein asws having said: '**Allahazw the Exalted Created the Prophetsas from the clay of Illiyeen... and Heazwj Mixed between the two clays... The hearts of the Momineen tend to incline towards what they have been created from**'.
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنِي الْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ رِجْعِي عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى خَلَقَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ تِلْكَ الطَّيِّنَةِ وَ خَلَقَ أَبْدَانَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ الْكُفَّارَ مِنْ طِينَةِ سِجِّينٍ قُلُوبَهُمْ وَأَبْدَانَهُمْ فَخَلَطَ بَيْنَ الطَّيئَتَيْنِ فَمِنْ هَذَا يَلِدُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْكَافِرَ وَ يَلِدُ الْكَافِرُ الْمُؤْمِنَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْمُؤْمِنُ السَّيِّئَةَ وَ مِنْ هَاهُنَا يُصِيبُ الْكَافِرُ الْحَسَنَةَ فَقُلُوبُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ تَحِنُ إِلى مَا خَلِقُوا مِنْهُ وَقُلُوبُ الْكَافِرِينَ تَحِنُ إِلَى مَا خَلِقُوا مِنْهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to me by Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Rabie, 'From Aliasws Bin Al-Hussein asws having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allahazw the Exalted Created the Prophetsas from the clay of Illiyeen... and Heazwj Mixed between the two clays... The hearts of the Momineen tend to incline towards what they have been created from**'.

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. And it is narrated to me by Ahmad b. al-Hussein, from Ahmad b. Ali b. Haysam, from Idrees, from Muhammad b. Sinan al-Abdy, from Jabir al-Jufy who said, 'I was with Muhammad (a.s.) b. Ali (a.s.), and he (a.s.) said: "O Jabir! **We (a.s.) and ones who love us (a.s.) are Created from one clay, white, pure, from high Illiyeen**. We (a.s.) are Created from its top layer and ones who love us (a.s.) are Created from below it. So, when it will be the Day of Qiyamah, its top will meet up with its lower. And when it will be the Day of Qiyamah, **we (a.s.) shall strike our (a.s.) hands to a side of our (a.s.) Prophet (s.a.w.a.), and our (a.s.) Shias would strike their hands to our (a.s.) sides**. So, where do you think Allah (a.z.w.j.) will destine His (a.z.w.j.) Prophet (s.a.w.a.) and his (s.a.w.a.) offspring to be? And where do you think his (s.a.w.a.) offspring would destine ones who love them (a.s.)?"' Jabir hit his hand upon his (a.s.) hand and said, 'We shall enter it (Paradise), by the Lord (a.z.w.j.) of the Kabah!' — thrice [cite: 3409, 3410-3421].
arabicText: 6- وَ حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ هَيْثَمٍ عَنْ إِدْرِيسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ الْعَبْدِي عَنْ جَابِرٍ الْجُعْفِيِّ قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ فَقَالَ يَا جَابِرُ خَلِقْنَا نَحْنُ وَ مُحِبِّينَا مِنْ طِينَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ بَيْضَاءَ نَقِيَّةٍ مِنْ أَعْلَى عِليِّينَ فَخَلِقْنَا نَحْنُ مِنْ أَعْلَاهَا وَ خُلِقَ محبينا مُحِبُّونَا مِنْ دُونِهَا فَإِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيَامَةِ الْتَقْتِ الْعُلْيَا بِالسُّفْلَى و إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيَامَةِ ضَرَبْنَا بِأَيْدِينَا إِلَى حُجْزَة نَبِيِّنَا وَ ضَرَبَ أَشْيَاعُنَا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ إِلَى حُجْزَيْنَا فَأَيْنَ تَرَى يُصَيِّرُ اللَّهُ نَبِيَّهُ وَ ذُرِّيَّتَهُ وَ أَيْنَ تَرَى يُصَيِّرُ ذُرِّيَّتُهُ محبيها فَضَرَبَ جَابِرٌ يَدَهُ عَلَى يَدِهِ فَقَالَ دَخَلْنَاهَا وَ رَبِّ الْكَعْبَةِ ثَلاثاً[cite: 3408].
thaqalaynSanad: 6. And it is narrated to me by Ahmad b. al-Hussein, from Ahmad b. Ali b. Haysam, from Idrees, from Muhammad b. Sinan al-Abdy, from Jabir al-Jufy who said, 'I was with Muhammad (a.s.) b. Ali (a.s.), and he (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: "O Jabir! **We (a.s.) and ones who love us (a.s.) are Created from one clay, white, pure, from high Illiyeen**. We (a.s.) are Created from its top layer and ones who love us (a.s.) are Created from below it. So, when it will be the Day of Qiyamah, its top will meet up with its lower. And when it will be the Day of Qiyamah, **we (a.s.) shall strike our (a.s.) hands to a side of our (a.s.) Prophet (s.a.w.a.), and our (a.s.) Shias would strike their hands to our (a.s.) sides**. So, where do you think Allah (a.z.w.j.) will destine His (a.z.w.j.) Prophet (s.a.w.a.) and his (s.a.w.a.) offspring to be? And where do you think his (s.a.w.a.) offspring would destine ones who love them (a.s.)?" Jabir hit his hand upon his (a.s.) hand and said, 'We shall enter it (Paradise), by the Lord (a.z.w.j.) of the Kabah!' — thrice.

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from al-Nasr b. Shuayb, from Abdul Gaffar al-Jazy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the believer from the clay of Paradise, and Created the Nasibi (hostile one) from the clay of Fire**.' And he (a.s.) said: 'When Allah (a.z.w.j.) wants good with a servant, He makes his soul and body to be good, so he does not hear anything from the good except he recognizes it, nor does he hear anything from the evil except he denies it.' He (the narrator) said: 'And I heard him (a.s.) saying: **The clays are three: clay of the Prophets (a.s.), and the believer is from that clay except that the Prophets (a.s.) are its pure origin... and the believer is the branch from the sticky clay**.' And he (a.s.) said: 'The clay of a Nasibi is from toxic fumes, and as for the weak ones, it is from dust' [cite: 3428-3438].
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنِ النَّضْرِ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْغَفَّارِ الْجَازِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ الْمُؤْمِنَ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْجَنَّةِ وَ خَلَقَ النَّاصِبَ مِنْ طِينَةِ النَّارِ وَ قَالَ إِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِعَبْدِ خَيْراً طَيِّبَ رُوحَهُ وَ جَسَدَهُ فَلَا يَسْمَعُ شَيْئاً مِنَ الْخَيْراً إِلَّا عَرَفَهُ وَ لَا يَسْمَعُ شَيْئًا مِنَ الْمُنْكَرِ إِلَّا أَنْكَرَهُ قالَ وَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ الطَّيِّنَاتُ ثَلَاثَةٌ طِينَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُ مِنْ تِلْكَ الطَّيئَةِ إِلَّا أَنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ هُمْ صَفْوَتُهَا وَهُمُ الْأَصْلُ وَلَهُمْ فَضْلُهُمْ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْفَرْعُ مِنْ طين لازب كذلك لا يُفَرِّقُ اللَّهُ بَيْنَهُمْ وَ بَيْنَ شِيعَتِهِمْ و قَالَ طِينَةُ النَّاصِبِ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ وَ أَمَّا الْمُسْتَضْعَفُونَ فَ مِنْ تُرَابِ لَا يَتَحَوَّلُ مُؤْمِنٌ عَنْ إِيمَانِهِ وَ لَا نَاصِبٌ عَنْ نَصْبِهِ وَ لِلَّهِ الْمَشِيَّةُ فِيهِمْ جَمِيعاً[cite: 3428].
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from al-Nasr b. Shuayb, from Abdul Gaffar al-Jazy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the believer from the clay of Paradise, and Created the Nasibi (hostile one) from the clay of Fire**.' And he (a.s.) said: 'When Allah (a.z.w.j.) wants good with a servant, He makes his soul and body to be good, so he does not hear anything from the good except he recognizes it, nor does he hear anything from the evil except he denies it.' He (the narrator) said: 'And I heard him (a.s.) saying: **The clays are three: clay of the Prophets (a.s.), and the believer is from that clay except that the Prophets (a.s.) are its pure origin... and the believer is the branch from the sticky clay**. Like that, Allah (a.z.w.j.) does not separate between them (a.s.) and their Shias.' And he (a.s.) said: 'The clay of a Nasibi is from toxic fumes, and as for the weak ones, it is from dust. A believer will not transfer from his faith nor will a Nasibi from his hostility, and for Allah (a.z.w.j.) is the desire regarding them all.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Imran b. Musa, from Ibrahim b. Mahziyar, from Ali, from al-Hussein b. Saeed, from al-Hassan b. Mahboub al-Hashimy, from Hanan b. Sadeyr, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Kneaded our (a.s.) clay and the clay of our (a.s.) Shias**. He (a.z.w.j.) Mixed us (a.s.) with them and Mixed them with us (a.s.). So, **the one who has in his creation anything from our (a.s.) clay would incline towards us (a.s.)**. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! You are all from us (a.s.)' [cite: 3439-3444].
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا عِمْرَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبِ الْهَاشِمِي عَنْ حَنَانِ بْنِ سَدِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَجَنَ طِينَتَنَا وَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِنَا فَخَلَطْنَا بِهِمْ وَ خَلَطَهُمْ بِنَا فَمَنْ كَانَ فِي خَلْقِهِ شَيْءٌ مِنْ طِينَتَنَا حَنَّ إِلَيْنَا فَأَنْتُمْ وَ اللَّهِ مِنَّا[cite: 3439].
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Imran b. Musa, from Ibrahim b. Mahziyar, from Ali, from al-Hussein b. Saeed, from al-Hassan b. Mahboub al-Hashimy, from Hanan b. Sadeyr, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Kneaded our (a.s.) clay and the clay of our (a.s.) Shias**. He (a.z.w.j.) Mixed us (a.s.) with them and Mixed them with us (a.s.). So, **the one who has in his creation anything from our (a.s.) clay would incline towards us (a.s.)**. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! You are all from us (a.s.).'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. By this chain, from al-Hussein b. Saeed, from al-Hassan b. Maymoun, from the one who informed him, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from Illiyeen and Created ones who love us (a.s.) from below that... and Created our (a.s.) enemies from Sijjeen** and Created ones who love them from what He (a.z.w.j.) had Created them from. Therefore, due to that, each type inclines to each similar type' [cite: 3445, 3446-3457].
arabicText: 9- بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ عَمَّنْ أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خَلَقْنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ مُحِبِّينَا مِنْ دُونِ مَا خَلَقْنَا مِنْهُ وَ خَلَقَ عَدُوَّنَا مِنْ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ مُحِبيهِمْ لِما خَلَقَهُمْ مِنْهُ فَلِذَلِكَ يَهْوِي كُلِّ إِلَى كُل[cite: 3445].
thaqalaynSanad: 9. By this chain, from al-Hussein b. Saeed, from al-Hassan b. Maymoun, from the one who informed him, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from Illiyeen and Created ones who love us (a.s.) from below that... and Created our (a.s.) enemies from Sijjeen** and Created ones who love them from what He (a.z.w.j.) had Created them from. Therefore, due to that, each type inclines to each similar type.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to me by Imran b. Musa, from Musa b. Ja'far, from Ali b. Ma'bad, from Ibrahim b. Is'haq, from al-Hussein b. Yazeed, from Ja'far (a.s.) b. Muhammad (a.s.), from his (a.s.) father (a.s.), from his (a.s.) grandfather (a.s.) who said: Ali (a.s.) b. al-Hussein (a.s.) said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Jibraeel (a.s.) to Paradise and he (a.s.) came with clay... and He (a.z.w.j.) Sent the Angel of Death to the earth and he came with clay... He (a.z.w.j.) Gathered the two clays then Divided them into two halves. He (a.z.w.j.) Made us (a.s.) from the better division and Made our (a.s.) Shias from our (a.s.) clay**.' So, whatever ugly deeds are done by our (a.s.) Shias, it is from the mingling of the wicked clay, and their destination is to Paradise. And whatever good deeds are done by our (a.s.) enemies, it is from the mingling of our (a.s.) clay, and their destination is to the Fire [cite: 3458, 3459-3466].
arabicText: 10 - حَدَّثَنِي عِمْرَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ع إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَعَثَ جَبْرَئِيلَ إلى الجنَّةِ فَأَتَاهُ بِطِينَةٍ مِنْ طِينَتِهَا وَ بَعَثَ مَلَكَ الْمَوْتِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ فَجَاءَهُ بِطِينَةٍ مِنْ طِينَتِهَا فَجَمَعَ الطَّيئَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ قَسَمَهَا نِصْفَيْنِ فَجَعَلَنَا مِنْ خَيْرِ الْقِسْمَيْنِ وَ جَعَلَ شِيعَتَنَا مِنْ طِيبَتِنَا فَمَا كَانَ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا مِمَّا يُرْغَبُ بِهِمْ عَنْهُ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ الْقَبِيحَةِ فَذَاكَ مِمَّا خَالَطَهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّنَةِ الْخَبِيثَةِ وَ مَصِيرُهَا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ مَا كَانَ فِي عَدُوِّنَا مِنْ بَر وَ صَلَاةٍ وَ صَوْمِ وَ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ الحَسَنَةِ فَذَاكَ لِمَا خَالَطَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَتِنَا الطَّيِّبَةِ وَ مَصِيرُهُمْ إِلَى النَّارِ[cite: 3458].
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to me by Imran b. Musa, from Musa b. Ja'far, from Ali b. Ma'bad, from Ibrahim b. Is'haq, from al-Hussein b. Yazeed, from Ja'far (a.s.) b. Muhammad (a.s.), from his (a.s.) father (a.s.), from his (a.s.) grandfather (a.s.) who said: Ali (a.s.) b. al-Hussein (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Jibraeel (a.s.) to Paradise and he (a.s.) came with clay from its clays, and He (a.z.w.j.) Sent the Angel of Death to the earth, and he came with clay from its clays. He (a.z.w.j.) Gathered the two clays, then Divided them into two halves. He (a.z.w.j.) Made us (a.s.) from the better of the two divisions, and Made our (a.s.) Shias from our (a.s.) clay**. So, whatever was from our (a.s.) Shias, from what is indulged by them from it, from the ugly deeds, so that is from what their mingling from the wicked clay, and its destination is to Paradise. And whatever was from our (a.s.) enemies, from righteousness, Salat, and Fasts, so that is due to what was their mingling from our (a.s.) clay, and their destination is to the Fire.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. Hammad, from his brother Ahmad b. Hammad, from Ibrahim b. Abdul Hameed, from his father, from Abu al-Hussein (a.s.) the 1st, who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the Prophets (a.s.) and the successors (a.s.) on the day of Friday, and it is the day during which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took their Covenant**.' And he (a.s.) said: '**We (a.s.) and our (a.s.) Shias are Created from treasured clay. No deviating one will deviate from it up to the Day of Qiyamah**' [cite: 3467, 3468-3478].
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ أَخِيهِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءَ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَ هُوَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي أَخَذَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ مِيثَاقَهُمْ وَ قَالَ خُلِقْنَا نَحْنُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا مِنْ طِينَةٍ مَخْرُونَةٍ لَا يَشِدُّ مِنْهَا شَادُّ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ[cite: 3467].
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. Hammad, from his brother Ahmad b. Hammad, from Ibrahim b. Abdul Hameed, from his father, from Abu al-Hussein (a.s.) the 1st, who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the Prophets (a.s.) and the successors (a.s.) on the day of Friday, and it is the day during which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took their Covenant**. We (a.s.) and our (a.s.) Shias are Created from treasured clay. No deviating one will deviate from it up to the Day of Qiyamah.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad b. Musa, from al-Hassan b. Musa, from Ali b. Hassan, from Abdul Rahman b. Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and his (s.a.w.a.) family (a.s.) from clay of the Throne, so neither will anyone be reduced from them (a.s.) nor will anyone be increased from them (a.s.)**' [cite: 3479, 3480-3481].
arabicText: 12- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً وَ عِتْرَتَهُ مِن طِينَةِ الْعَرْشِ فَلَا يَنْقُصُ مِنْهُمْ وَاحِدٌ وَ لَا يَزِيدُ مِنْهُمْ وَاحِدٌ[cite: 3479].
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad b. Musa, from al-Hassan b. Musa, from Ali b. Hassan, from Abdul Rahman b. Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and his (s.a.w.a.) family (a.s.) from clay of the Throne, so neither will anyone be reduced from them (a.s.) nor will anyone be increased from them (a.s.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub b. Yazeed, and Muhammad b. Isa, from Ziyad al-Qandy, from al-Fazl b. Isa al-Hashimy who said, 'I and my father entered to see Abu Abdullah (a.s.). He said to him (a.s.): "Is it from the words of the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) that Salman is a man from us (a.s.), the People of the Household?" He (a.s.) said: "Yes." He said: "I.e., from the sons of Abdul Muttalib (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "From us (a.s.) the People of the Household." He said to him (a.s.): "I.e., from the sons of Abu Talib (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "From us (a.s.), the People of the Household." He said to him (a.s.): "I do not understand it." He (a.s.) said: "Understand it, O Isa, for he is from us (a.s.)... **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created our (a.s.) clay from Illiyeen, and Created the clay of our (a.s.) Shias from below that. So, they are from us (a.s.). And He (s.a.w.a.) Created our (a.s.) enemies from Sijjeen**... and Salman is better than Luqman (a.s.)"' [cite: 3482, 3483-3494].
arabicText: 13 - حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ زِيَادٍ الْقَنْدِي عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ عِيسَى الْهَاشِمِي قَالَ : دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَا وَ أَبِي فَقَالَ لَهُ أَ مِنْ قَوْلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص سَلْمَانُ رَجُلٌ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ فَقَالَ أَيْ مِنْ وُلْدِ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ فَقَالَ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَي مِنْ وُلْدِ أَبِي طَالِبِ فَقَالَ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنِّي لَا أَعْرِفُهُ فَقَالَ فَاعْرِفْهُ يَا عِيسَى فَإِنَّهُ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ثُمَّ أَوْمَا بِيَدِهِ إِلَى صَدْرِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَيْسَ حَيْثُ تَذْهَبُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ طِينَتَنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَهُمْ مِنَّا وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ عَدُوِّنَا مِنْ سحين وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَهُمْ مِنْهُمْ وَ سَلْمَانُ خَيْرٌ مِنْ لُقْمَانَ[cite: 3482].
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub b. Yazeed, and Muhammad b. Isa, from Ziyad al-Qandy, from al-Fazl b. Isa al-Hashimy who said, 'I and my father entered to see Abu Abdullah (a.s.). He said to him (a.s.):
thaqalaynMatn: "Is it from the words of the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) that Salman is a man from us (a.s.), the People of the Household?" He (a.s.) said: "Yes." He said: "I.e., from the sons of Abdul Muttalib (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "From us (a.s.) the People of the Household." He said to him (a.s.): "I.e., from the sons of Abu Talib (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "From us (a.s.), the People of the Household." He said to him (a.s.): "I do not understand it." He (a.s.) said: "Understand it, O Isa, for he is from us (a.s.), the People of the Household. It isn't where you are going with it. **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created our (a.s.) clay from Illiyeen, and Created clay of our (a.s.) Shias from below that. So, they are from us (a.s.)**. And He (s.a.w.a.) Created our (a.s.) enemies from Sijjeen, and Created clay of their adherents from below that, and they are from them; and Salman is better than Luqman (a.s.)."

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by one of our companions, from Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from Usman b. Isa, from Abdul Rahman b. al-Hajjaj who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Created Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) from clay of Illiyeen, and Created their hearts from clay above that, and Created their (a.s.) Shias from clay of Illiyeen and Created hearts of their (a.s.) Shias from clay above Illiyeen**' [cite: 3495, 3496-3506].
arabicText: 14 حَدَّثَنَا بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ : إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً وَ آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِليِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ فَوْقِ ذَلِكَ وَخَلَقَ شِيعَتَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِليِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ فَوْقِ عِلِّيِّينَ[cite: 3495].
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by one of our companions, from Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from Usman b. Isa, from Abdul Rahman b. al-Hajjaj who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Created Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) from clay of Illiyeen, and Created their hearts from clay above that, and Created their (a.s.) Shias from clay of Illiyeen and Created hearts of their (a.s.) Shias from clay above Illiyeen**.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad al-Barqy, from Salih b. Sahl who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.): "Is the believer from the clay of the Prophets (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "Yes"' [cite: 3507, 3508-3509].
arabicText: 15 - حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ سَهْلٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عِ الْمُؤْمِنُ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ عِ قَالَ نَعَمْ[cite: 3507].
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad al-Barqy, from Salih b. Sahl who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.):
thaqalaynMatn: "Is the believer from the clay of the Prophets (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "Yes."

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah b. Muhammad b. Ibrahim b. Muhammad, from Masoud b. Yusuf b. Kuleyb, from al-Hassan b. Hammad, from Fuzeyl b. al-Zubeyr, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'O Fuzeyl! Do you not know that the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) said: "**We (a.s.), the People of the Household, are created from Illiyeen, and our (a.s.) hearts are created from that which we (a.s.) are created from, and our (a.s.) Shias are created from lower than that**... And our (a.s.) enemies are created from Sijjeen... So, can anyone from the people of Illiyeen have the capacity to become from the people of Sijjeen?"' [cite: 3511, 3512-3518].
arabicText: 16 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مَسْعُودِ بْنِ يُوسُفَ بْنِ كُلَيْبٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: يا فضيل أَمَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ص قَالَ إِنَّا أَهْلُ بَيْتٍ خُلِقْنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خُلِقَ قُلُوبُنَا مِنَ الَّذِي خَلِقْنَا مِنْهُ وَ خُلِقَ شِيعَتُنَا مِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَ خُلِقَ قُلُوبُ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْهُ وَ إِنَّ عَدُونَا خُلِقُوا مِنْ سِجِّينٍ وَ خُلِقَ قُلُوبُهُمْ مِنَ الَّذِي خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ وَ خُلِقَ شِيعَتُهُمْ مِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنْ ذلِكَ وَ خُلِقَ قُلُوبُ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِمَّا خُلِقُوا مِنْهُ فَهَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَحَدٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ عِلِّيِّينَ أَنْ يَكُونَ مِنْ أَهْلِ سِحْينٍ وَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَهْلُ سِجِّينٍ أَنْ يَكُونُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ عِلِّيِّينَ[cite: 3511].
thaqalaynSanad: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah b. Muhammad b. Ibrahim b. Muhammad, from Masoud b. Yusuf b. Kuleyb, from al-Hassan b. Hammad, from Fuzeyl b. al-Zubeyr, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'O Fuzeyl! Do you not know that the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) said: "**We (a.s.), the People of the Household, are created from Illiyeen, and our (a.s.) hearts are created from that which we (a.s.) are created from, and our (a.s.) Shias are created from lower than that, and hearts of our (a.s.) Shias are created from it**. And our (a.s.) enemies are created from Sijjeen, and their hearts are created from that which they have been created from, and their loyalists are created from lower than that. So, can anyone from the people of Illiyeen have the capacity to become from the people of Sijjeen? And can anyone from the people of Sijjeen have the capacity to become from the people of Illiyeen?"'

chapter: Chapter 9: In it is creation of the bodies of the Imams (a.s.) and their (a.s.) hearts, and bodies of the Shias and their hearts, lest the exaggeration enters the people regarding the wonders of their (a.s.) knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 17. And from him, from Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from al-Hassan b. Mahboub, from Sayf b. Ameyra, from Abu Bakr al-Hazrami, from Ali (a.s.) b. al-Hussein (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias along with us (a.s.) upon our (a.s.) Wilayah**. Neither will they be increasing nor reducing. **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from clay of Illiyeen, and Created our (a.s.) Shias from clay lower than that**, and Created our (a.s.) enemies from clay of Sijjeen' [cite: 3519, 3520-3530].
arabicText: 17 - وَ عَنْهُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةً عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ الْحَضْرَمِي عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ: قَدْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ ميثاق شِيعَتِنَا مَعَنَا عَلَى وَلَا يَتِنَا لَا يَزِيدُونَ وَ لَا يَنْقُصُونَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقْنَا مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ وَخَلَقَ شِيعَتَنَا مِنْ طِيبَةٍ أَسْفَلَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ عَدُونَا مِنْ طِينَةِ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ أَوْلِيَاءَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةٍ أَسْفَلَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ[cite: 3519].
thaqalaynSanad: 17. And from him, from Muhammad b. al-Hussein, from al-Hassan b. Mahboub, from Sayf b. Ameyra, from Abu Bakr al-Hazrami, from Ali (a.s.) b. al-Hussein (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias along with us (a.s.) upon our (a.s.) Wilayah**. Neither will they be increasing (in number) nor reducing. **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from clay of Illiyeen, and Created our (a.s.) Shias from clay lower than that**, and Created our (a.s.) enemies from clay of Sijjeen, and Created their friends from clay lower than that.'

chapter: Rare from Chapter 9
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to me by Ali b. Hassan, from Ali b. Atiya al-Zayyat, raising it to Amir al-Mu'minin (a.s.) who said: '**There is a river for Allah (a.z.w.j.) below His (a.z.w.j.) Throne... and on the banks of the river there are two Created spirits — the Holy Spirit and a Spirit from His (a.z.w.j.) Command**. And that for Allah (a.z.w.j.) there are ten clays, five from a substance from the Paradise, and five from the earth... **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us from the ten clays altogether, and Blew into us (a.s.) from the two Spirits altogether**. So, the better one is our (a.s.) clay' [cite: 3532, 3533-3543].
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي عَلِيُّ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ الزَّيَّاتِ يَرْفَعُهُ إِلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ع إِنَّ لِلَّهِ نَهَرًا دُونَ عَرْشِهِ وَ دُونَ النَّهَرِ الَّذِي دُونَ عَرْشِهِ نُورٌ مِنْ نُورِهِ وَ إِنَّ فِي حَافَتَي النَّهَرِ رُوحَيْنِ مَخْلُوقَيْنِ رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ وَ رُوحٌ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ وَ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ عَشْرَ طِينَاتٍ خَمْسَهُ مِنْ نَفْحَ الْجَنَّةِ وَ خَمسة مِنَ الْأَرْضِ وَ فَسَّرَ الْجَنَانَ وَ فَسَّرَ الْأَرْضَ ثُمَّ قَالَ مَا مِنْ نَبِي وَ لَا مِنْ مَلَكَ مِنْ بَعْدِ حَبْلِهِ إِلَّا نَفَخَ فِيهِ مِنَ الرُّوحَيْنِ وَ جَعَلَ النَّبِيَّ ص مِنْ إِحْدَى الطَّيئَتَيْنِ فَقُلْتُ لأبي الحَسَنِ ع مَا الْحَبْلُ قَالَ الْخَلْقُ غَيْرَنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَنَا مِنَ الْعَشْرِ الطَّيِّنَاتِ جَمِيعاً وَ نَفَخَ فِينَا مِنَ الرُّوحَيْنِ جَمِيعاً فَأَطْيَبُهُمَا طيباً طيننا[cite: 3532].
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to me by Ali b. Hassan, from Ali b. Atiya al-Zayyat, raising it to Ali (a.s.) b. Abu Talib (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**There is a river for Allah (a.z.w.j.) below His (a.z.w.j.) Throne, and below the river which is below His (a.z.w.j.) Throne, there is a Noor (Light) from His (a.z.w.j.), and on the banks of the river there are two Created spirits — the Holy Spirit and a Spirit from His (a.z.w.j.) Command**. And that for Allah (a.z.w.j.) there are ten clays, five from a substance from the Paradise, and five from the earth. There is none from a Prophet (s.a.w.a.), nor from an Angel except there was blown into it from (one of) the two Spirits... but **Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from the ten clays altogether, and Blew into us (a.s.) from the two Spirits altogether**. So, the better one is our (a.s.) clay.'

chapter: Chapter 10: Regarding creation of bodies of the Imams (a.s.), and regarding creation of their (a.s.) souls and their (a.s.) Shias
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad, from Abu Yahya al-Wasitiy, from one of our companions who said: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.) are Created from Illiyeen, and our (a.s.) souls are Created from above that, and the souls of our (a.s.) Shias are from Illiyeen, and their bodies are Created from below that**. So, from the reason of that relationship between us (a.s.) and them, their hearts incline towards us (a.s.)" [cite: 3564, 3565-3567].
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِي يَحْيَى الْوَاسِطِيِّ عَنْ بَعْضٍ أَصْحَابِنَا قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع خَلَقَنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ أَرْوَاحَنَا مِنْ فَوْقِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ أَرْوَاحَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ أَحْسَادَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَمِنْ أَجَلٍ تِلْكَ الْقَرَابَةِ بَيْنَنَا وَ بَيْنَهُمْ قُلُوبُهُمْ تَحِنُّ إِلَيْنَا[cite: 3564].
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad, from Abu Yahya al-Wasitiy, from one of our companions who said: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: "**We (a.s.) are Created from Illiyeen, and our (a.s.) souls are Created from above that, and the souls of our (a.s.) Shias are from Illiyeen, and their bodies are Created from below that**. So, from the reason of that relationship between us (a.s.) and them, their hearts incline towards us (a.s.)."

chapter: Chapter 10: Regarding creation of bodies of the Imams (a.s.), and regarding creation of their (a.s.) souls and their (a.s.) Shias
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Imran b. Musa, from Ibrahim b. Mihran b. Mahziyar, from his brother Ali, from Muhammad b. Sinan, from Ismail b. Jabir and Karram, from Muhammad b. Muzarib, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Made us (a.s.) from Illiyeen and Made souls of our (a.s.) Shias from what we (a.s.) were Made from... and their bodies are Created from below that. And our (a.s.) enemies are Created from Sijjeen**... and their bodies are Created from below that' [cite: 3568, 3569-3573].
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثنا عِمْرَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ أَخِيهِ عَلِيٌّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ وَ كَرَامٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مضَارِبٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى جَعَلَنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ جَعَلَ أَرْوَاحَ شِيعَتِنَا ممَّا جَعَلَنَا مِنْهُ وَ مِنْ ثُمَّ تَحِنُّ أَرْوَاحُهُمْ إِلَيْنَا وَ خَلَقَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ عَدُوَّنَا مِنْ سِحِينٍ وَ خَلَقَ أَرْوَاحَ شِيعَتِهِمْ بِما خَلَقَهُمْ مِنْهُ وَ خَلْقَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ مِنْ ثُمَّ تَهْوِي أَرْوَاحُهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ[cite: 3568].
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Imran b. Musa, from Ibrahim b. Mihran b. Mahziyar, from his brother Ali, from Muhammad b. Sinan, from Ismail b. Jabir and Karram, from Muhammad b. Muzarib, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Made us (a.s.) from Illiyeen and Made souls of our (a.s.) Shias from what we (a.s.) were Made from, and from then their souls incline towards us (a.s.); and their bodies are Created from below that. And our (a.s.) enemies are Created from Sijjeen**, and the souls of their adherents are from what they have been Created from, and their bodies are Created from below that, and from then they souls incline towards them.'

chapter: Chapter 10: Regarding creation of bodies of the Imams (a.s.), and regarding creation of their (a.s.) souls and their (a.s.) Shias
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. Shuayn, from Imran b. Ishaq al-Zafrany, from Muhammad b. Marwan, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from Noor (Light) of His (a.z.w.j.) Magnificence, then Imaged our (a.s.) creation from treasured hidden clay from beneath the Throne, and Settled that Noor in it. So, we (a.s.) happen to be creatures and humans of luminosity**. No share has been Made for anyone in the like of which we (a.s.) are Created from. **And souls of our (a.s.) Shias are Created from our (a.s.) bodies... and Allah (a.z.w.j.) did not Make any share for anyone in the like except for the Prophets (a.s.) and the Messengers (a.s.)**' [cite: 3574, 3575-3587].
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ الزَّعْفَرَانِي عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُ هُ يَقُولُ خَلَقْنَا اللَّهُ مِنْ نُورِ عَظَمَتِهِ ثُمَّ صَوَّرَ خَلَقْنَا مِنْ طِينَةٍ مَخرُونَةٍ مَكْنُونَةٍ مِنْ تَحْتِ الْعَرْشِ فَأَسْكَنَ ذَلِكَ النُّورَ فِيهِ فكُنَّا نَحْنُ خَلْقاً وَ بَشَرًا نُورَانِيِّينَ لم يَجْعَلْ لِأَحَدٍ فِي مِثْلِ الَّذِي خَلَقْنَا مِنْهُ نَصِيباً و خَلَقَ أَرْوَاحَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ أَبْدَانِنَا وَ أَبْدَانَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةٍ مَحْزُونَةٍ مَكْنُونَةٍ أَسْفَلَ من ذلك الطيئَةِ وَ لَمْ يَجْعَل الله لِأَحَدٍ فِي مِثْل ذَلِكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَهُمْ مِنْهُ نَصِيباً إِلَّا الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ فَلِذَلِكَ صِرْنَا نَحْنُ وَ هُمُ النَّاسَ وَ سَائِرُ النَّاسِ هَمَجا فِي النَّارِ وَ إِلَى النَّارِ[cite: 3574].
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad b. Shuayn, from Imran b. Ishaq al-Zafrany, from Muhammad b. Marwan, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created us (a.s.) from Noor (Light) of His (a.z.w.j.) Magnificence, then Imaged our (a.s.) creation from treasured hidden clay from beneath the Throne, and Settled that Noor in it. So, we (a.s.) happen to be creatures and humans of luminosity**. No share has been Made to be for anyone in the like of which we (a.s.) are Created from. And souls of our (a.s.) Shias are Created from our (a.s.) bodies, and their bodies are from treasured hidden clay lower than that clay, and Allah (a.z.w.j.) did not Make any share to be for anyone in the like that which He (a.z.w.j.) Created them from except for the Prophets (a.s.) and the Messengers (a.s.). So, due to that, we (a.s.) and them became the people, and the rest of the people would fall into the Fire and go to the Fire.'

chapter: Chapter 11: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Hadeeth are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Abu Al Khattab, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ammar Bin Marwan, from Al Munakhal, from Jabir who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The Hadeeth of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) are difficult, becoming more difficult. None will believe in it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Tested for the Eman.**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنِ الْمُتَخَلِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّ حَدِيثَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَعْبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ لَا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ إِلَّا مَلَكَ مُقَرَّبِّ أَوْ نَبِي مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ عَبْدُ امْتَحَنَ اللَّهُ قَلْبَهُ لِلْإِيمَانِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Abu Al Khattab, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ammar Bin Marwan, from Al Munakhal, from Jabir who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: '**The Hadeeth of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) are difficult, becoming more difficult. None will believe in it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Tested for the Eman.**'

chapter: Chapter 11: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Hadeeth are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Abu Ja'far, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Zareeh Al Muhariby, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, from Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult. None can tolerate it except a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or an Angel of Proximity, and from the Angels without Proximity.**'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ ذَرِيحٍ الْمُحَارِبِي عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ حَدِيثَنَا صَعْبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ لَا يَحْتَمِلُهُ إِلَّا نَبِيُّ مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ مَلَكُ مُقَرَّبٌ وَ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ غَيْرُ مُقَرَّبٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Abu Ja'far, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Zareeh Al Muhariby, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, from Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Hussein (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult. None can tolerate it except a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or an Angel of Proximity, and from the Angels without Proximity.**'

chapter: Chapter 11: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Hadeeth are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Abu Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abu Al Jaroud, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**The Hadeeth of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) are difficult, becoming more difficult, heavy, veiled, abstract, intelligent. None can tolerate it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Tested for the Eman, or a fortified city.**'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْجَارُودِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ حَدِيثَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَعْبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ ثَقِيلٌ مُقْتَعُ أَجْرَدُ ذَكْوَانُ لا يَحْتَمِلُهُ إِلَّا مَلكَ مُقَرَّبٌ أَوْ نَبِي مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ عَبْد امْتَحَنَ اللهُ قَلْبَهُ لِلْإِيمَانِ أَوْ مَدِينَةٌ حَصِينَةٌ فَإِذَا قَامَ قَائِمُنَا نَطَقَ وَ صَدَّقَهُ الْقُرْآنُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Abu Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abu Al Jaroud, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Hadeeth of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) are difficult, becoming more difficult, heavy, veiled, abstract, intelligent. None can tolerate it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Tested for the Eman, or a fortified city.** When our (a.s.) Qaim (a.s.) rises, he (a.s.) will speak and the Quran will ratify him (a.s.).'

chapter: Chapter 11: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Hadeeth are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hussein, from Wuheyb Bin Hafs, from Abu Baseer who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult. None will believe in it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.** So, whatever your hearts recognise, take it, and whatever they deny, return it to us (a.s.).'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ وُهَيْبِ بْنِ حَفْصٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع حَدِيثُنَا صَعَبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ لَا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ إِلَّا مَلَكٌ مُقَرَّبٌ أَوْ نَبِيٌّ مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ عَبْدُ امْتَحَنَ اللهُ قَلْبَهُ لِلْإِيمَانِ فَمَا عَرَفَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ فَخُذُوهُ وَ مَا أَنْكَرَتْ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَيْنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hussein, from Wuheyb Bin Hafs, from Abu Baseer who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult. None will believe in it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.** So, whatever your hearts recognise, take it, and whatever they deny, return it to us (a.s.).'

chapter: Chapter 11: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Hadeeth are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Sabbah Al Muzny, from Al Haris Bin Haseyr, from Al Asbagh Bin Nubata, from Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult, harsh, reining. None can tolerate it except three: an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a Momin servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.**'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ صَبَّاحٍ الْمُزَنِي عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ حَصِيرٍ عَنِ الْأَصْبَعِ بْنِ نُبَاتَةَ عَنْ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنْ حَدِيثَنَا صَعْبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ حَشِنٌ مَخْشُوسٌ فَانْبِذُوا إِلَى النَّاسِ نَبْداً فَمَنْ عَرَفَ فَرِيدُوهُ وَ مَنْ أَنْكَرَ فَأَمْسِكُوا لَا يَحْتَمِلُهُ إِلَّا ثَلَاثَ مَلَكٌ مُقَرَّبٌ أَوْ نَبِي مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ عَبْدٌ مُؤْمِنٌ امْتَحَنَ اللَّهُ قَلْبَهُ لِلْإِيمَانِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Sabbah Al Muzny, from Al Haris Bin Haseyr, from Al Asbagh Bin Nubata, from Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Our (a.s.) Hadeeth are difficult, becoming more difficult, harsh, reining. None can tolerate it except three: an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (a.s.), or a Momin servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.** Cast a little to the people. So, the one who recognise, increase it for him, and one who denies, withhold.'

chapter: Chapter 12: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), Their (a.s.) Instructions are Difficult, Becoming More Difficult
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Muhammad Bin Abu Umeyr, from Mansour, from Mukhallad Bin Hamza Bin Nasr, from Abu Rabie Al Shamy, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I was seated with him (a.s.), and I saw that Abu Ja'far (a.s.) had stood up and raised his (a.s.) head and he (a.s.) was saying: 'O Abu Al-Rabie! Words of Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.): **Our (a.s.) instructions are difficult, becoming more difficult. None can tolerate it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (s.a.w.a.), or a Momin servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ مُخَلَّدِ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ نَصْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي رَبِيعِ الشَّامِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَهُ جَالِساً فَرَأَيْتُ أَنَّ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع قَدْ قَامَ فَرَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ يَا أَبَا الرَّبِيعِ حَدِيثٌ تَضَعُهُ الشَّيعَةُ بِأَلْسِنَتِهَا لَا تَدْرِي مَا كُنْهُهُ قُلْتُ مَا هُوَ جَعَلَنِيَ اللَّهُ فداك قَالَ قَوْلُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ع إِنَّ أَمْرَنَا صَعْبٌ مُسْتَصْعَبٌ لَا يَحْتَمِلُهُ إِلَّا مَلَكٌ مُقَرَّبٌ أَوْ نَبِيٌّ مُرْسَلٌ أَوْ عَبْدٌ مُؤْمِنٌ امْتَحَنَ اللَّهُ قَلْبَهُ لِلْإِيمَانِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Muhammad Bin Abu Umeyr, from Mansour, from Mukhallad Bin Hamza Bin Nasr, from Abu Rabie Al Shamy, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I was seated with him (a.s.), and I saw that Abu Ja'far (a.s.) had stood up and raised his (a.s.) head and he (a.s.) was saying: 'O Abu Al-Rabie! Hadeeth get mulled over by the Shias with their tongues, you don't know what its essence is'. I said, 'May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Make me to be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! What is it?'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: 'Words of Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.): **Our (a.s.) instructions are difficult, becoming more difficult. None can tolerate it except an Angel of Proximity, or a Messenger Prophet (s.a.w.a.), or a Momin servant whose heart Allah (a.z.w.j.) Tested for the Eman.**'

chapter: Chapter 13: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), They (a.s.) are the Guides Guiding to What the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) Came With
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina, from Bureyd Al Ijaly, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'But rather, you are a Warner, and for every people there is a Guide' [13:7], he (a.s.) said: '**Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) is the warner, and during every era there is a guide from us (a.s.) guiding them to what the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) of Allah (a.z.w.j.) came with. Then the guide from after him (s.a.w.a.) is Ali (a.s.), then the successors (a.s.), one after one.**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَذَيْنَةَ عَنْ بُرَيْدِ الْعِجْلِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللهِ تَعَالَى إِنما أَنْتَ مُنْذِرٌ وَ لِكُلِّ قَوْمٍ هَادٍ قَالَ رَسُولُ الله ص الْمُنْذِرُ وَ فِي كُلِّ زَمَانٍ مِنَّا هَادٍ يَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَى مَا جَاءَ بِهِ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ الهُدَاهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ عَلِيٌّ ع ثُمَّ الْأَوْصِيَاءُ وَاحِداً بَعْدَ وَاحِدٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina, from Bureyd Al Ijaly, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'But rather, you are a Warner, and for every people there is a Guide' [13:7],
thaqalaynMatn: he (a.s.) said: '**Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) is the warner, and during every era there is a guide from us (a.s.) guiding them (people) to what the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) of Allah (a.z.w.j.) came with. Then the guide from after him (s.a.w.a.) is Ali (a.s.), then the successors (a.s.), one after one.**'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) are the Truthful
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated by Al Hussein Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Ahmad Bin Aaiz, from Ibn Uzina, from Bureyd Al Ijaly who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'O you who believe! Fear Allah and be with the truthful ones' [9:119]. He (a.s.) said: '**It means us (a.s.).**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَائِدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنْ بُرَيْدِ الْعِجْلِيِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَكُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ قَالَ إِيَّانَا عَلَى.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated by Al Hussein Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Ahmad Bin Aaiz, from Ibn Uzina, from Bureyd Al Ijaly who said, 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'O you who believe! Fear Allah and be with the truthful ones' [9:119].
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**It means us (a.s.).**'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding the Difference Between the Imams (a.s.) of Justice from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (Leaders) of Tyranny from Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Talha Bin Zayd and Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar with other than this chain, raising it to Talha Bin Zayd, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'I (a.s.) read in the book of my (a.s.) father (a.s.): **The Imams in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) are two (type of) Imams - An Imam (a.s.) of guidance and an imam of straying.**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ بِغَيْرِ هَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ يَرْفَعُهُ إِلَى طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع قَالَ : قَرَأْتُ فِي كِتَابِ أَبي الأَئِمَّةُ فِي كِتَابِ اللهِ إِمَامَانِ إِمَامُ هُدًى وَ إِمَامُ ضَلَالٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Talha Bin Zayd and Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar with other than this chain, raising it to Talha Bin Zayd, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I (a.s.) read in the book of my (a.s.) father (a.s.): **The Imams in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) are two (type of) Imams - An Imam (a.s.) of guidance and an imam of straying.** As for the Imams (a.s.) of guidance, they (a.s.) giving priority to the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) orders, and Judgment of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) decisions; and as for the imams of straying, they are giving priority to their own orders before the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.).'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding the Difference Between the Imams (a.s.) of Justice from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (Leaders) of Tyranny from Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Ali, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Talha Bin Zayd, from Ja'far (a.s.) Bin Muhammad (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said: '**The Imams in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) are two (types of) Imams**. Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Said: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73), not by the instructions of the people. **They (a.s.) advance the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) own orders, and Judgment of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) own decisions**. And Said: "And We Made them imams calling to the Fire" (28:41). **They are advancing their own orders before the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and their own decisions before the Judgments of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and taking with their own whims in opposition to what is in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٌّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ: الْأَئِمَّةُ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ إِمَامَانِ قَالَ اللهُ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا لَا بِأَمْرِ النَّاسِ يُقَدِّمُونَ أَمْرَ اللَّهِ قَبْلَ أَمْرِهِمْ وَ حُكْمَ اللَّهِ قَبْلَ حُكْمِهِمْ وَ قَالَ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ يُقَدِّمُونَ أَمْرَهُمْ قَبْلَ أَمْرِ اللهِ وَ حُكْمَهُمْ قَبْلَ حُكْمِ اللهِ وَ يَأْخُذُونَ بِأَهْوَائِهِمْ خِلَافَاً لِمَا فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein Bin Ali, from Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Talha Bin Zayd, from Ja'far (a.s.) Bin Muhammad (a.s.), from his father (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Imams in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) are two (types of) Imams**. Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Said: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73), not by the instructions of the people. **They (a.s.) advance the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) own orders, and Judgment of Allah (a.z.w.j.) before their (a.s.) own decisions**. And Said: "And We Made them imams calling to the Fire" (28:41). **They are advancing their own orders before the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and their own decisions before the Judgments of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and taking with their own whims in opposition to what is in the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding the Difference Between the Imams (a.s.) of Justice from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (Leaders) of Tyranny from Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by one of our companions, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Al Hussein Bin Abu Al A'la, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Surely, the world cannot exist unless and in it are two Imams, a righteous one and an immoral one**, those Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Said: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73). And as for the immoral, they are those Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Said: "**And We Made them imams calling to the Fire, and on the Day of Judgment, they will not be helped**" (28:41).'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الدُّنْيَا لَا تَكُونُ إِلَّا وَ فِيهَا إِمَامَانِ بَر وَ فَاجِرٌ فَالْبَرُ الَّذِي قَالَ اللهُ تَعَالَى وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا وَ أَمَّا الْفَاجِرُ فَالَّذِي قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ وَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا يُنْصَرُونَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by one of our companions, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Al Hussein Bin Abu Al A'la, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Surely, the world cannot exist unless and in it are two Imams, a righteous one and an immoral one**, those Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Said: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73). And as for the immoral, they are those Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Said: "**And We Made them imams calling to the Fire, and on the Day of Judgment, they will not be helped**" (28:41).'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding the Difference Between the Imams (a.s.) of Justice from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (Leaders) of Tyranny from Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Usman Bin Isa, from Ali, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**The people are not set right except by a just Imam and an immoral imam**. Surely Allah (a.z.w.j.) says: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73), and Said: "And We made them imams calling to the Fire" (28:41).'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَلِيَّ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: لَا يُصْلِحُ النَّاسَ إِلَّا إِمَامٌ عَادِلٌ وَ إِمَامٌ فَاجِرٌ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا وَ قَالَ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Usman Bin Isa, from Ali, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The people are not set right except by a just Imam and an immoral imam**. Surely Allah (a.z.w.j.) says: "And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command" (21:73), and Said: "And We made them imams calling to the Fire" (28:41).'

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding Recognizing the Imams (a.s.) of Guidance from the Imams of Straying
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. Ahmad Bin Muhammad narrated to us, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Al-Hussain Bin Sa'eed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al-Hussain Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): 'And We made them Imams guiding by Our command' (21:73). He (a.s.) said: '**It is about the Imams from the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْمُخْتَارِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا قَالَ فِي الْأَئِمَّةِ مِنْ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. Ahmad Bin Muhammad narrated to us, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Al-Hussain Bin Sa'eed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al-Hussain Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): 'And We made them Imams guiding by Our command' (21:73).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**It is about the Imams from the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding Recognizing the Imams (a.s.) of Guidance from the Imams of Straying
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Al-Hussain Bin Sa'eed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al-Hussain Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): 'And We made them imams calling to the Fire' (28:41). He (a.s.) said: '**It is about the imams of tyranny from besides the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْمُخْتَارِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ قَالَ فِي أَئِمَّةِ الْجَوْرِ مِنْ غَيْرِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Al-Hussain Bin Sa'eed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al-Hussain Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): 'And We made them imams calling to the Fire' (28:41).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**It is about the imams of tyranny from besides the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding Recognizing the Imams (a.s.) of Guidance from the Imams of Straying
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussain Bin Saeed, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, from Yaqoub Bin Shuaib who said, 'I asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) about the saying of Allah: "**And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command**" (21:73). He (a.s.) said: "They are the Imams (a.s.) from the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)." And about the saying: "**And We made them imams calling to the Fire**" (28:41). He (a.s.) said: "They are the imams of tyranny from besides the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)."'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّضْرِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزةَ عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا قَالَ هُمُ الْأَئِمَّةُ مِنْ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص وَ عَنْ قَوْلِهِ وَ جَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ قَالَ هُمُ أَئِمَّةُ الْجَوْرِ مِنْ غَيْرِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussain Bin Saeed, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, from Yaqoub Bin Shuaib who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) about the saying of Allah: "**And We made them as Imams guiding by Our Command**" (21:73). He (a.s.) said: "They are the Imams (a.s.) from the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)." And about the saying: "**And We made them imams calling to the Fire**" (28:41). He (a.s.) said: "They are the imams of tyranny from besides the progeny of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)."'

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding Recognizing the Imams (a.s.) of Guidance from the Imams of Straying
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Mansour who said, 'I asked him (a.s.) about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "And when they commit an immorality they say: We found our fathers doing this, and Allah has Commanded us for it. Say: Surely, Allah does not Command for immoralities; are you saying against Allah what you do not know?" (7:28). He (a.s.) said: "**Have you ever seen anyone claiming that Allah (a.z.w.j.) Commanded with adultery, or drinking wine?**" I said: "No." He (a.s.) said: "**Then what are these immoralities that they claim Allah Commanded with?**" I said: "Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His Guardian (a.s.) are more Knowing." He (a.s.) said: "**These are regarding the imams of tyranny; they claimed that Allah Commanded them with taking as leaders a people whom Allah did not Command to be taken as leaders**. So Allah refuted that upon them and informed us that they had said lies against Him, so Allah named them 'immorality' (fuhsh)."'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَنْصُورٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى وَ إِذا فَعَلُوا فَاحِشَةً قَالُوا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهَا آبَاءَنَا وَ اللَّهُ أَمَرَنَا بِهَا قُلْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَأْمُرُ بِالْفَحْشَاءِ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ فَقَالَ أَرَأَيْتَ أَحَدٌ يَزْعُمُ أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَمَرَ بِالزِّنَاءِ وَ شُرْبِ الْخَمْرِ أَوْ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْمَحَارِمِ فَقُلْتُ لا فَقَالَ مَا هَذِهِ الْفَاحِشَةُ الَّتِي يَدْعُونَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَمَرَ بِمَا فَقُلْتُ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ وَ وَلِيُّهُ قَالَ فَإِنَّ هَذِهِ فِي أَئِمَّةِ الْجَوْرِ ادَّعَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَمَرَهُمْ بِالايتِمامِ بِقَوْمٍ لَمْ يَأْمُرِ الله بالايتمَامِ بِهِمْ فَرَدَّ اللهُ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ أَخْبَرَنَا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ قَالُوا عَلَيْهِ الْكَذِبَ فَسَمَّى اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ فَاحِشَةً.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Mansour who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I asked him (a.s.) about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "And when they commit an immorality they say: We found our fathers doing this, and Allah has Commanded us for it. Say: Surely, Allah does not Command for immoralities; are you saying against Allah what you do not know?" (7:28). He (a.s.) said: "**Have you ever seen anyone claiming that Allah (a.z.w.j.) Commanded with adultery, or drinking wine?**" I said: "No." He (a.s.) said: "**Then what are these immoralities that they claim Allah Commanded with?**" I said: "Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His Guardian (a.s.) are more Knowing." He (a.s.) said: "**These are regarding the imams of tyranny; they claimed that Allah Commanded them with taking as leaders a people whom Allah did not Command to be taken as leaders**. So Allah refuted that upon them and informed us that they had said lies against Him, so Allah named them 'immorality' (fuhsh)."'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and that Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Obligated Obedience and Cordiality to Them (a.s.), and They (a.s.) are the Envied Ones Upon What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Given Them (a.s.) of His (a.z.w.j.) Grace
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. Muhammad Bin Isa narrated, from a man, from Hisham Bin Al Hakam who said: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.): "Or are they envying the people upon what Allah has Given them from His Grace? So We have Given the Progeny of Ibrahim, the Book and the Wisdom, and have Given them a grand kingdom" (4:54). What is that grand kingdom?' He (a.s.) said: '**The Obligatory obedience, and from that obedience, Hell would be for them on the Day of Qiyamah, O Hisham!**'
arabicText: 1- مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلى ما آتَاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَقَدْ آتَيْنَا آلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الكِتابَ وَ الحِكْمَةَ وَ آتَيْنَاهُمْ مُلكاً عَظِيماً مَا ذَلِكَ الْمُلْكُ الْعَظِيمُ قَالَ فَرْضُ الطَّاعَةِ وَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ طَاعَةُ جَهَنَّمَ هُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَا هِشَامُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. Muhammad Bin Isa narrated, from a man, from Hisham Bin Al Hakam who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.): "Or are they envying the people upon what Allah has Given them from His Grace? So We have Given the Progeny of Ibrahim, the Book and the Wisdom, and have Given them a grand kingdom" (4:54). What is that grand kingdom?' He (a.s.) said: '**The Obligatory obedience, and from that obedience, Hell would be for them on the Day of Qiyamah, O Hisham!**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and that Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Obligated Obedience and Cordiality to Them (a.s.), and They (a.s.) are the Envied Ones Upon What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Given Them (a.s.) of His (a.z.w.j.) Grace
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. Ahmad Bin Muhammad narrated to us, from Al-Ahwazi, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al Hussein Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "Or are they envying the people upon what Allah has Given them from His Grace? So We have Given the Progeny of Ibrahim, the Book and the Wisdom, and have Given them a grand kingdom" (4:54). He (a.s.) said: '**It is the Obligatory obedience.**'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْأَهْوَازِيِّ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْمُخْتَارِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلى ما آتَاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَقَدْ آتَيْنَا آلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَآتَيْنَاهُمْ مُلكاً عَظِيماً قَالَ الطَّاعَةَ الْمَفْرُوضَةَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. Ahmad Bin Muhammad narrated to us, from Al-Ahwazi, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Al Hussein Bin Al-Mukhtar, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "Or are they envying the people upon what Allah has Given them from His Grace? So We have Given the Progeny of Ibrahim, the Book and the Wisdom, and have Given them a grand kingdom" (4:54).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**It is the Obligatory obedience.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding Imams (a.s.) of Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and that Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted Obligated Obedience and Cordiality to Them (a.s.), and They (a.s.) are the Envied Ones Upon What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Given Them (a.s.) of His (a.z.w.j.) Grace
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. Ahmad Bin Muhammad narrated to us, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding the saying of Allah (the Exalted): "Or are they envying the people upon what Allah has Given them from His Grace?" (4:54). He (a.s.) said: '**We are the envied ones.**'
