thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) saying: '**I am an eye of Allah, and I am a Side of Allah, and I am a Hand of Allah, and I am a door of Allah.**'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. Ahmad Bin Musa, from Al Hassan Bin Musa Al Khashab, from Ali Bin Hassan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Kaseer who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**We are the Masters of the Commands of Allah, and Treasures of the Knowledge of Allah, and receptacle of the Revelation of Allah, and People of the Religion of Allah, and upon us descended the Book of Allah, and through us Allah is being worshipped, and had it not been for us, Allah would not have been recognised, and we are the inheritors of the Prophet of Allah and are his family.**' [cite: 939, 941, 949]
arabicText: 3- أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَى الْخَشَابِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ نَحْنُ وَلَاهُ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ وَ حَزَنَةٌ عِلْمِ اللهِ وَ عَيْبَةُ وَحْيِ اللَّهِ وَ أَهْلُ دِينِ اللَّهِ وَ عَلَيْنَا نَزَلَ كِتَابُ اللَّهِ وَبِنَا عُبِدَ اللَّهُ وَ لَوْلَانَا مَا عُرِفَ اللَّهُ وَ نَحْنُ وَرَبُّهُ نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ وَ عِتْرَتُهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. Ahmad Bin Musa, from Al Hassan Bin Musa Al Khashab, from Ali Bin Hassan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Kaseer who said,
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**We are the Masters of the Commands of Allah, and Treasures of the Knowledge of Allah, and receptacle of the Revelation of Allah, and People of the Religion of Allah, and upon us descended the Book of Allah, and through us Allah is being worshipped, and had it not been for us, Allah would not have been recognised, and we are the inheritors of the Prophet of Allah and are his family.**'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Al Barqy, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Abdullah Bin Abu Yafour who said, 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: 'O Ibn Abu Yafour! Allah Blessed and Exalted is One, Alone with the Oneness, Individual with His Command. So, He Created creatures and particularised them for that Command. **We are they, O Ibn Yafour! We are the Divine Authorities of Allah among His servants and His witnesses among His creatures, and His trustees, and His treasurers upon His Knowledge, and the callers to His way, and the ones standing with that. Thus, the one who obeys us, so he has obeyed Allah.**' [cite: 950-955]
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَا ابْنَ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى وَاحِدٌ مُتَوَحِّدٌ بِالْوَحْدَانِيَّةِ مُتَفَرِّدُ بِأَمْرِهِ فَخَلَقَ خَلْقاً فَفَرَّدَهُمْ لِذَلِكَ الْأَمْرِ فَنَحْنُ هُمْ يَا ابْنَ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ فَنَحْنُ حُحَجُ اللَّهِ فِي عِبَادِهِ وَ شُهَدَاؤُهُ فِي خَلْقِهِ وَ أَمَنَاؤُهُ وَ خُزَانُهُ عَلَى عِلْمِهِ وَ الدَّاعُونَ إِلَى سَبِيلِهِ وَ الْقَائِمُونَ بِذَلِكَ فَمَنْ أَطَاعَنَا فَقَدْ أَطَاعَ اللَّهَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Al Barqy, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Abdullah Bin Abu Yafour who said, 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'O Ibn Abu Yafour! Allah Blessed and Exalted is One, Alone with the Oneness, Individual with His Command. So, He Created creatures and particularised them for that Command. **We are they, O Ibn Yafour! We are the Divine Authorities of Allah among His servants and His witnesses among His creatures, and His trustees, and His treasurers upon His Knowledge, and the callers to His way, and the ones standing with that. Thus, the one who obeys us, so he has obeyed Allah.**'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Husayn, from Fazalat, from Al Qasim, from Al Qasim Bin Bureyd, from Malik Al Juhny who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**I am a tree from a Side of Allah, so the one who connects with us Allah would Connect with him.**' Then he recited this Verse: **'Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!''** (39:56). [cite: 956, 958-960]
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ بُرَيْدٍ عَنْ مَالِكٍ الْجُهَنِي قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ ع يَقُولُ أَنَا شَجَرَةٌ مِنْ جَنْبِ اللَّهِ فَمَنْ وَصَلَنَا وَصَلَهُ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يا حسرتي على ما فَرَّطْتُ فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ وَ إِنَّ كُنْتُ لَمِنَ السَّاخِرِينَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Husayn, from Fazalat, from Al Qasim, from Al Qasim Bin Bureyd, from Malik Al Juhny who said,
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**I am a tree from a Side of Allah, so the one who connects with us Allah would Connect with him.**' Then he recited this Verse: **'Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!''** (39:56).'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Hamza Bin Bazie, from Ali Al Saie who said: 'I asked Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) (and) Abu Al-Hassan Al-Maazy (7th Imam (a.s.)), about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!'" (39:56). He (a.s.) said: "**The Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.), it is Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and like that are the ones from the successors (a.s.) from after him (a.s.) with the elevated position, until the matter ends up to their (a.s.) last one.**"' [cite: 963-967]
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ بَرِيعٍ عَنْ عَلِيَّ السَّائِي قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَاعِ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الْمَاضِي عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَل أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يا حَسْرَتى عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ فِي جَنْبِ اللهِ وَ إِنْ كُنْتُ لَمِنَ السَّاخِرِينَ قَالَ جَنْبُ اللَّهِ هُوَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَكَذَلِكَ مَنْ كَانَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ مِنَ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ بِالْمَكَانِ الْمَرْفُوعِ إِلَى أَنْ يَنْتَهِيَ الْأَمْرُ إِلَى آخِرِهِمْ وَ اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ كَائِنٌ بَعْدَهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Hamza Bin Bazie, from Ali Al Saie who said: 'I asked Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) (and) Abu Al-Hassan Al-Maazy (7th Imam (a.s.)), about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!'" (39:56).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**The Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.), it is Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and like that are the ones from the successors (a.s.) from after him (a.s.) with the elevated position, until the matter ends up to their (a.s.) last one, and Allah (a.z.w.j.) is most Knowing with the ones (a.s.) who would be existing from after him (a.s.).**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Muhammad Bin Suleyman, from his father who said: 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: "**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Selected us (a.s.) for Himself (a.z.w.j.), and Made us (a.s.) to be His (a.z.w.j.) elites from His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon His (a.z.w.j.) Revelations, and His (a.z.w.j.) treasurers, and place of His (a.z.w.j.) Secrets and receptacles of His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge.** Then He (a.z.w.j.) Gave us (a.s.) the intercession (right to intercede). **We (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) listening Ear, and His (a.z.w.j.) speaking Tongue by His (a.z.w.j.) Permission, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon whatever is Revealed, from an excuse and warning and argument.**"' [cite: 978-982]
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا ، عَبَّادُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللهِ ع إِنَّ اللهَ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى انْتَحَبَنَا لِنَفْسِهِ فَجَعَلْنَا صَفْوَتَهُ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ وَ أَمَنَاءَهُ عَلَى وَحْبِهِ وَ خَزَانَهُ فِي أَرْضِهِ وَ مَوْضِعَ سِرِّهِ وَ عَيْبَةَ عِلْمِهِ ثُمَّ أَعْطَانَا الشَّفَاعَةَ فَنَحْنُ أُدُنُهُ السَّامِعَةُ وَ عَيْنُهُ النَّاظِرَهُ وَ لِسَانُهُ النَّاطِقُ بِإِذْنِهِ وَ أَمَنَاؤُهُ عَلَى مَا نَزَلَ مِنْ عُذْرٍ وَ نُذْرٍ وَ حُجَّةٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Muhammad Bin Suleyman, from his father who said: 'Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Selected us (a.s.) for Himself (a.z.w.j.), and Made us (a.s.) to be His (a.z.w.j.) elites from His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon His (a.z.w.j.) Revelations, and His (a.z.w.j.) treasurers, and place of His (a.z.w.j.) Secrets and receptacles of His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge.** Then He (a.z.w.j.) Gave us (a.s.) the intercession (right to intercede). **We (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) listening Ear, and His (a.z.w.j.) speaking Tongue by His (a.z.w.j.) Permission, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon whatever is Revealed, from an excuse and warning and argument.**'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Al Rabie Muhammad Al Musly, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman who said: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "The Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: 'Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!' (39:56)." He (a.s.) said: "**Ali (a.s.) is Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"' [cite: 983-986]
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي الربيع مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُسْلِيِّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ أَنْ تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يَا حَسْرَتي عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ فِي جَنْبِ اللَّهِ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عِ جَنْبُ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Al Rabie Muhammad Al Musly, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman who said: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "The Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: 'Lest a soul should be saying, 'O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!' (39:56)."
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**Ali (a.s.) is Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Abu Khalid Al Qammat, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: 'I said to him (a.s.), "O son (a.s.) of Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! **What are your (a.s.) statuses from your (a.s.) Lord (a.z.w.j.)?**" He (a.s.) said: "**His (a.z.w.j.) Divine Authorities upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) door which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon His (a.z.w.j.) Secrets, and interpreters of His (a.z.w.j.) Revelation.**"' [cite: 987-990]
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ أَبِي خَالِدٍ الْقَمَاطِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ يَا ابْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ مَا مَنْزِلَتُكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَقَالَ حُجَّتُهُ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ وَ بَابُهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ وَ أَمَنَاؤُهُ عَلَى سِرِّهِ وَ تَرَاجِمَةُ وَحْيِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Abu Khalid Al Qammat, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: 'I said to him (a.s.), "O son (a.s.) of Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)!"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**His (a.z.w.j.) Divine Authorities upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) door which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from, and His (a.z.w.j.) trustees upon His (a.z.w.j.) Secrets, and interpreters of His (a.z.w.j.) Revelation.**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah Al Basry, from Abu Al Magra'a, from Abu Baseer, from Khaysama, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: "**We (a.s.) are the Side (Proximity) of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Elites, and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Choice, and we (a.s.) are a depository of the inheritances of the Prophets (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are trustees of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are Divine Authorities of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are the Constituents of the Eman, and we (a.s.) are the foundations of Al-Islam;** and we (a.s.) are Mercy of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and **we (a.s.) are those, Allah (a.z.w.j.) Began by us (a.s.) and He (a.z.w.j.) will be Ending by us (a.s.)**, and we (a.s.) are the Imams (a.s.) of guidance, and lamps for the darkness, and we (a.s.) are the minarets of guidance, and we (a.s.) are the foremost ones, and we (a.s.) are the latter ones, and **we (a.s.) are the raised flag for the creatures, one who adheres with us (a.s.) would catch-up (with Salvation) and one who stays behind would drown;** and we (a.s.) are the guides of the resplendent (faces), and we (a.s.) are the Choice of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and **we (a.s.) are the Way to and Path of Allah (a.z.w.j.), the Straight (Approach) to Allah (a.z.w.j.)**, and we (a.s.) are from the Favours of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and we (a.s.) are the Manifesto, and we (a.s.) are the mine of Prophet-hood, and we (a.s.) the place of the Message, and we (a.s.) are those, to us (a.s.) is the interchange of the Angels; and we (a.s.) are the Lantern for the one who wants to be illuminated by us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are the way for the one who is guided by us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are the guides to the Paradise, and we (a.s.) are the honour of Al-Islam, and we (a.s.) are the bridges and the archways, one who passes upon these would precede and one who stays behind from these would be obliterated; and we (a.s.) are the greatest peak, and we (a.s.) are those, due to us (a.s.) the Mercy descends and due to us (a.s.) you are being quenched the rain, and we (a.s.) are those due to us (a.s.) the Punishment is diverted away from you all. So, the one who recognises us (a.s.), and helps us (a.s.), and recognises our (a.s.) rights, and takes with our (a.s.) instructions, so he is from us (a.s.) and (his return is) to us (a.s.)."' [cite: 1002-1012, 1018-1019]
arabicText: 10 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَصْرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي الْمَغْرَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ نَحْنُ حَنْبُ اللَّهِ وَ نَحْنُ صَفْوَتُهُ وَ نَحْنُ حِبَرَتُهُ وَنَحْنُ مُسْتَوْدَعُ مَوَارِيثِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ نَحْنُ أَمَنَاءُ اللَّهِ وَ نَحْنُ حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ وَ نَحْنُ أَرْكَانُ الإِيمَانِ وَ نَحْنُ دَعَائِمُ الْإِسْلَامِ وَ نَحْنُ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ وَ نَحْنُ الَّذِينَ بِنَا يَفْتَحُ اللَّهُ وَبِنَا يَحْتِمُ وَ نَحْنُ أَئِمَّةُ الْهُدَى وَ مَصَابِيحُ الدُّجَى وَ نَحْنُ مَنَارُ الْهُدَى وَ نَحْنُ السَّابِقُونَ وَ نَحْنُ الْآخِرُونَ وَنَحْنُ الْعَلَمُ الْمَرْفُوعُ لِلْخَلْقِ مَنْ تَمَسَّكَ بِنَا لَحِقَ وَ مَنْ تَخَلَّفَ عَنَّا غَرِقَ وَ نَحْنُ فَادَةُ الْغُرُ الْمُحَجَّلِينَ وَ نَحْنُ خِيَرَةُ اللهِ وَ نَحْنُ الطَّرِيقُ وَ صِرَاط اللهِ الْمُسْتَقِيمُ إِلَى اللهِ وَ نَحْنُ مِنْ نِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ وَ نَحْنُ الْمِنْهَاجُ وَ نَحْنُ مَعْدِنُ النبوَّة وَ نَحْنُ مَوْضِعُ الرِّسَالَةِ وَ نَحْنُ الَّذِينَ إِلَيْنَا مُخْتَلَفُ الْمَلَائِكَةِ و نَحْنُ السَّرَاجُ لِمَنِ اسْتَضَاءَ بِنَا وَ نَحْنُ السَّبِيلُ لِمَنِ اقْتَدَى بِنَا وَ نَحْنُ الهُدَاةُ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ نَحْنُ عِزَّ الْإِسْلَام وَ نَحْنُ الْجُسُورُ وَالْقَنَاطِرُ مَنْ مَضَى عَلَيْهَا سَبَقَ وَ مَنْ تَخَلَّفَ عَنْهَا مُحِق و نحن السَّنَامُ الْأَعْظَمُ وَنَحْنُ الَّذِينَ بِنَا تَنْزِلُ الرَّحْمَةُ وَ بِنَا تُسْقَوْنَ الْغَيْثَ وَ نَحْنُ الَّذِينَ بِنَا يُصْرَفُ عَنْكُمُ الْعَذَابُ فَمَنْ عَرَفْنَا وَ نَصَرْنَا وَ عَرَفَ حَقَّنَا وَ أخَذَ بِأَمْرِنَا فَهُوَ مِنَّا وَ إِلَيْنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah Al Basry, from Abu Al Magra'a, from Abu Baseer, from Khaysama, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "**We (a.s.) are the Side (Proximity) of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Elites, and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Choice, and we (a.s.) are a depository of the inheritances of the Prophets (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are trustees of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are Divine Authorities of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and we (a.s.) are the Constituents of the Eman, and we (a.s.) are the foundations of Al-Islam;** and we (a.s.) are Mercy of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and **we (a.s.) are those, Allah (a.z.w.j.) Began by us (a.s.) and He (a.z.w.j.) will be Ending by us (a.s.)**, and we (a.s.) are the Imams (a.s.) of guidance, and lamps for the darkness, and we (a.s.) are the minarets of guidance, and we (a.s.) are the foremost ones, and we (a.s.) are the latter ones, and **we (a.s.) are the raised flag for the creatures, one who adheres with us (a.s.) would catch-up (with Salvation) and one who stays behind would drown;** and we (a.s.) are the guides of the resplendent (faces), and we (a.s.) are the Choice of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and **we (a.s.) are the Way to and Path of Allah (a.z.w.j.), the Straight (Approach) to Allah (a.z.w.j.)**, and we (a.s.) are from the Favours of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and we (a.s.) are the Manifesto, and we (a.s.) are the mine of Prophet-hood, and we (a.s.) the place of the Message, and we (a.s.) are those, to us (a.s.) is the interchange of the Angels; and we (a.s.) are the Lantern for the one who wants to be illuminated by us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are the way for the one who is guided by us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) are the guides to the Paradise, and we (a.s.) are the honour of Al-Islam, and we (a.s.) are the bridges and the archways, one who passes upon these would precede and one who stays behind from these would be obliterated; and we (a.s.) are the greatest peak, and we (a.s.) are those, due to us (a.s.) the Mercy descends and due to us (a.s.) you are being quenched the rain, and we (a.s.) are those due to us (a.s.) the Punishment is diverted away from you all. So, the one who recognises us (a.s.), and helps us (a.s.), and recognises our (a.s.) rights, and takes with our (a.s.) instructions, so he is from us (a.s.) and (his return is) to us (a.s.)."

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from his father, from Muhammad, from Ibn Uzina, from Bureyd Al ljaly who said: 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: "And like that, We Made you (Imams) as an intermediary community in order for you (Imams) to become witnesses upon the people, and the Rasool to become a witness upon you" (2:143). He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.) are the intermediary community, and we (a.s.) are the witnesses of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) Divine Authorities in His (a.z.w.j.) earth.**"' [cite: 1020-1024]
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ أَبِيهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَذَيْنَةَ عَنْ بُرَيْدِ الْعِجْلِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى وَ كَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَاكُمْ أُمَّةً وَسَطًا لِتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَ يَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيداً قَالَ نَحْنُ أُمَّةُ الْوَسَطِ وَ نَحْنُ شُهَدَاءُ اللهِ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ وَ حُجَّتُهُ فِي أَرْضِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from his father, from Muhammad, from Ibn Uzina, from Bureyd Al ljaly who said: 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: "And like that, We Made you (Imams) as an intermediary community in order for you (Imams) to become witnesses upon the people, and the Rasool to become a witness upon you" (2:143).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.) are the intermediary community, and we (a.s.) are the witnesses of Allah (a.z.w.j.) upon His (a.z.w.j.) creatures, and His (a.z.w.j.) Divine Authorities in His (a.z.w.j.) earth.**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Hamza Bin Bazie, from Ali Bin Suweyd who said: 'From Abu Al-Hassan Al-Musa (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: "O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!" (39:56). He (a.s.) said: "**The Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.) is Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and like that are the ones (a.s.) from the successors (a.s.) to come after him (a.s.), with the lofty position, until the matter ends up to the last of them (a.s.).**"' [cite: 1025-1028]
arabicText: 12 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ بَرِيعٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى يا حَسْرَتي عَلى ما فَرَّطْتُ في جَنْبِ اللَّهِ قَالَ جَنْبُ اللَّهِ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَكَذَلِكَ مَنْ كَانَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ مِنَ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ بِالْمَكَانِ الرَّفِيعِ إِلَى أَنْ يَنْتَهِيَ الْأَمْرُ إِلَى آخِرِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Hamza Bin Bazie, from Ali Bin Suweyd who said: 'From Abu Al-Hassan Al-Musa (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: "O regret, upon what I wasted regarding the Side of Allah, and I was from the mocking ones!" (39:56).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**The Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.) is Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and like that are the ones (a.s.) from the successors (a.s.) to come after him (a.s.), with the lofty position, until the matter ends up to the last of them (a.s.).**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail Al Neshaboury, from Ahmad bin Al Hassan Al Kufy, from Ismail Bin Nasr, and Ali Bin Abdullah Al Hashimy, from Abdul Muhazim Bin Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: 'Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) was saying: "**I am the Knowledge of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the retaining Heart of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and the speaking Tongue of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and the looking Eye of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the Hand of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"' [cite: 1029-1031]
arabicText: 13- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ النَّيْشَابُورِي عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الْكُونِي عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ نَصْرٍ وَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمَاشِمِي عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمُرَاحِمِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع يَقُولُ أَنَا عِلْمُ اللهِ وَ أَنَا قَلْبُ اللَّهِ الْوَاعِي وَ لِسَانُ اللَّهِ النَّاطِقُ وَ عَيْنُ اللَّهِ النَّاظِرُ وَ أنَا حَنَّبُ اللَّهِ وَ أَنَا يَدُ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail Al Neshaboury, from Ahmad bin Al Hassan Al Kufy, from Ismail Bin Nasr, and Ali Bin Abdullah Al Hashimy, from Abdul Muhazim Bin Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) was saying: "**I am the Knowledge of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the retaining Heart of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and the speaking Tongue of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and the looking Eye of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and I am the Hand of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from al Barqy, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Al Halby, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Malik Al Juhny who said: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: "**I am a tree from the Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.) or (said): 'His (a.z.w.j.) part, so the one who connects with us (a.s.) connects with Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"' [cite: 1042-1045]
arabicText: 14 - حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْبَرْقِي عَنِ النَّصْرِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى الْخَلَبِيِّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ مَالِكٍ الْجُهَنِي قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَ يَقُولُ إِنَّا شَجَرَةٌ مِنْ جَنْبِ اللهِ أَوْ جِذْوَةٌ فَمَنْ وَصَلَنَا وَصَلَهُ اللَّهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from al Barqy, from Al Nazr Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Al Halby, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Malik Al Juhny who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: "**I am a tree from the Side of Allah (a.z.w.j.) or (said): 'His (a.z.w.j.) part, so the one who connects with us (a.s.) connects with Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"'

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ibn Jabala, from Al Batainy, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "Can you narrate to me with a Hadeeth regarding you (Imams (a.s.))?" He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.) are the Masters of the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and inheritors of the Revelation of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and family of the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"' [cite: 1046-1049]
arabicText: 15 - حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنِ ابْنِ حَبَلَةَ عَنِ الْبَطَائِنِي عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع أَلا تُحَدِّثْنِي فِيكُمْ بِحَدِيثٍ قَالَ نَحْنُ وَلَاهُ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ وَ وَرَبَّهُ وَحْيِ اللَّهِ وَ عِتْرَةُ نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ibn Jabala, from Al Batainy, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "Can you narrate to me with a Hadeeth regarding you (Imams (a.s.))?"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.) are the Masters of the Commands of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and inheritors of the Revelation of Allah (a.z.w.j.) and family of the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"

chapter: Chapter 3: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), they are the Divine Authorities of Allah, and the Door of Allah, and Masters of the Command of Allah, and the Face of Allah which He can be accessed, and Side of Allah, and Eye of Allah, and Treasurers of His Knowledge, Majestic is His Majesty, and the Givers of it
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ali Bin Al Salt, from Al Hakam, and Ismail, from Bureyd who said: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying: "**By us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is worshipped, and by us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is recognised, and by us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is considered as being One, and Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is the veil of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"' [cite: 1050-1052]
arabicText: 16 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٌّ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الصَّلْتِ عَنِ الْحَكَمِ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ بِنَا عُبِدَ اللَّهُ وَبِنَا عُرِفَ اللَّهُ وَبِنَا وَحدَ اللَّهُ وَ مُحَمَّدٌ ص حِجَابُ اللَّهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 16. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ali Bin Al Salt, from Al Hakam, and Ismail, from Bureyd who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying: "**By us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is worshipped, and by us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is recognised, and by us (a.s.) Allah (a.z.w.j.) is considered as being One, and Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is the veil of Allah (a.z.w.j.).**"'

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Baseer, from Al Hars Bin Al Mugheira who said: 'We were in the presence of Abu Abdullah (a.s.), and a man asked him (a.s.) about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88). He (a.s.) said: "**What are they saying?**" I said: "They are saying, 'All things would perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face'." He (a.s.) said: "**Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They have said a grievous (thing)! But rather He (a.z.w.j.) Means, all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be Accessed from, and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be Accessed from it.**"' [cite: 1067-1073]
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةً عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنِ الْحَرْثِ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ: كُنَّا عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللهِ تَعَالَى كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ فَقَالَ مَا يَقُولُونَ قُلْتُ يَقُولُونَ هَلَكَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ فَقَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ لَقَدْ قَالُوا عَظِيماً إِنَّمَا عَلَى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ هَالِك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ وَ نَحْنُ وَجْهُهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Baseer, from Al Hars Bin Al Mugheira who said: 'We were in the presence of Abu Abdullah (a.s.), and a man asked him (a.s.) about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**What are they saying?**" I said: "They are saying, 'All things would perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face'." He (a.s.) said: "**Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They have said a grievous (thing)! But rather He (a.z.w.j.) Means, all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be Accessed from, and we (a.s.) are His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be Accessed from it.**"

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Al Hajal, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ibn Mahboub, from Al Ahowl, from Sallam Bin Al Mustaneer who said: 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88). He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.), by Allah (a.z.w.j.), are the Face which He (a.z.w.j.) Said, and he will never perish on the Day of Qiyamah, one who comes to Allah (a.z.w.j.) with what He (a.z.w.j.) had Commanded him with, from being obedient to us (a.s.) and our (a.s.) Wilayah.** That is the Face which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Said: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88). There isn't any one (a.s.) from us (a.s.) passing away except and there is a replacement from Him (a.z.w.j.), up to the Day of Qiyamah." [cite: 1074-1079]
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا الحَجَّالُ عَنْ صَالِحٍ بْنِ السَّنْدِي عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنِ الْأَحْوَلِ عَنْ سَلَامِ بْنِ الْمُسْتَنِيرِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ هَالِك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ قَالَ نَحْنُ وَ اللَّهِ وَجْهُهُ الَّذِي قَالَ وَ لَنْ يَهْلِكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مَنْ أَتَى اللَّهَ بِمَا أَمَرَ بِهِ مِنْ طَاعَتِنَا وَ مُوَالَاتِنَا ذَاكَ الْوَجْهُ الَّذِي قَالَ اللَّهُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ لَيْسَ مِنَّا مَيْتٌ يَمُوتُ إِلَّا خَلَّفَ مِنْهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Al Hajal, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ibn Mahboub, from Al Ahowl, from Sallam Bin Al Mustaneer who said: 'I asked Abu Ja'far (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.): "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**We (a.s.), by Allah (a.z.w.j.), are the Face which He (a.z.w.j.) Said, and he will never perish on the Day of Qiyamah, one who comes to Allah (a.z.w.j.) with what He (a.z.w.j.) had Commanded him with, from being obedient to us (a.s.) and our (a.s.) Wilayah.** That is the Face which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Said: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88). There isn't any one (a.s.) from us (a.s.) passing away except and there is a replacement from Him (a.z.w.j.), up to the Day of Qiyamah."

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Al Hajal, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ibn Umeyr, from Mansour, from a companion of Abu Hamza, from Abu Hamza who said: 'I said to Abu Ja'far (a.s.), "May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Make me to be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! Inform me about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88)." He (a.s.) said: "**O so and so! All things will perish and the Face would remain, Allah (a.z.w.j.) is more Magnificent from being described, but its meaning is all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Religion. We (a.s.) are the Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it. We (a.s.) will not cease to be among servants of Allah (a.z.w.j.) for as long as for Allah (a.z.w.j.) there is a 'Rawiya' (Need) for them.**"' [cite: 1080-1098]
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ جَلِيسٍ لِأَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع جَعَلَنِي اللَّهُ فِدَاكَ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ قَالَ يَا فُلَانُ فَيَهْلِكُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَ يَبْقَى الْوَجْهُ اللَّهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ أَنْ يُوصَفَ وَ لَكِنَّ مَعْنَاهَا كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا دِينَهُ نَحْنُ الْوَجْهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ لَمْ نَزَلْ فِي عِبَادِ اللَّهِ مَا دَامَ لِلَّهِ فِيهِمْ رَوِيَّةٌ قُلْتُ وَ مَا الرُّويَّةُ جَعَلَنِي اللهُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ حَاجَةٌ فَإِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ فِيهِمْ حَاجَةٌ رَفَعَنَا إِلَيْهِ فَيَصْنَعُ بِنَا مَا أَحَبَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Al Hajal, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ibn Umeyr, from Mansour, from a companion of Abu Hamza, from Abu Hamza who said: 'I said to Abu Ja'far (a.s.), "May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Make me to be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! Inform me about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88)."
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**O so and so! All things will perish and the Face would remain, Allah (a.z.w.j.) is more Magnificent from being described, but its meaning is all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Religion. We (a.s.) are the Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it. We (a.s.) will not cease to be among servants of Allah (a.z.w.j.) for as long as for Allah (a.z.w.j.) there is a 'Rawiya' (Need) for them. When there does not happen to be any need for Him (a.z.w.j.) regarding them, He (a.z.w.j.) will Raise us (a.s.) to Him (a.z.w.j.) and Do with us (a.s.) what He (a.z.w.j.) Loves.**"

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ali Bin Hadeed, from Ali Bin Abu Al Mugheira, from Abu Sallam Al Nahhas, from Sowrat Bin Kuleyb who said: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying: "**We (a.s.) are the double (Masaany) which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Gave His Prophet (s.a.w.a.) and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the earth. We (a.s.) turn around between your midst. He recognises us (a.s.), one who recognises us (a.s.), and he is ignorant of us (a.s.) one who is ignorant of us (a.s.). So, the one who is ignorant of us (a.s.), in front of him is the certainty (of destruction).**"' [cite: 1099-1103]
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَدِيدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ أَبِي سَلَامِ النَّحَاسِ عَنْ سَوْرَةَ بْنِ كُلَيْبٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ نَحْنُ الْمَثَانِي الَّذِي أَعْطَاهُ اللهُ نَبِيَّنَا ص و نَحْنُ وَجْهُ اللَّهِ فِي الْأَرْضِ نَتَقَلَّبُ بَيْنَ أَظْهُرِكُمْ عَرَفَنَا مَنْ عَرَفَنَا وَ جَهِلَنَا مَنْ جَهِلَنَا فَمَنْ جَهِلَنَا فَأَمَامَهُ الْيَقِينُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ali Bin Hadeed, from Ali Bin Abu Al Mugheira, from Abu Sallam Al Nahhas, from Sowrat Bin Kuleyb who said: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "**We (a.s.) are the double (Masaany) which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Gave His Prophet (s.a.w.a.) and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the earth. We (a.s.) turn around between your midst. He recognises us (a.s.), one who recognises us (a.s.), and he is ignorant of us (a.s.) one who is ignorant of us (a.s.). So, the one who is ignorant of us (a.s.), in front of him is the certainty (of destruction).**"

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'I said to him (a.s.), "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! Inform me about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88)." He (a.s.) said: "**O so and so! All things to perish and the Face remains, Allah (a.z.w.j.) is Greater than to be described, but its meaning is, all thing will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Religion, and we (a.s.) are the Face which Allah (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it.**"' [cite: 1104-1117]
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ قَالَ يَا فُلَانُ يَهْلِكُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ وَ يَبْقَى الْوَجْهُ اللَّهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ أَنْ يُوصَف وَ لَكِنْ مَعْنَاهَا كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا دِينَهُ وَ نَحْنُ الْوَجْهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى اللَّهُ مِنْهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'I said to him (a.s.), "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! Inform me about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: 'All things will perish except for His Face' (28:88)."
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**O so and so! All things to perish and the Face remains, Allah (a.z.w.j.) is Greater than to be described, but its meaning is, all thing will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Religion, and we (a.s.) are the Face which Allah (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it.**"

chapter: Chapter 4: Regarding the Imams (a.s.) from Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) that they are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) mentioned in the Book
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein, from Fazalat, from Al Batainy, from Ibn Ameyra, from Abu Baseer, from Al Haris Bin Al Mugheira who said: 'We were in the presence of Abu Abdullah (a.s.), a man asked him (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88). He (a.s.) said: "**What are they (people) saying?**" I said: "They are saying, 'all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face'." He (a.s.) said: "**Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They have said a grievous (thing). But rather it means all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it, and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it.**"' [cite: 1118-1124]
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ عَنِ الْبَطَائِنِي عَنِ ابْنِ عَمِيرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ: كُنَّا عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هَالِك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ فَقَالَ مَا يَقُولُونَ قُلْتُ يَقُولُونَ هَلَكَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ فَقَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ لَقَدْ قَالُوا عَظِيمًا إِنَّمَا عَلَى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ هَالِك إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ وَ نَحْنُ وَجْهُهُ الَّذِي يُؤْتَى مِنْهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein, from Fazalat, from Al Batainy, from Ibn Ameyra, from Abu Baseer, from Al Haris Bin Al Mugheira who said: 'We were in the presence of Abu Abdullah (a.s.), a man asked him (a.s.) about Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: "All things will perish except for His Face" (28:88).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**What are they (people) saying?**" I said: "They are saying, 'all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face'." He (a.s.) said: "**Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They have said a grievous (thing). But rather it means all things will perish except His (a.z.w.j.) Face which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it, and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.) which He (a.z.w.j.) can be accessed from it.**"

chapter: Chapter 5: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and they are the double (Masaany) which the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) was given
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Musa Bin Sa'adan, from Abdullah Bin Al Qasim, from Haroun Bin Kharja who said: 'Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) said to me: "**We (a.s.) are the 'Masaany' which Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was Given, and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.). We (a.s.) turn around between your midst, so the one who recognises us (a.s.), and one who does not recognise us (a.s.), in front of him is the certainty (of his destruction).**"' [cite: 1137-1140]
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ سَعْدَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ خَارِجَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ع نَحْنُ الْمَثَانِي الَّتِي أُوتِيَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ نَحْنُ وَجْهُ اللهِ نَتَقَلَّبُ بَيْنَ أَظْهُرِكُمْ فَمَنْ عَرَفْنَا وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَعْرِفْنَا فَأَمَامَهُ الْيَقِينُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Musa Bin Sa'adan, from Abdullah Bin Al Qasim, from Haroun Bin Kharja who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) said to me: "**We (a.s.) are the 'Masaany' which Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was Given, and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.). We (a.s.) turn around between your midst, so the one who recognises us (a.s.), and one who does not recognise us (a.s.), in front of him is the certainty (of his destruction).**"'

chapter: Chapter 5: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and they are the double (Masaany) which the Prophet (s.a.w.a.) was given
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Al Hassan, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ibn Sinan, from Abu Sallam, from one of his companions, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: "**We (a.s.) are the 'Masaany' which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Gave our Prophet (s.a.w.a.), and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.). We (a.s.) turn around in the earth between your midst.**"' [cite: 1141-1143]
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي سَلَّامٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: نَحْنُ الْمَثَانِي الَّتِي أَعْطَى اللَّهُ نَبِيَّنَا وَ نَحْنُ وَجْهُ اللَّهِ نَتَقَلَّبُ فِي الْأَرْضِ بَيْنَ أَظْهُرِكُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Al Hassan, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Ibn Sinan, from Abu Sallam, from one of his companions, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: "**We (a.s.) are the 'Masaany' which Allah (a.z.w.j.) Gave our Prophet (s.a.w.a.), and we (a.s.) are the Face of Allah (a.z.w.j.). We (a.s.) turn around in the earth between your midst.**"

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Ibn Bazie, and Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! In the sky there are seventy types of Angels**. If the people of the earth, all of them were to be united upon them to count on type of them, they would not be able to count them, and **they are making it a Religion with our Wilayah (a.s.)**.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنِ ابْنِ بَرِيعٍ وَ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الصَّبَاحِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ فِي السَّمَاءِ لَسَبْعِينَ صِنْفاً مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ لَوِ اجْتَمَعَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَهْلُ الْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ يُحْصُونَ عَدَدَ صِنْفٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا أَحْصَوْهُمْ وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَدِينُونَ بِوَلَا يَتِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Ibn Bazie, and Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! In the sky there are seventy types of Angels**. If the people of the earth, all of them were to be united upon them to count on type of them, they would not be able to count them, and **they are making it a Religion with our Wilayah (a.s.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. And it is reported by Ali Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) - similar to it.
arabicText: 2 وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الصَّبَاحِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع بِمِثْلِ ذَلِكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. And it is reported by Ali Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.)
thaqalaynMatn: - similar to it.

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Isa, from his brother, from Abdul Rahman Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr Al Sayrafi, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**This matter of yours (Wilayah) was Presented to the Angels, but none acknowledged with it except the ones of Proximity.**'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَخِيهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي الْبِلَادِ عَنْ سَدِيرِ الصَّيْرَي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ أَمْرَكُمْ هَذَا عُرِضَ عَلَى الْمَلَائِكَةِ فَلَمْ يُقِرَّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْمُعْرَبُونَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Isa, from his brother, from Abdul Rahman Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr Al Sayrafi, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**This matter of yours (Wilayah) was Presented to the Angels, but none acknowledged with it except the ones of Proximity.**'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Muhammad bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah Al Kinany, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! In the sky there are seventy types of Angels**. If the people of the earth were to gather upon counting the number of types of them, they would not be able to count them, and **they are making it a Religion with our Wilayah (a.s.)**.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَالٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الصَّبَاحِ الْكِتَابِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: قَالَ وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ فِي السَّمَاءِ لَسَبْعِينَ صِنفاً مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ لَوِ اجْتَمَعَ أَهْلُ الْأَرْضِ أَنْ يَعُدُّوا عَدَدَ صِنْفٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا عَدُّوهُمْ وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَدِينُونَ بِوَلَا يَتِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Fazzal, from Muhammad bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Al Sabbah Al Kinany, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! In the sky there are seventy types of Angels**. If the people of the earth were to gather upon counting the number of types of them, they would not be able to count them, and **they are making it a Religion with our Wilayah (a.s.)**.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**This matter of yours (Wilayah) was Presented to the Angels, but none acknowledged with it except the ones of Proximity**, and Presented to the Prophets, but none acknowledge with it except the Messengers, and Presented to the Momineen, but none acknowledged with it except the Examined ones.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي الْبِلَادِ عَنْ سَدِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ أَمْرَكُمْ هَذَا عُرِضَ عَلَى الْمَلَائِكَةِ فَلَمْ يُقِرَّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْمُقَرَّبُونَ و عُرِضَ عَلَى الْأَنْبِيَاء فَلَمْ يُقِرَّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْمُرْسَلُونَ وَ عُرِضَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَلَمْ يُقِرَّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْمُمْتَحَنُونَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**This matter of yours (Wilayah) was Presented to the Angels, but none acknowledged with it except the ones of Proximity**, and Presented to the Prophets, but none acknowledge with it except the Messengers, and Presented to the Momineen, but none acknowledged with it except the Examined ones.'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Al Haysam, from his father, from Al Sumali, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: '**O Abu Hamza! Do you see that the ones Chosen for our matter (Wilayah), from the Angels were the ones of Proximity, and from the Prophets (a.s.) were the Messengers (a.s.), and from the Momineen were the Examined ones?**'
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْهَيْثَمِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: قَالَ لِي يَا أَبَا حَمْزَةَ أَلَا تَرَى أَنَّهُ اخْتَارَ لِأَمْرِنَا مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ وَ مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ وَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الْمُمْتَحَنِينَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Al Haysam, from his father, from Al Sumali, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said to me: '**O Abu Hamza! Do you see that the ones Chosen for our matter (Wilayah), from the Angels were the ones of Proximity, and from the Prophets (a.s.) were the Messengers (a.s.), and from the Momineen were the Examined ones?**'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Musa, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, a slave of Harb, from Abu Ja'far Al Hammamy Al Kufi, from Al Azhar Al Biteekhy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Presented the Wilayah of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.). The Angels accepted it and an Angel called Futrus refused it, so Allah (a.z.w.j.) Broke his wings.** When Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) was born, Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Jibraeel among seventy thousand Angels to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to congratulate him of his (a.s.) birth. (On his way) he passed by Futrus. Futrus said to him, "O Jibraeel! Where are you going?" He said: "Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Sent me to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to congratulate him of the birth of a son (a.s.) during this night." Futrus said to him, "Carry me with you and ask Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to supplicate for me." Jibraeel said to him: "Ride on my wing." He rode his wing and came to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.). He entered to see him and congratulated him and said to him: "O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! Futrus, there is brotherhood between me and him, and he asked me to ask you to supplicate to Allah (a.z.w.j.) for him, for Him to return his wings to him." Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Futrus: "Will you do it?" He said, "Yes." So **Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) presented to him the Wilayah of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and he accepted it.** Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "Your concern is with the cradle (of Al-Hussein (a.s.)). Wipe with it and wallow in it." Futrus went to the cradle of Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) and Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) supplicated for him. Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "I looked at his feather and it emerged and the blood flowed from it, and it prolonged until it adhered to his other wing, and he ascended along with Jibraeel to the sky and came to his place."'
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ مَوْلَى حَرْبٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ الْحَمَّامِي الْكُوفِ عَنِ الْأَزْهَرِ الْبِطِّيخِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَرَضَ وَلَا يَةَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَقَبِلَهَا الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَ أَبَاهَا مَلَكٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ فُطْرُسُ فَكَسَرَ اللَّهُ جَنَاحَهُ فَلَمَّا وُلِدَ الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ ع بَعَثَ اللَّهُ جَبْرَئِيلَ فِي سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ ملك إلى مُحَمَّدٍ ص يُهَنتُهُمْ بِوَلَادَتِهِ فَمَرَّ بِقُطْرُسَ فَقَالَ لَهُ فُطْرُسُ يَا جِبْرَئِيلُ إِلَى أَيْنَ تَذْهَبُ قَالَ بَعَثَنِي اللهُ إِلَى مُحَمَّدٍ ص أَهَنَّتُهُمْ بِمَوْلُودٍ وُلِدَ فِي هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ فقَالَ لَهُ مُطْرُسُ احْمِلْنِي مَعَكَ وَ سَل مُحَمَّداً يَدْعُو لي فقالَ لَهُ جبرئيل اركب جَناحِي فَرَكبَ جَناحَهُ فَأَتَى مُحَمَّداً فدخل عَلَيْهِ وَ هَنَّاهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ فُطْرُسَ بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَهُ أَخَوَةٌ وَ سَأَلَنِي أَنْ أَسْأَلَكَ أَنْ تَدْعُوَ اللَّهَ لَهُ أَنْ يَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ جَنَاحَهُ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص لِفُطْرُسَ أَتَفْعَلُ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَعَرَضَ عَلَيْهِ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص وَلَايَةَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَقَبِلَهَا فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص شَأْنَكَ بِالْمَهْدِ فَتَمَسَّحْ بِهِ وَ تَمَرَّغْ فِيهِ قَالَ فَمَضَى فُطْرُسُ إِلَى مَهْدِ الحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيَّ عَ وَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص يَدْعُو لَهُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص فَنَظَرْتُ إِلَى رِيشِهِ وَ إِنَّهُ لَيَطْلُعُ وَ يَجْرِي مِنْهُ الدَّمُ وَ يَطُولُ حَتَّى لحق بجناحه الآخرِ وَ عُرجَ مَعَ جَبْرَئِيلَ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ وَ صَارَ إِلَى مَوْضِعِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Musa, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, a slave of Harb, from Abu Ja'far Al Hammamy Al Kufi, from Al Azhar Al Biteekhy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Presented the Wilayah of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.). The Angels accepted it and an Angel called Futrus refused it, so Allah (a.z.w.j.) Broke his wings.** When Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) was born, Allah (a.z.w.j.) Sent Jibraeel among seventy thousand Angels to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to congratulate him of his (a.s.) birth. (On his way) he passed by Futrus. Futrus said to him, "O Jibraeel! Where are you going?" He said: "Allah (a.z.w.j.) has Sent me to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to congratulate him of the birth of a son (a.s.) during this night." Futrus said to him, "Carry me with you and ask Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) to supplicate for me." Jibraeel said to him: "Ride on my wing." He rode his wing and came to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.). He entered to see him and congratulated him and said to him: "O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! Futrus, there is brotherhood between me and him, and he asked me to ask you to supplicate to Allah (a.z.w.j.) for him, for Him to return his wings to him." Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Futrus: "Will you do it?" He said, "Yes." So **Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) presented to him the Wilayah of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), and he accepted it.** Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "Your concern is with the cradle (of Al-Hussein (a.s.)). Wipe with it and wallow in it." Futrus went to the cradle of Al-Hussein (a.s.) Bin Ali (a.s.) and Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) supplicated for him. Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "I looked at his feather and it emerged and the blood flowed from it, and it prolonged until it adhered to his other wing, and he ascended along with Jibraeel to the sky and came to his place."'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Umar, from Umar Bin Abdul Aziz, from Al Khaybari, from Ibn Zabyan, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: 'We heard him saying: "**The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted did not attain the Proximity from Him (a.z.w.j.) except by that which you (Shias) are upon**, and that the Angels are describing what you are describing, and are seeking what you are seeking, and that from the Angels there are Angels saying, '**Our word regarding the Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is that which you (Shias) are made to be upon**.'"'
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عُمَرَ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ عَنِ الْخَيْبَرِيِّ عَنِ ابْنِ طَبْيَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْنَا يَقُولُ مَا حَاوَرَتْ مَلَائِكَةُ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فِي دُنُوهَا مِنْهُ إِلَّا بِالَّذِي أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَيَصِفُونَ مَا تَصِفُونَ وَ يَطْلُبُونَ مَا تَطْلُبُونَ وَ إِنَّ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مَلَائِكَةً يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ قَوْلَنَا فِي آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ الَّذِي جَعَلْتَهُمْ عَلَيْهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Umar, from Umar Bin Abdul Aziz, from Al Khaybari, from Ibn Zabyan, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'We heard him (a.s.) saying: "**The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted did not attain the Proximity from Him (a.z.w.j.) except by that which you (Shias) are upon**, and that the Angels are describing what you are describing, and are seeking what you are seeking, and that from the Angels there are Angels saying, '**Our word regarding the Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is that which you (Shias) are made to be upon**.'"'

chapter: Chapter 6: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With and Wilayah of the Angels
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Muhammad, from Al Qasim Bin Muhammad, from Suleyman Bin Dawood, from Hammad Bin Isa who said: 'A man asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying, "Are the Angels more numerous or the children of Adam (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: "**By the One (a.z.w.j.) in Whose Hand is my soul! The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the skies are more numerous than the number of (grains of) dust**, and there is no place for a foot in the sky except and in it is an Angel extolling Holiness to Him (a.z.w.j.), and Glorifying. And in the earth, there is neither any tree, nor like a stick, except and in it is an Angel allocated with it, coming to Allah (a.z.w.j.) every day with its work, and Allah (a.z.w.j.) is more Knowing with it, and **there is no one from them except and he draws nearer to Allah (a.z.w.j.) during every day by our Wilayah (a.s.), People of the Household, and he seeks Forgiveness for ones who love us (a.s.), and curses our enemies**, and asks Allah (a.z.w.j.) to Send upon them the Punishments with a (constant) Sending."'
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ دَاوُدَ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى قَالَ : سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ ع فَقَالَ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَكْثَرُ أَوْ بَنُو آدم فَقَالَ وَ الَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ لَمَلَائِكَةُ اللهِ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ عَدَدِ التَّرَابِ وَ مَا فِي السَّمَاءِ مَوْضِعُ قَدَمٍ إِلَّا وَ فِيهِ مَلَكُ يُقدِّسُ لَهُ وَ يُسَبِّحُ و لَا فِي الْأَرْضِ شَجَرَةٌ وَ لَا مِثْلُ غَرْزَةٍ إِلَّا وَ فِيهَا مَلَكٌ مُؤَكَّلٌ بِمَا يَأْتِي اللَّهَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ بِعَمَلِهَا وَ اللهُ أَعْلَمُ بها وَ مَا مِنْهُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا وَ يَتَقَرَّبُ إِلَى اللَّهِ فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ بِوَلَا يَتِنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لِمُحَبِّينَا وَ يَلْعَنُ أَعْدَاءَنَا وَ يَسْأَلُ اللهَ أَنْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ إِرْسَالًا.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Muhammad, from Al Qasim Bin Muhammad, from Suleyman Bin Dawood, from Hammad Bin Isa who said: 'A man asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying, "Are the Angels more numerous or the children of Adam (a.s.)?"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**By the One (a.z.w.j.) in Whose Hand is my soul! The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the skies are more numerous than the number of (grains of) dust**, and there is no place for a foot in the sky except and in it is an Angel extolling Holiness to Him (a.z.w.j.), and Glorifying. And in the earth, there is neither any tree, nor like a stick, except and in it is an Angel allocated with it, coming to Allah (a.z.w.j.) every day with its work, and Allah (a.z.w.j.) is more Knowing with it, and **there is no one from them except and he draws nearer to Allah (a.z.w.j.) during every day by our Wilayah (a.s.), People of the Household, and he seeks Forgiveness for ones who love us (a.s.), and curses our enemies**, and asks Allah (a.z.w.j.) to Send upon them the Punishments with a (constant) Sending."

chapter: Rare from Chapter 6
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. Ibrahim Bin Hashim narrated, from Al Husayn Bin Sayf, from his father, from Abu Al Samit, regarding the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "And He Subdued for you whatever is in the skies and whatever is in the earth altogether from it" (45:13). He said: '**Compelled them to be in their (a.s.) (Imams') obedience.**'
arabicText: 1- إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي الصَّامِتِ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ وَ سَخَّرَ لَكُمْ ما في السَّماواتِ وَ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً مِنْهُ قَالَ أَخْبَرَهُمْ بِطَاعَتِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. Ibrahim Bin Hashim narrated, from Al Husayn Bin Sayf, from his father, from Abu Al Samit, regarding the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "And He Subdued for you whatever is in the skies and whatever is in the earth altogether from it" (45:13).
thaqalaynMatn: He said: '**Compelled them to be in their (a.s.) (Imams') obedience.**'

chapter: Rare from Chapter 6
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Sayyari narrated, from Ubeydullah Bin Abu Abdullah Al Farsi and others raising it to Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**Al-Karoubeen are a group of our Shias from the first creation. Allah (a.z.w.j.) Made them to be behind the Throne.** If the Noor (light) of one of them were to be apportioned upon the inhabitants of the earth, it would be sufficient for them.' Then he (a.s.) said: 'When Musa (a.s.) asked his Lord (a.z.w.j.) what he asked, **He (a.z.w.j.) Commanded one of the Karoubeen to flash to the mountain, and made it to be rubble.**'
arabicText: 2- أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ السَّيَّارِي عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْفَارِسِيِّ وَغَيْرِهِ رَفَعُوهُ إلى أبي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْكَرُوبِيِّينَ قَوْمٌ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا مِنَ الْخَلْقِ الْأَوَّلِ جَعَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ خَلْفَ الْعَرْشِ لَوْ قُسِمَ نُورُ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ لَكَفَاهُمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ مُوسَى عَ لَمَّا أَنْ سَأَلَ رَبَّهُ مَا سَأَلَ أَمَرَ وَاحِداً مِنَ الْكَرُوبيِّينَ فَتَحَلَّى لِلْجَبَلِ فَجَعَلَهُ دَكَّاً.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Sayyari narrated, from Ubeydullah Bin Abu Abdullah Al Farsi and others raising it to Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Al-Karoubeen are a group of our Shias from the first creation. Allah (a.z.w.j.) Made them to be behind the Throne.** If the Noor (light) of one of them were to be apportioned upon the inhabitants of the earth, it would be sufficient for them.' Then he (a.s.) said: 'When Musa (a.s.) asked his Lord (a.z.w.j.) what he asked, **He (a.z.w.j.) Commanded one of the Karoubeen to flash to the mountain, and made it to be rubble.**'

chapter: Chapter 7: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With, From the Wilayah of the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam) Prophets for Them (a.s.) Regarding the Covenant and Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to me by Abu Ja'far Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "And We had Covenanted to Adam before, but he forgot, and We did not find for him having determination" (20:115). He (a.s.) said: "**Covenanted to him regarding Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (a.s.) from after him, but he (a.s.) neglected and there did not happen to be any determination for him that they (a.s.) are like that.** And rather the determined ones (Ul Al-Azam) have been named as the determined ones because **He (a.z.w.j.) Covenanted to them regarding Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the successors (a.s.) from after him, and the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.) and his ways, and He (a.z.w.j.) United their determination that, that is like that, and the acknowledgment with it.**"
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مُفَضَّلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ وَ لَقَدْ عَهِدْنَا إِلَى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَنَسِي وَ لَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً قَالَ عَهِدَ إِلَيْهِ فِي مُحَمَّدٍ وَ الْأَئِمَّةِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ فَتَرِكَ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ عَزْمٌ فِيهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ هَكَذَا و إِنما سمي أُولُو الْعَزمِ أُولِي الْعَزمِ لِأَنَّهُ عَهِدَ إِلَيْهِمْ فِي مُحَمَّدٍ وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ الْمَهْدِي وَسِيرَتِهِ فَأَجْمَعَ عَزْمُهُمْ أَنْ ذَلِكَ كَذَلِكَ وَ الْإِفْرَارَ بِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to me by Abu Ja'far Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "And We had Covenanted to Adam before, but he forgot, and We did not find for him having determination" (20:115).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**Covenanted to him regarding Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the Imams (a.s.) from after him, but he (a.s.) neglected and there did not happen to be any determination for him that they (a.s.) are like that.** And rather the determined ones (Ul Al-Azam) have been named as the determined ones because **He (a.z.w.j.) Covenanted to them regarding Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) and the successors (a.s.) from after him, and the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.) and his ways, and He (a.z.w.j.) United their determination that, that is like that, and the acknowledgment with it.**"

chapter: Chapter 7: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With, From the Wilayah of the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam) Prophets for Them (a.s.) Regarding the Covenant and Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to me by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Dawood Al Ijaly, from Zurara, from Humran, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: 'When Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Created what exists, He (a.z.w.j.) Mixed a sweet water and a salty bitter water and Took the clay from the Earth. He then Mixed it with a severe mixing and Said to the companions of the right hand, and they were in that like particles: "**Walk to the Paradise in safety.**" And Said to the companions of the left hand: "**Walk to the Fire and I (a.z.w.j.) don't Care.**" Then Said: "Am I not your Lord?" They said, "Yes, we testify." (Lest you should be saying on the Day of Judgment, "We were oblivious of this" (7:172)). Then **the Covenant was taken from the Prophets (a.s.). He (a.z.w.j.) Said: "Am I not your Lord? And this here is Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) the Rasool of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and this here is Ali (a.s.) Amir Al-Momineen." They said: "Yes."** He (a.z.w.j.) Affirmed the Prophet-hood for them, and Took the Covenant on the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam): "**I (a.z.w.j.) am your Lord, and Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is My Rasool, and Ali (a.s.) is Amir Al-Momineen and the successors (a.s.) after him are the Masters of My Command (Wali Al-Amr), and the Reservoirs of My Knowledge, and by the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.) My religion will be victorious, and My government will be manifested, and revenge will be taken from My enemies, and I will be worshipped willingly or unwillingly.**" They said: "We testify and accept, O Lord!" Adam (a.s.) neither rejected it nor accepted it or showed any determination for those five (a.s.) regarding the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.), and there was no determination on accepting him (a.s.). (And We had Covenanted to Adam before, but he forgot, and We did not find for him having determination (20:115)). Then He (a.z.w.j.) Ordered the Fire to be inflamed. He (a.z.w.j.) Said to the companions of the left hand: "Enter it." They did not do it. And Said to the companions of the right hand: "Enter it." They entered it, and it was for them cool and safe. **Thus it was confirmed, the obedience, and the disobedience, and the Wilayah.**'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ دَاوُدَ الْعِجْلِيِّ عَنْ زُرَارَةً عَنْ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى حَيْثُ خَلَقَ الْخَلْقَ خلَقَ مَاءً عَذْباً وَ مَاءً مَالِحاً أُجَاجاً فَامْتَرَجَ الْمَاءَانِ فَأَخَذَ طِيناً مِنْ أَديمِ الْأَرْضِ فَعَرَكَهُ عَرَّكَاً شَدِيداً فَقَالَ لِأَصْحَابِ الْيَمِينِ وَ هُمْ كَالذَّرْ يَدِبُّونَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ بِسَلَامٍ وَ قَالَ لِأَصْحَابِ الشِّمَالِ يَدِبُّونَ إِلَى النَّارِ وَ لَا أُبَالِي ثُمَّ قَالَ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قَالُوا بَلَى شَهِدْنا أَنْ تَقُولُوا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هَذَا غَافِلِينَ قالَ ثُمَّ أَخَذَ الْمِيثَاقَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّين فقالَ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ أَنَّ هَذَا مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَ أَنَّ هَذَا عَلِيٌّ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ قَالُوا بَلَى فَثَبَتَتْ لَهُمُ النُّبُوَّهُ وَ أَخَذَ الْمِيثَاقَ عَلَى أُولِي الْعَزمِ أَنِّي رَبَّكُمْ وَ مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللهِ وَ عَلِيٌّ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ أَوْصِيَاؤُهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَلَاهُ أَمْرِي وَ خَزَانُ عِلْمِي وَ أَنَّ الْمَهْدِي أَنْتَصِرُ بِهِ لِدِينِي وَ أَظْهِرُ بِهِ دَوْلَتِي وَ أَنْتَقِمُ بِهِ مِنْ أَعْدَائِي وَ أَعْبَدُ بِهِ طَوْعاً وَ كَرْهَا قَالُوا أَقْرَرْنَا وَ شَهِدْنَا يَا رَبِّ و لَمْ يَجْحَدُ آدَمُ وَ لَمْ يُقِرَّ فَثَبَتَتِ الْعَزِيمَةُ لهؤلاء الخمْسَةِ فِي الْمَهْدِي وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لِآدَمَ عَزْمُ عَلَى الْإِقْرَارِ بِهِ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ حَلَ وَ لَقَدْ عَهِدْنَا إِلَى آدَمَ مِنْ قَبْلُ فَنَسِيَ وَ لَمْ تَجِدْ لَهُ عَزْماً قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَعْنِي فَتَرَكَ ثم أمر ناراً فَتَأَجَّحَتْ فَقَالَ لِأَصْحَابِ الشِّمَالِ ادْخُلُوهَا فَهَابُوهَا وَ قَالَ لِأَصْحَابِ الْيَمِينِ ادْخُلُوهَا فَدَخَلُوهَا فَكَانَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ بَرْداً وَ سَلَامًا فَقَالَ أَصْحَابُ الشِّمَالِ يَا رَبِّ أَقِلْنَا فَقَالَ قَدْ أَقَلْتُكُمُ اذْهَبُوا فَادْخُلُوهَا فَهَابُوهَا فَثَمَّ ثَبَتَتِ الطَّاعَةُ وَ الْمَعْصِيَةُ وَ الْوَلَايَةُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to me by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Dawood Al ljaly, from Zurara, from Humran, from Abu Ja'farasws having said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'When Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted Created what exists, He (a.z.w.j.) Mixed a sweet water and a salty bitter water and Took the clay from the Earth. He then Mixed it with a severe mixing and Said to the companions of the right hand, and they were in that like particles: "**Walk to the Paradise in safety.**" And Said to the companions of the left hand: "**Walk to the Fire and I (a.z.w.j.) don't Care.**" Then Said: "Am I not your Lord?" They said, "Yes, we testify." (Lest you should be saying on the Day of Judgment, "We were oblivious of this" (7:172)). Then **the Covenant was taken from the Prophets (a.s.). He (a.z.w.j.) Said: "Am I not your Lord? And this here is Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) the Rasool of Allah (a.z.w.j.), and this here is Ali (a.s.) Amir Al-Momineen." They said: "Yes."** He (a.z.w.j.) Affirmed the Prophet-hood for them, and Took the Covenant on the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam): "**I (a.z.w.j.) am your Lord, and Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) is My Rasool, and Ali (a.s.) is Amir Al-Momineen and the successors (a.s.) after him are the Masters of My Command (Wali Al-Amr), and the Reservoirs of My Knowledge, and by the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.) My religion will be victorious, and My government will be manifested, and revenge will be taken from My enemies, and I will be worshipped willingly or unwillingly.**" They said: "We testify and accept, O Lord!" Adam (a.s.) neither rejected it nor accepted it or showed any determination for those five (a.s.) regarding the Mahdi (a.t.f.s.), and there was no determination on accepting him (a.s.). (And We had Covenanted to Adam before, but he forgot, and We did not find for him having determination (20:115)). Then He (a.z.w.j.) Ordered the Fire to be inflamed. He (a.z.w.j.) Said to the companions of the left hand: "Enter it." They did not do it. And Said to the companions of the right hand: "Enter it." They entered it, and it was for them cool and safe. **Thus it was confirmed, the obedience, and the disobedience, and the Wilayah.**'

chapter: Chapter 7: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With, From the Wilayah of the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam) Prophets for Them (a.s.) Regarding the Covenant and Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. And it is reported as well from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Hisham Bin Salim, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) similar to it.
arabicText: 3- وَ رَوَاهُ أَيْضاً عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَ مِثْلَهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. And it is reported as well from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Hisham Bin Salim, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.)
thaqalaynMatn: similar to it.

chapter: Chapter 7: What Allah (a.z.w.j.) Has Specialised the Imams (a.s.) From Progeny (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) With, From the Wilayah of the Determined Ones (Ul Al-Azam) Prophets for Them (a.s.) Regarding the Covenant and Others
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4.
