arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَعْمَرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى هذا نَذِيرٌ مِنَ النُّذْرِ الْأُولى يَعْنِي مُحَمَّداً ص حَيْثُ دَعَاهُمْ بِالْإِقْرَارِ بِاللَّهِ فِي الدَّرِّ الْأَوَّلِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by one of our companions, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ali Bin Asbat, from Ali Bin Ma'mar, from his father who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I asked Abu Abdullah (a.s.) about the Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted: "This one is a Warner from the former Warners" (53:56). He (a.s.) said: "**Meaning by it Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), where he (s.a.w.a.) called them for the acknowledgment with Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the first (world of the) particles.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ali Bin Hashim, from Muhammad Bin Ubeydullah Bin Abu Rafie, from his father, from his grandfather who said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "**My (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled in the clay and I (s.a.w.a.) was Taught their names just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31], and I (s.a.w.a.) saw the bearers of the flags. Every time they passed by you, O Ali (a.s.), and your (a.s.) Shias, I (s.a.w.a.) sought Forgiveness for you all (Shias).**"
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي رَافِعٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مُثْلَ أُمَّتِي فِي الطين و عُلِّمْتُ الْأَسْمَاءَ كَمَا عَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاء كُلَّها وَ رَأَيْتُ أَصْحَابَ الرَّايَاتِ فَكُلَّمَا مَرَرْتُ بِكَ يَا عَلِيُّ وَ بِشِيعَتِكَ اسْتَغْفَرْتُ لَكُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ali Bin Hashim, from Muhammad Bin Ubeydullah Bin Abu Rafie, from his father, from his grandfather who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "**My (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled in the clay and I (s.a.w.a.) was Taught their names just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31], and I (s.a.w.a.) saw the bearers of the flags. Every time they passed by you, O Ali (a.s.), and your (a.s.) Shias, I (s.a.w.a.) sought Forgiveness for you all (Shias).**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Mugatil Bin Muqatil, from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) who said: 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: "**Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), his (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled for him (s.a.w.a.) in the clay. He (s.a.w.a.) was introduced to them with their names and names of their fathers and their manners, and their appearances.**" We said to him (a.s.): "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! The entirety of the community, from its beginning to its end?" He (a.s.) said: "**Like that, Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said.**"
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنْ مُقَاتِلِ بْنِ مُقَاتِلٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَاعِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ص مُثْلَتْ لَهُ أُمَّتُهُ في الطِّينِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ وَ أَسْمَاءِ آبَائِهِمْ وَ أَخْلَاقِهِمْ وَ حَلَّاهُمْ قَالَ قُلْنَا لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ جَمِيعُ الْأُمَّةِ مِنْ أَوَّلها إلى آخِرِهَا قَالَ هَكَذَا قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Mugatil Bin Muqatil, from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said: "**Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), his (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled for him (s.a.w.a.) in the clay. He (s.a.w.a.) was introduced to them with their names and names of their fathers and their manners, and their appearances.**" We said to him (a.s.): "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! The entirety of the community, from its beginning to its end?" He (a.s.) said: "**Like that, Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abu Al Jaroud who said: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying: "Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'My (s.a.w.a.) community was presented to me yesterday by this room, its beginning to its end.'" A speaker said: "O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! The people were presented to you (s.a.w.a.), did you (s.a.w.a.) see the ones who have not been created yet?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**It was imaged for me, by the One (a.z.w.j.) in Whose Hand is my soul, until I (s.a.w.a.) recognize them more than one of you recognizes his companion.**"
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْجَارُودِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص عُرِضَتْ عَلَيَّ أُمَّتِي الْبَارِحَةَ لَدَى هَذِهِ الحجْرَةِ أَوَّلها إِلَى آخِرِهَا قَالَ قَالَ قَائِلٌ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَدْ عُرِضَ عَلَيْكَ مَنْ خُلِقَ أَرَأَيْتَ مَنْ لَمْ يُخْلُقُ قَالَ صُورَ لِي وَ الَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ حَتَّى لَأَنَا أَعْرَفُ بِهِمْ مِنْ أَحَدِكُمْ بِصَاحِبِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abu Al Jaroud who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'I heard Abu Ja'far (a.s.) saying: "Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: 'My (s.a.w.a.) community was presented to me yesterday by this room, its beginning to its end.'" A speaker said: "O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! The people were presented to you (s.a.w.a.), did you (s.a.w.a.) see the ones who have not been created yet?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**It was imaged for me, by the One (a.z.w.j.) in Whose Hand is my soul, until I (s.a.w.a.) recognize them more than one of you recognizes his companion.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) who said: "**Surely, Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), his (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled for him (s.a.w.a.) in the clay, and he (s.a.w.a.) was introduced to them with their names and names of their fathers, and their appearances.**" I said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! The entire community from its beginning to its end?" He (a.s.) said: "**That is how Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said, or Ja'far (a.s.).**"
arabicText: 10 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضاع قَالَ: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ص مُثْلَتْ لَهُ أُمَّتُهُ فِي الطِّينِ فَعَرَفَهُمْ بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ وَ أَسْمَاءِ آبَالِهِمْ وَ حُلَاهُمْ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ جَمِيعُ الْأُمَّةِ مِنْ أَوَّلِهَا إِلَى آخِرِهَا قَالَ هَكَذَا قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ أَوْ جَعْفَرٌ ع.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Abbad Bin Suleyman, from Sa'ad Bin Sa'ad, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: "**Surely, Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), his (s.a.w.a.) community was resembled for him (s.a.w.a.) in the clay, and he (s.a.w.a.) was introduced to them with their names and names of their fathers, and their appearances.**" I said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! The entire community from its beginning to its end?" He (a.s.) said: "**That is how Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said, or Ja'far (a.s.).**"

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated to us by Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Ibn Kharbouz, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Ali (a.s.): "**My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled my (s.a.w.a.) community for me in the clay, and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) their names, all of them, just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers of the flags passed by me (s.a.w.a.), and I sought Forgiveness for you, O Ali (a.s.), and for your (a.s.) Shias.** My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Promised me a quality regarding your (a.s.) Shias." Ali (a.s.) said: "And what is it, O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**The Forgiveness for the ones from them who believes and is pious, no one from them would be left out, neither a young nor old, and for them their evil deeds would be replace by the good deeds.**"
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا الْعَبَّاسُ بْنُ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنِ ابْنِ حَرَّبُودُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ : قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص لِعَلِيَّ إِنَّ رَبِّي مَثْلَ لِي أُمَّتِي فِي الطِّينِ وَ عَلَّمَنِي أَسْمَاءَهُمْ كُلَّهَا كَمَا عَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاءَ كُلَّهَا فَمَرَّ بِي أَصْحَابُ الرَّايَاتِ فَاسْتَغْفَرْتُ لَكَ وَ لِشِيعَتِكَ يَا عَلِيُّ إِنَّ رَبِّي وَعَدَنِي فِي شِيعَتِكَ حَصْلَةً قُلْتُ وَ مَا هِيَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ الْمَغْفِرَةٌ لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ وَ اتَّقَى لَا يُغَادِرُ مِنْهُمْ صَغِيرَةً وَ لَا كَبِيرَةً وَ لَهُمْ تُبَدِّلُ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ حَسَنَاتٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated to us by Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Ibn Kharbouz, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Ali (a.s.): "**My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled my (s.a.w.a.) community for me in the clay, and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) their names, all of them, just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers of the flags passed by me (s.a.w.a.), and I sought Forgiveness for you, O Ali (a.s.), and for your (a.s.) Shias.** My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Promised me a quality regarding your (a.s.) Shias." Ali (a.s.) said: "And what is it, O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**The Forgiveness for the ones from them who believes and is pious, no one from them would be left out, neither a young nor old, and for them their evil deeds would be replace by the good deeds.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Sa'dan Bin Muslim, from Salih Bin Sahl, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was asked, "By which thing do you (s.a.w.a.) precede the children of Adam (a.s.)?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**I (s.a.w.a.) am the first one to acknowledge with 'Yes' (when) Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of the Prophets (a.s.): and Made them testify against their own selves: 'Am I not your Lord?' They said, 'Yes, we testify' [7:172], so I (s.a.w.a.) was the first one to answer.**"
arabicText: 12 - حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ سَعْدَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ صَالِحٍ بْنِ سَهْلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: سُئِلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ بِأَيِّ شَيْءٍ سَبَقْتَ وَلَدَ آدَمَ قَالَ أَنَا أَوَّلُ مَنْ أَقرَّ بِبَلَى إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَ أَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قَالُوا بَلَى فَكُنْتُ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَجَابَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Sa'dan Bin Muslim, from Salih Bin Sahl, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was asked, "By which thing do you (s.a.w.a.) precede the children of Adam (a.s.)?" He (s.a.w.a.) said: "**I (s.a.w.a.) am the first one to acknowledge with 'Yes' (when) Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of the Prophets (a.s.): and Made them testify against their own selves: 'Am I not your Lord?' They said, 'Yes, we testify' [7:172], so I (s.a.w.a.) was the first one to answer.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Marouf Bin Kharbouz, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Ali (a.s.): "**Surely my (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled my (s.a.w.a.) community for me (s.a.w.a.) in the clay and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) their names just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers of the flags passed by me (s.a.w.a.) and I sought Forgiveness for you, O Ali (a.s.), and for your (a.s.) Shias.**"
arabicText: 13 - حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مَعْرُوفِ بْنِ حَرَّبُونَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لِعَلِيَّ ع إِنَّ رَبِّي مَثَلَ أُمَّتِي فِي الطِّينِ وَ عَلَّمَنِي أَسْمَاءَهُمْ كَمَا عَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاءَ كُلَّها فَمَرَّ بِي أَصْحَابُ الرَّايَاتِ فَاسْتَغْفَرْتُ لَكَ وَ لِشِيعَتِكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Ja'far, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Hammad Bin Isa, from hareyz, from Marouf Bin Kharbouz, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said to Ali (a.s.): "**Surely my (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled my (s.a.w.a.) community for me (s.a.w.a.) in the clay and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) their names just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers of the flags passed by me (s.a.w.a.) and I sought Forgiveness for you, O Ali (a.s.), and for your (a.s.) Shias.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from one of our companions, from Hanan Bin Sadeyr who said: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "**My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled for me (s.a.w.a.) my (s.a.w.a.) community in the clay and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) the names of my (s.a.w.a.) community, just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers passed by me (s.a.w.a.) and I sought Forgiveness for Ali (a.s.) and his (a.s.) Shias.**"
arabicText: 14- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ حَنَانِ بْنِ سَدِيرٍ عَنْهُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص إِنَّ رَبِّي مَثْلَ بِي أُمَّتِي فِي الطَّينِ وَ عَلَّمَنِي أَسْمَاءَ أُمَّتِي كَمَا عَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاء كُلَّها فَمَرَّ بِي أَصْحَابُ الرَّايَاتِ فَاسْتَغْفَرْتُ لِعَلِيَّ وَ شِيعَتِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from one of our companions, from Hanan Bin Sadeyr who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "**My (s.a.w.a.) Lord (a.z.w.j.) Resembled for me (s.a.w.a.) my (s.a.w.a.) community in the clay and Taught me (s.a.w.a.) the names of my (s.a.w.a.) community, just as He (a.z.w.j.) Taught Adam (a.s.) the names, all of them [2:31]. The bearers passed by me (s.a.w.a.) and I sought Forgiveness for Ali (a.s.) and his (a.s.) Shias.**"'

chapter: Chapter 14: Regarding Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.), He (s.a.w.a.) Recognised What He (s.a.w.a.) Had Seen in the (Realms of) the Shadows and the Particles, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad or someone else, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Hanan, from Sudeyf Al Makky who said: 'I heard Muhammad Bin Ali (a.s.) saying: "Jabir Bin Abdullah narrated to me that he (s.a.w.a.) saw Ali (a.s.) and Al-Hassan (a.s.) and Al-Hussein (a.s.). He (s.a.w.a.) said to them (a.s.): '**O you (a.s.)! You (a.s.) are from the pure ones (a.s.) who have been Taken regarding them (a.s.) the Covenant in the realm of the particles.**'"
arabicText: 15- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ أَوْ غَيْرُهُ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنْ حَنَانٍ عَنْ سُدَيْفِ الْمَكِّيِّ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ ع يَقُولُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللهِ أَنَّهُ نَظَرَ إِلَى عَلِي وَ الْحَسَنِ وَ الْحُسَيْنِ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ يَا هَؤُلَاءِ أَنْتُمْ مِنَ النَّفَرِ الَّذِينَ أُخذ مِيثَاقُكُمْ فِي الذَّرِّ.
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad or someone else, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Hanan, from Sudeyf Al Makky who said: 'I heard Muhammad Bin Ali (a.s.) saying: 'Jabir Bin Abdullah narrated to me that he (s.a.w.a.) saw Ali (a.s.) and Al-Hassan (a.s.) and Al-Hussein (a.s.).
thaqalaynMatn: He (s.a.w.a.) said to them (a.s.): '**O you (a.s.)! You (a.s.) are from the pure ones (a.s.) who have been Taken regarding them (a.s.) the Covenant in the realm of the particles.**'"

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Salih Bin Sahl, 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and he was from his (a.s.) companions. He greeted unto him (a.s.), then said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I love you (a.s.) and befriend you (a.s.).' Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said to him: '**You are not as you say. Woe be unto you! Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented to us (a.s.) the one who loved us (a.s.). By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see your soul to be among the ones presented to us (a.s.). So, where were you?**' The man was silent at that and did not respond to him (a.s.).
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ صَالِحٍ بْنِ سَهْلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَّ رَجُلًا جَاءَ إِلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع وَ هُوَ مَعَ أَصْحَابِهِ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ قالَ أَنَا وَاللَّهِ أُحِبُّكَ وَ أَتَوَلَّاكَ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع مَا أَنْتَ كَمَا قُلْتَ وَيْلَكَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفَيْ عَامٍ ثُمَّ عَرَضَ عَلَيْنَا الْمُحِبَّ لَنَا فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا رَأَيْتُ رُوحَكَ فِيمَنْ عُرِضَ عَلَيْنَا فَأَيْنَ كُنْتَ فَسَكَتَ الرَّجُلُ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ وَ لَمْ يُرَاجِعُهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Salih Bin Sahl, 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and he was from his (a.s.) companions. He greeted unto him (a.s.), then said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I love you (a.s.) and befriend you (a.s.).'
thaqalaynMatn: Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) said to him: '**You are not as you say. Woe be unto you! Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented to us (a.s.) the one who loved us (a.s.). By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see your soul to be among the ones presented to us (a.s.). So, where were you?**' The man was silent at that and did not respond to him (a.s.).

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Amro Bin Usman, from Abu Muhammad Al Mashady, from the family of Raja'a Al Bajaly, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man said to Amir Al-Momineen Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.), 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! By Allah (a.z.w.j.) I (a.s.) love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said to him: 'You are lying'. He said, 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I am swearing by Allah (a.z.w.j.) that I love you (a.s.), and you (a.s.) are saying: 'You are lying'?' He (a.s.) said: '**And what would make you know that Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years and Settled them in the air. Then He (a.z.w.j.) Presented these to us (a.s.) People (a.s.) of the Household. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! There is no soul from these except and I (a.s.) recognised its form. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see you among them, so where were you?**' Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: 'He was in the Fire'.
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ الْمَشْهَدِي مِنْ آلِ رَجَاءِ الْبَحَلِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: قَالَ رَجُلٌ لِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ع يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنَا وَاللَّهِ أُحِبُّكَ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ كَذَبْتَ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللهِ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَحْلِفُ بِاللَّهِ أَنِّي أُحِبُّكَ فَتَقُولُ كَذَبْتَ قالَ وَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلفي عام وَ أَسْكَنَهَا الْهَوَاءَ ثُمَّ عَرَضَهَا عَلَيْنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا مِنْهَا رُوحٌ إِلَّا وَ قَدْ عَرَفْنَا بَدَنَهُ فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا رَأَيْتُكَ فِيهَا فَأَيْنَ كُنْتَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع كَانَ فِي النَّارِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Amro Bin Usman, from Abu Muhammad Al Mashady, from the family of Raja'a Al Bajaly, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man said to Amir Al-Momineen Ali (a.s.) Bin Abu Talib (a.s.), 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! By Allah (a.z.w.j.) I (a.s.) love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said to him: 'You are lying'. He said, 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I am swearing by Allah (a.z.w.j.) that I love you (a.s.), and you (a.s.) are saying: 'You are lying'?'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**And what would make you know that Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years and Settled them in the air. Then He (a.z.w.j.) Presented these to us (a.s.) People (a.s.) of the Household. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! There is no soul from these except and I (a.s.) recognised its form. By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see you among them, so where were you?**' Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: 'He was in the Fire'.

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Adam Abu Al Hassan, from Ismail Bin Abu Hamza, from the one who narrated it, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I love you (a.s.). He (a.s.) said: 'You are lying!' The man said, 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you recognise what is in my heart'. Ali (a.s.) said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented them to us (a.s.). So, where were you? I (a.s.) did not see you!**'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ آدَمَ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَقَالَ وَ اللَّهِ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّكَ فَقَالَ كَذَبْتَ فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ سُبْحَانَ الله كأن كَأَنَّكَ تَعْرِفُ مَا فِي قَلْبِي فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ ع إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفِي عَامٍ ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَيْنَا فَأَيْنَ كُنْتَ لَمْ أرك.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Adam Abu Al Hassan, from Ismail Bin Abu Hamza, from the one who narrated it, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I love you (a.s.). He (a.s.) said: 'You are lying!' The man said, 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you recognise what is in my heart'.
thaqalaynMatn: Ali (a.s.) said: '**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented them to us (a.s.). So, where were you? I (a.s.) did not see you!**'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Adam, from Abu Al Husayn, from Ismail Bin Abu Hamza, from the one who narrated it, From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and said, 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! By Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said to him: 'You are lying'. The man said to him (a.s.), 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you (a.s.) recognise what is within my soul'. He (a.s.) said: '**Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) was angered and he (a.s.) raised his (a.s.) hands towards the sky and said: 'How can that not happen to be and He (a.z.w.j.) is our Lord (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted! He (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented to us (a.s.) the one who loves (us (a.s.)) and the ones who hate (us (a.s.)). By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see you among the ones who love us (a.s.). So, where were you?**'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ آدَمَ عَنْ أَبِي الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى أميرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَقَالَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّكَ فَقَالَ لَهُ كَذَبْتَ فَقَالَ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ كَأَنَّكَ تَعْرِفُ مَا فِي نَفْسِي قالَ فَغَضِبَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع وَ رَفَعَ يَدَهُ إِلى السَّمَاءِ وَ قَالَ كَيْفَ لَا يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ وَ هُوَ رَبُّنَا تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفَيْ عَامٍ ثُمَّ عَرَضَ عَلَيْنَا الْمُحِبُّ مِنَ الْمُبْغِضِ فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا رَأَيْتُكَ فِيمَنْ أَحَبَّنَا فَأَيْنَ كُنْتَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Adam, from Abu Al Husayn, from Ismail Bin Abu Hamza, from the one who narrated it, From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: 'A man came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) and said, 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! By Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said to him: 'You are lying'. The man said to him (a.s.), 'Glory be to Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you (a.s.) recognise what is within my soul'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) was angered and he (a.s.) raised his (a.s.) hands towards the sky and said: 'How can that not happen to be and He (a.z.w.j.) is our Lord (a.z.w.j.) Blessed and Exalted! He (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, then Presented to us (a.s.) the one who loves (us (a.s.)) and the ones who hate (us (a.s.)). By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) did not see you among the ones who love us (a.s.). So, where were you?**'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim who said, 'It is narrated to me by Sallam Bin Abu Umeyr, from Umara who said, 'I was seated in the presence of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) when a man came and greeted unto him (a.s.), then said: 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), by Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)! He (a.s.) asked him, then said to him: '**The souls were Created before the bodies by two thousand years, then they dwelt in the atmosphere, so whatever recognised it, it is from these. Then they get together over here, and whatever they deny is from these. Then they differ over here, and my (a.s.) soul denies your soul.**'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي سَلامُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عُمَارَةً قَالَ: كُنْتُ جَالِسًا عِنْدَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَ إِذْ أَقْبَلَ رَجُلٌ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّكَ فَسَأَلَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ إِنَّ الْأَرْوَاحَ خُلِقَتْ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفِي عَامٍ ثُمَّ أَسْكَنَتِ الْهَوَاءَ فَمَا تَعَارَفَ مِنْهَا ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ هَاهُنَا وَ مَا تَنَاكَرَ مِنْهَا ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ هَاهُنَا وَ إِنَّ رُوحِي أَنْكَرَ رُوحَكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim who said, 'It is narrated to me by Sallam Bin Abu Umeyr, from Umara who said, 'I was seated in the presence of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) when a man came and greeted unto him (a.s.), then said: 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), by Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)!
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) asked him, then said to him: '**The souls were Created before the bodies by two thousand years, then they dwelt in the atmosphere, so whatever recognised it, it is from these. Then they get together over here, and whatever they deny is from these. Then they differ over here, and my (a.s.) soul denies your soul.**'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Abu Muhammad, from Imran Bin Musa, from Yunus Bin Ja'far, from Ali Bin Asbat, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.): 'A man said to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)! three times. Ali (a.s.) said: 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! You do not love me'. The man got angry and said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you (a.s.) are informed of what is within myself'. Ali (a.s.) said to him: '**No, but Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, and I (a.s.) did not see your soul therein.**'
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ لِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع وَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّكَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَ وَ اللَّهِ مَا تُحِبُّنِي فَغَضِبَ الرَّجُلُ فَقَالَ كَأَنَّكَ وَ اللَّهِ تُخْبِرُنِي مَا فِي نَفْسِي قَالَ لَهُ عَلِيٌّ ع لا وَلَكِنَّ اللهَ خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفَيْ عَامٍ فَلَمْ أَرَ رُوحَكَ فِيهَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Abu Muhammad, from Imran Bin Musa, from Yunus Bin Ja'far, from Ali Bin Asbat, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.): 'A man said to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.), I love you (a.s.)! three times. Ali (a.s.) said: 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! You do not love me'. The man got angry and said, 'By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! It is as if you (a.s.) are informed of what is within myself'.
thaqalaynMatn: Ali (a.s.) said to him: '**No, but Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, and I (a.s.) did not see your soul therein.**'

chapter: Chapter 15: Regarding Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), He (a.s.) Recognised What He (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant, and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Abu Muhammad, from Imran Bin Musa, from Ibrahim Bin Mahziyar, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Wahhab, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from his father, 'From one of the companions of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) who said, 'Abdul Rahman Bin Muljim, may Allah (a.z.w.j.) Curse him, entered to see Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) among a delegation of Egypt whom Muhammad Bin Abu Bakr had delegated, and with him was a letter of the delegation. He (the narrator) said, 'When he passed by the name of Abdul Rahman Bin Muljim, he (a.s.) said: 'You are Abdur Rahman? May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Curse Abdul Rahman. He said, 'Yes, O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), By Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), I love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said: 'You are lying, by Allah (a.z.w.j.), you do not love me - three times. He said, 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I am swearing three oaths, I love you (a.s.), and you (a.s.) are swearing three oaths that I do not love you (a.s.). He (a.s.) said: '**Woe be unto you! Or Alas! Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, and Settled them in the air. So, whichever recognised over there get together over here in the world, and whichever of these denied get together in the world, and my (a.s.) soul does not recognise your soul.**' He (the narrator) said, 'When he (a.s.) became ruler, he (a.s.) said: 'When it cheers you to look at my (a.s.) killer, then look at this one'. One of the people said, 'Or will you (a.s.) not kill him?' Or said, 'Kill him!' He (a.s.) said: '**Who is more astounding than this one instructing me (a.s.) to kill my (a.s.) killer.**'
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي الْبِلَادِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ بَعْضٍ أَصْحَابِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ قَالَ: دَخَلَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مُلْحَمٍ لَعَنَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فِي وَقَدِ مِصْرَ الَّذِي أَوْقَدَهُمْ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرِ رَهِ وَ مَعَهُ كِتَابُ الْوَقْدِ قَالَ فَلَمَّا مَرَّ بِاسْمِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مُلْحَمٍ قَالَ أَنتَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ لَعَنَ اللهُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ قَالَ نَعَمْ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِنِّي لَأُحِبُّكَ قَالَ كَذَبْتَ وَ اللَّهِ مَا تُحِبُّنِي ثَلَاثَاً قَالَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أُحْلِفُ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيْمَانٍ أَنِّي أُحِبُّكَ وَ أَنْتَ تَحْلِفُ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيْمَانٍ أَنِّي لَا أُحِبُّكَ قَالَ وَيْلَكَ أَوْ وَيْحَكَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ الْأَرْوَاحَ قَبْلَ الْأَبْدَانِ بِأَلْفَي عَامٍ فَأَسْكَنَهَا الهَوَاءَ فَمَا تَعَارَفَ مِنْهَا هُنَالِكَ الْتَلَفَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ مَا تَنَاكَرَ مِنْهَا اخْتَلَفَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ إِنَّ رُوحِي لَا تَعْرِفُ رُوحَكَ قَالَ فَلَمَّا وَلَّى قَالَ إِذَا سَرَّكُمْ أَنْ تَنْظُرُوا إِلَى قَاتِلِي فَانْظُرُوا إِلى هَذَا قَالَ بَعْضُ الْقَوْمِ أَ وَ لا تَقْتُلُهُ أَوْ قَالَ تَقْتُلُهُ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَعْجَبُ مِنْ هَذَا تَأْمُرُونِي أَنْ أَقْتُلَ قاتلي لع.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Abu Muhammad, from Imran Bin Musa, from Ibrahim Bin Mahziyar, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Wahhab, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from his father, 'From one of the companions of Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) who said, 'Abdul Rahman Bin Muljim, may Allah (a.z.w.j.) Curse him, entered to see Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) among a delegation of Egypt whom Muhammad Bin Abu Bakr had delegated, and with him was a letter of the delegation. He (the narrator) said, 'When he passed by the name of Abdul Rahman Bin Muljim, he (a.s.) said: 'You are Abdur Rahman? May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Curse Abdul Rahman. He said, 'Yes, O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), By Allah (a.z.w.j.), O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.), I love you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said: 'You are lying, by Allah (a.z.w.j.), you do not love me - three times. He said, 'O Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.)! I am swearing three oaths, I love you (a.s.), and you (a.s.) are swearing three oaths that I do not love you (a.s.).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**Woe be unto you! Or Alas! Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls before the bodies by two thousand years, and Settled them in the air. So, whichever recognised over there get together over here in the world, and whichever of these denied get together in the world, and my (a.s.) soul does not recognise your soul.**' He (the narrator) said, 'When he (a.s.) became ruler, he (a.s.) said: 'When it cheers you to look at my (a.s.) killer, then look at this one'. One of the people said, 'Or will you (a.s.) not kill him?' Or said, 'Kill him!' He (a.s.) said: '**Who is more astounding than this one instructing me (a.s.) to kill my (a.s.) killer.**'

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) Recognise What They (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Muhammad Bin Al Husayn both together, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Riab, from Ibn Bukeyr who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) was saying: "**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias with the Wilayah for us (a.s.), they were particles on the day He (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant upon the particles with the acknowledgment to Him (a.z.w.j.) with the Lordship, and to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) with the Prophet-hood.** And Allah (a.z.w.j.) Presented to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) his (s.a.w.a.) community in the clay while they were shadows, and Created them from the clay which He (a.z.w.j.) Created Adam (a.s.) from. **And Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls of our (a.s.) Shias before their bodies by two thousand years and Presented them to him (s.a.w.a.) and Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) introduced them, and introduced Ali (a.s.) to them, and we (a.s.) recognize them in the melody of (their) speech.**"
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ مَعاً عَنِ إِبْنِ مَحْبُوبِ عَنِ ابْنِ رِئَابِ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ قَالَ كَانَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَ شِيعَتِنَا بالْوَلَايَةِ لَنَا وَهُمْ ذَرّ يَوْمَ أَخَذَ الْمِيثَاقَ عَلَى الذَّرِّ بِالْإِقْرَارِ لَهُ بِالرُّبُوبِيَّةِ وَ لِمُحَمَّدٍ صَ بِالنُّبُوَّةِ وَ عَرَضَ اللَّهُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ ص أُمَّتَهُ في الطينِ وَ هُمْ أَظِلَّة وَخَلَقَهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّنَةِ الَّتِي خَلَقَ مِنْهَا آدَمَ وَ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ أَرْوَاحَ شِيعَتِنَا قَبْلَ أَبْدَائِهِمْ بِأَلْفَيْ عَامٍ وَ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَيْهِ وَ عَرَّفَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللهِ ص وَ عَرَّفَهُمْ عَلِياً ع وَ نَحْنُ نَعْرِفُهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Muhammad Bin Al Husayn both together, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Riab, from Ibn Bukeyr who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) was saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias with the Wilayah for us (a.s.), they were particles on the day He (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant upon the particles with the acknowledgment to Him (a.z.w.j.) with the Lordship, and to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) with the Prophet-hood.** And Allah (a.z.w.j.) Presented to Muhammad (s.a.w.a.) his (s.a.w.a.) community in the clay while they were shadows, and Created them from the clay which He (a.z.w.j.) Created Adam (a.s.) from. **And Allah (a.z.w.j.) Created the souls of our (a.s.) Shias before their bodies by two thousand years and Presented them to him (s.a.w.a.) and Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) introduced them, and introduced Ali (a.s.) to them, and we (a.s.) recognize them in the melody of (their) speech.**"

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) Recognise What They (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Mahboub, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, 'From Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "They are fulfilling the vows" [76:7]. He (a.s.) said: "**They fulfil the vows of our (a.s.) Wilayah which were Taken upon them during the Covenant.**"
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ ابْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَن ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَ يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذْرِ قَالَ يُوفُونَ بِالنَّدْرِ الَّذِي أَخَذَ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي الْمِيثَاقِ مِنْ وَلَا يَتِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Mahboub, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzeyl, 'From Abu Al-Hassan (a.s.) regarding Words of Allah (a.z.w.j.) Mighty and Majestic: "They are fulfilling the vows" [76:7].
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**They fulfil the vows of our (a.s.) Wilayah which were Taken upon them during the Covenant.**"

chapter: Chapter 16: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) Recognise What They (a.s.) Had Seen During the Covenant and Other
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hammad Al Kufy, from his father, from Nasr Bin Muzahim, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: "**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias from the ribs (Sulb) of Adam (a.s.). Thus, we (a.s.) recognise with that the love of the loving one even if he expresses to the contrary of that with his tongue, and we (a.s.) recognise the hating one even if he expresses his love for us (a.s.), People of the Household.**"
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَمَّادٍ الْكُوفِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ نَصْرِ بْنِ مُزَاحِمٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شَمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ صُلْبِ آدَمَ فَتَعْرِفُ بِذَلِكَ حُبُّ الْمُحِبِّ وَ إِنْ أَظْهَرَ خِلَافَ ذَلِكَ بِلِسَانِهِ وَنَعْرِفُ بَعْضَ الْمُبْغِضِ وَ إِنْ أَظْهَرَ حُبَّنَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Hammad Al Kufy, from his father, from Nasr Bin Muzahim, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: "**Allah (a.z.w.j.) Took the Covenant of our (a.s.) Shias from the ribs (Sulb) of Adam (a.s.). Thus, we (a.s.) recognise with that the love of the loving one even if he expresses to the contrary of that with his tongue, and we (a.s.) recognise the hating one even if he expresses his love for us (a.s.), People of the Household.**"

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a Kirdeyn who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), 'I was sick, and it was so that when I ate with the man, I was harmed by it, and I ate from your (a.s.) meal, I was not harmed by it'. He (a.s.) said: '**You (a.s.) had eaten a meal of a people the Angels shake hands with them (a.s.) upon their (a.s.) furnishing**'. I said, 'And they appear to you (a.s.) all?' He (a.s.) said: '**They (a.s.) are kinder with our (a.s.) children than we (a.s.) are**'.
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ مِسْمَعِ كِرْدِينٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع إِنِّي اعْتَلَلْتُ فَكُنْتُ إِذَا أَكَلْتُ عِنْدَ الرَّجُلِ تَأَذَّيْتُ بِهِ وَ إِنِّي أَكَلْتُ مِنْ طَعَامِكَ وَ لَمْ أَتَأَذَ بِهِ قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَتَأْكُلُ طَعَامَ قَوْمٍ تُصَافِحُهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ عَلَى فُرُشِهِمْ قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ يَظْهَرُونَ لَكُمْ قَالَ هُمْ أَلْطَفُ بِصِبْيَاتِنَا مِنَّا.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ibn Yazeed, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a Kirdeyn who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), 'I was sick, and it was so that when I ate with the man, I was harmed by it, and I ate from your (a.s.) meal, I was not harmed by it'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**You (a.s.) had eaten a meal of a people the Angels shake hands with them (a.s.) upon their (a.s.) furnishing**'. I said, 'And they appear to you (a.s.) all?' He (a.s.) said: '**They (a.s.) are kinder with our (a.s.) children than we (a.s.) are**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad Al Barqy, from Muhammad Bin al Qasim, from Al Husayn Bin Abu Al A'ala, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**O Husayn! Our (a.s.) houses are the landing strips of the Angels, and stations of the Revelation**', and he (a.s.) struck his (a.s.) hands to a pillow in the room and said: '**O Husayn! By Allah (a.z.w.j.), a pillow is taller after the Angels have leaned upon it, and sometimes we pick up their fluff from it**'.
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: يَا حُسَيْنُ بُيُوتُنَا مَهْبِطُ الْمَلَائِكَةِ وَ مَنْزِلُ الْوَحْيِ وَ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى مَسَاوِرَ فِي الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ يَا حُسَيْنُ مَسَاوِرُ وَ اللَّهِ طَالَ مَا اتَّكَتْ عَلَيْهَا الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَ رُبَّمَا الْتَقَطْنَا مِنْ زَغَبِهَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibn Isa, from Muhammad Al Barqy, from Muhammad Bin al Qasim, from Al Husayn Bin Abu Al A'ala, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**O Husayn! Our (a.s.) houses are the landing strips of the Angels, and stations of the Revelation**', and he (a.s.) struck his (a.s.) hands to a pillow in the room and said: '**O Husayn! By Allah (a.z.w.j.), a pillow is taller after the Angels have leaned upon it, and sometimes we pick up their fluff from it**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Imran Bin Musa, from Musa Bin Ja'far, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Abdullah Bin Sahl Al Ash'ary, from his father, from Abu Al Yas'a who said, 'Humran Bin Ayn entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! It has reached us that the Angels tend to descend unto you (a.s.) all'. He (a.s.) said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.), and tread our furnishings. Have you not read the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: Surely those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah!', then they are steadfast, the Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised [41:30]**'.
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا عِمْرَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٌّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَهْلِ الْأَشْعَرِي عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي الْيَسَعِ قَالَ: دَخَلَ حُمْرَانُ بْنُ أَعْيُنَ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ قَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ يَبْلُغُنَا أَنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ تَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَقَالَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ وَ اللَّهِ لَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا وَ تَطَأُ فُرُشَنَا أَمَا تَقْرَأْ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَ لا تَحْزَنُوا وَ أَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Imran Bin Musa, from Musa Bin Ja'far, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Abdullah Bin Sahl Al Ash'ary, from his father, from Abu Al Yas'a who said, 'Humran Bin Ayn entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! It has reached us that the Angels tend to descend unto you (a.s.) all'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.), and tread our furnishings. Have you not read the Book of Allah (a.z.w.j.) the Exalted: Surely those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah!', then they are steadfast, the Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised [41:30]**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Rabie Bin Abu Al Khattab, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Suleyman bin Khalid, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) regarding Words of the Exalted: Surely, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah!', then they are steadfast, the Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised [41:30], Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) make them recline upon the cushions in our (a.s.) houses**'.
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَبِي الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَ لا تَحْزَنُوا وَ أَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللهِ ع أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ وَشَدْنَاهُمُ الْوَسَائِدَ فِي منازلنا.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Rabie Bin Abu Al Khattab, from Ja'far Bin Bashir, from Suleyman bin Khalid, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) regarding Words of the Exalted: Surely, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah!', then they are steadfast, the Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised [41:30],
thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) make them recline upon the cushions in our (a.s.) houses**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Al Hassan Bin Fazzal, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Musaddiq Bin Sadaqa, from Al Sabatany who said, 'I came across something upon a pillow which was in the house of Abu Abdullah (a.s.). One of our companions said to him (a.s.), 'What is this? May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)!' And it was resembling something than happens to be among a lot of grass, as if it was beads. Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**This is from what falls off from the wings of the Angels**'. Then he (a.s.) said: '**O Ammar! The Angels tend to come to us (a.s.), and they pass by upon the heads of our (a.s.) children. O Ammar! The Angels tend to compete with each other over our (a.s.) cushions**'.
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُصَدِّقِ بْنِ صَدَقَةَ عَنِ السَّابَاطِي قَالَ : أَصَبْتُ شَيْئاً عَلَى وَسَائِدَ كَانَتْ فِي مَنْزِلِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع فَقَالَ لَهُ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا مَا هَذَا جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ وَكَانَ يُشْبِهُ شَيْئًا يَكُونُ فِي الحَشِيشِ كَثِيرًا كَأَنَّهُ حَرَزَةٌ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع هَذَا مِمَّا يَسْقُطُ مِنْ أَجْنِحَةِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا عَمَّارُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَأْتِينَا وَ إِنَّهَا لَتَمُرُّ بِأَجْنِحَتِهَا عَلَى رُءُوسِ صِبْيَانِنَا يَا عَمَّارُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتُرَاحِمُنَا عَلَى نَمَارِقِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Al Hassan Bin Fazzal, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Musaddiq Bin Sadaqa, from Al Sabatany who said, 'I came across something upon a pillow which was in the house of Abu Abdullah (a.s.). One of our companions said to him (a.s.), 'What is this? May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)!' And it was resembling something than happens to be among a lot of grass, as if it was beads.
thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said: '**This is from what falls off from the wings of the Angels**'. Then he (a.s.) said: '**O Ammar! The Angels tend to come to us (a.s.), and they pass by upon the heads of our (a.s.) children. O Ammar! The Angels tend to compete with each other over our (a.s.) cushions**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakarn, from Malik Bin Atiyya Al Ahmasy, from Al Sumali who said, 'I entered to see Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Husayn (a.s.). I was withheld in the house for a moment, then entered the house to see him (a.s.), and he (a.s.) had picked up something and inserted his (a.s.) hand behind the curtain and gave it to someone in the house. I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! This which you (a.s.) picked up, which thing is it?' He (a.s.) said: '**It is a remnant from the fluff of the Angels. We (a.s.) gather it when they come to us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) make a toy for our (a.s.) children**'. I said to him (a.s.). 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! And they come to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) said: '**O Abu Hamza! They tend to fight with each other over our (a.s.) pillows**'.
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ الْأَحْمَسِيِّ عَنِ الثَّمَانِي قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ع فَاحْتُبِسْتُ فِي الدَّارِ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ دَخَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ الْبَيْتَ وَ هُوَ يَلْتَقِطْ شَيْئاً وَ أَدْخَلَ يَدَهُ فِي وَرَاءِ السَّتْرِ فَنَاوَلَهُ مَنْ كَانَ فِي الْبَيْتِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ هَذَا الَّذِي أَرَاكَ تَلْتَقِطُ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ فَقَالَ فَضْلَةٌ مِنْ زَغَبِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ نَجْمَعُهُ إِذَا جَاءُونَا وَ سحاباً لِأَوْلادِنَا قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَأْتُونَكُمْ قَالَ يَا أَبَا حَمْزَةَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيُرَاحِمُونًا على تُكَأْتِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakarn, from Malik Bin Atiyya Al Ahmasy, from Al Sumali who said, 'I entered to see Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Husayn (a.s.). I was withheld in the house for a moment, then entered the house to see him (a.s.), and he (a.s.) had picked up something and inserted his (a.s.) hand behind the curtain and gave it to someone in the house. I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! This which you (a.s.) picked up, which thing is it?'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**It is a remnant from the fluff of the Angels. We (a.s.) gather it when they come to us (a.s.), and we (a.s.) make a toy for our (a.s.) children**'. I said to him (a.s.). 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! And they come to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) said: '**O Abu Hamza! They tend to fight with each other over our (a.s.) pillows**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abdullah bin Abdul Rahman Al Basry, from Abu Al Magra'a, from Abu Baseer, from Khaysama, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: '**We (a.s.) are those the Angels interchange to us (a.s.)**'.
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَعْرُوفِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْبَصْرِي عَنْ أَبِي الْمَغْرَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ نَحْنُ الَّذِينَ إِلَيْنَا تَخْتَلِفُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abdullah bin Abdul Rahman Al Basry, from Abu Al Magra'a, from Abu Baseer, from Khaysama, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**We (a.s.) are those the Angels interchange to us (a.s.)**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik, from Al Sumali, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: '**From us (a.s.) is one who hears the voice and does not see the face, and that the Angels tend to fight each other over our (a.s.) pillows, and we (a.s.) tend to take from their fluff and make a toy for our (a.s.) children**'.
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مَالِكٍ عَنِ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مِنَّا مَنْ يَسْمَعُ الصَّوْتَ وَ لَا يَرَى الصُّورَةَ وَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتُرَاحِمنَا عَلَى تُكَأْتِنَا وَ إِنَّا لَنَأْخَذُ مِنْ زَعْبِهِمْ فَتَجْعَلُهُ سِحَابِاً لِأَوْلَادِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik, from Al Sumali, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**From us (a.s.) is one who hears the voice and does not see the face, and that the Angels tend to fight each other over our (a.s.) pillows, and we (a.s.) tend to take from their fluff and make a toy for our (a.s.) children**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a Kirdeyn Al Basry who said, 'I did not use to eat quite excessively during the night and the day. Sometimes I would seek permission (late) to see Abu Abdullah, and he (a.s.) would have taken the meal so that I would not see it. Then I entered and he (a.s.) called for it and I attained with him (a.s.) from the meal, and I was not harmed by that, and then I followed it with the meal with others, and I was not able upon settling down, and could not sleep from the murmur (gas). I complained of that to him (a.s.) and informed him (a.s.) with that whenever I ate with him (a.s.) I was not harmed by it. He (a.s.) said: '**O Abu Sayyar! You are eating food of a righteous people. The Angels shake their (a.s.) hands upon their (a.s.) furnishings**'. I said, 'They appear to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) wiped his (a.s.) hand upon one of his (a.s.) children and said: '**They are kinder with our (a.s.) children than we (a.s.) are with them**'.
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ مِسْمَع كِرْدِينِ الْبَصْرِي قَالَ: كُنتُ لَا أَزِيدُ عَلَى أُكْلَةٍ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَ النَّهَارِ فَرُبَّمَا اسْتَأْذَنْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع وَ أُخِذَتِ الْمَائِدَةُ لَعَلِّي لَا أَرَاهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُ دَعَا بِمَا فَأَصَبْتُ مَعَهُ مِنَ الطَّعَامِ وَ لَا أَتَأَذًى بِذَلِكَ وَ إِذَا عَقَبْتُ بِالطَّعَامِ عِنْدَ غَيْرِهِ لَمْ أَقْدِرْ عَلَى أَنْ أَقرَّ وَ لَمْ أَتُمْ مِنَ النَّفْحَةِ فَشَكَوْتُ ذَلِكَ إِلَيْهِ وَ أَخْبَرْتُهُ بِأَنِّي إِذَا أَكَلْتُ عِنْدَهُ ثُمَّ أَتَاذُ بِهِ فَقَالَ يَا أَبَا سَيَّارٍ إِنَّكَ لَتَأْكُلُ طَعَامَ قَوْمٍ صَالِحِينَ تُصَافِحُهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ عَلَى فُرْشِهِمْ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَظْهَرُونَ لَكُمْ قَالَ فَمَسَحَ يَدَهُ عَلَى بَعْضٍ صِبْيَانِهِ فَقَالَ هُمْ أَلْطَفُ بِصِبْيَاتِنَا مِنَّا بِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad and Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a Kirdeyn Al Basry who said, 'I did not use to eat quite excessively during the night and the day. Sometimes I would seek permission (late) to see Abu Abdullah, and he (a.s.) would have taken the meal so that I would not see it. Then I entered and he (a.s.) called for it and I attained with him (a.s.) from the meal, and I was not harmed by that, and then I followed it with the meal with others, and I was not able upon settling down, and could not sleep from the murmur (gas). I complained of that to him (a.s.) and informed him (a.s.) with that whenever I ate with him (a.s.) I was not harmed by it.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O Abu Sayyar! You are eating food of a righteous people. The Angels shake their (a.s.) hands upon their (a.s.) furnishings**'. I said, 'They appear to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) wiped his (a.s.) hand upon one of his (a.s.) children and said: '**They are kinder with our (a.s.) children than we (a.s.) are with them**'.

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Aban Bin Usman, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**Verily, the Angels tend to descend upon us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses, and we (a.s.) tend to see them.**'
arabicText: 10- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَةَ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا فِي مَنَازِلِنَا وَ نَرَاهُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Aban Bin Usman, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Verily, the Angels tend to descend upon us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses, and we (a.s.) tend to see them.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Abdul A'la who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and they bring us (a.s.) the news of the earth.**'
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَأْتُونَا بِأَخْبَارِ الْأَرْضِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Abdul A'la who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and they bring us (a.s.) the news of the earth.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Al Sindy Bin Muhammad, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ishaq Bin Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend upon us (a.s.), and they tread upon our (a.s.) furnishings.**'
arabicText: 12- حَدَّثَنَا السَّنْدِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ مَلَائِكَةَ اللَّهِ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا وَ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَطَئُونَ فُرُشَنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Al Sindy Bin Muhammad, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ishaq Bin Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend upon us (a.s.), and they tread upon our (a.s.) furnishings.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al-Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me: '**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend to us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses and bring us (a.s.) the news of everything.**'
arabicText: 13- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ قَالَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع يَا أَبَا الْحَجَّاجِ إِنَّ مَلَائِكَةَ اللَّهِ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا فِي مَنَازِلِنَا وَ يَأْتُونَا بِأَخْبَارِ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al-Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me:
thaqalaynMatn: '**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! The Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend to us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses and bring us (a.s.) the news of everything.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Seyf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and turn over upon our (a.s.) carpets.**'
arabicText: 14- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا وَ تَتَقَلَّبُ عَلَى فُرُشِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Seyf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and turn over upon our (a.s.) carpets.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Abu Abdullah Al Barqy, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "Do the Angels descend upon you (a.s.)?" He (a.s.) said: '**Yes, by Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They descend upon us (a.s.) and they are kinder to our (a.s.) children than us.**'
arabicText: 15- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ مِسْمَعٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَ تَتَنَزَّلُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ عَلَيْكُمْ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَتَتَنَزَّلُونَ عَلَيْنَا وَ هُمْ أَلْطَفُ بِصِبْيَانِنَا مِنَّا.
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Abu Abdullah Al Barqy, from Ibn Sinan, from Misma'a who said, 'I said to Abu Abdullah (a.s.), "Do the Angels descend upon you (a.s.)?"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**Yes, by Allah (a.z.w.j.)! They descend upon us (a.s.) and they are kinder to our (a.s.) children than us.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 16. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour Bin Yunus, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: '**Verily, the Angels tend to descend to us (a.s.) every day and night, bringing us (a.s.) the greetings of peace from our (a.s.) Lord (a.z.w.j.).**'
arabicText: 16- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ يُونُسَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا كُلَّ يَوْمٍ وَ لَيْلَةٍ تَأْتِينَا بِالسَّلَامِ مِنْ رَبِّنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 16. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour Bin Yunus, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: '**Verily, the Angels tend to descend to us (a.s.) every day and night, bringing us (a.s.) the greetings of peace from our (a.s.) Lord (a.z.w.j.).**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 17. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Musa, from Al Hassan Bin Musa, from Ali Bin Hassan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) with the news of the heavens and the earth.**'
arabicText: 17- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا بِأَخْبَارِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 17. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Musa, from Al Hassan Bin Musa, from Ali Bin Hassan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Kaseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) with the news of the heavens and the earth.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 18. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim and Ahmad Bin Al Hussein, from his father, from Abdul Kareem, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: "The Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised' (41:30). 'We are your Guardians in the life of the world and in the Hereafter, and for you therein is whatever your souls desire, and for you therein would be whatever you call for' (41:31). 'Being a hospitality from the Forgiving, Merciful' (41:32)." Then he (a.s.) said: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) recline them on our (a.s.) cushions.**"
arabicText: 18 - حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ وَ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَ لا تَحْزَنُوا وَ أَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ نَحْنُ أَوْلِيَاؤُكُمْ فِي الحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيا و في الآخِرَةِ وَ لَكُمْ فيها ما تَشْتَهِي أَنْفُسُكُمْ وَ لَكُمْ فِيهَا ما تَدَعُونَ نُزُلًا مِنْ غَفُورٍ رَحِيمٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا لَتُتْكِلُهُمْ عَلَى وَسَائِدِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 18. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim and Ahmad Bin Al Hussein, from his father, from Abdul Kareem, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said, 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "The Angels would descend unto them (saying): 'Do not fear, and do not grieve, and receive glad tidings of the Paradise which you were Promised' (41:30). 'We are your Guardians in the life of the world and in the Hereafter, and for you therein is whatever your souls desire, and for you therein would be whatever you call for' (41:31). 'Being a hospitality from the Forgiving, Merciful' (41:32)." Then he (a.s.) said: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) recline them on our (a.s.) cushions.**"

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 19. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: '**We (a.s.) are the ones to whom the Angels interchange.**'
arabicText: 19- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: نَحْنُ الَّذِينَ إِلَيْنَا تَخْتَلِفُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 19. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah, from Abu Baseer, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**We (a.s.) are the ones to whom the Angels interchange.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 20. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said: '**The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and we (a.s.) see them.**'
arabicText: 20- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا وَ نَرَاهُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 20. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hussein Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar, from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The Angels descend upon us (a.s.) and we (a.s.) see them.**'

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 21. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Hussein, from Al Hassan Bin Barra Al Asamma, from Abu Bukeyr, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying: "**The Angels tend to descend to us (a.s.) among our (a.s.) luggage, and turn over upon our (a.s.) furnishings, and attend our (a.s.) meals, and they come to us (a.s.) with every vegetation in its time, wet and dry, and turn (with kindness to) our (a.s.) children, and prevent the animals to arrive to us (a.s.), and come to us (a.s.) during the time of every Salat to pray it with us (a.s.).** And there is none from a day nor from a night they come to us (a.s.) except and the news of the people of the earth is with us (a.s.), and what has occurred during it, and there is none from a king dying in the earth and another stands, except and they come to us (a.s.) with his news, and how his ways were in the world."'
arabicText: 21- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ بَرَّةَ الْأَصَمَّ عَنْ أَبِي بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا فِي رِحَالِنَا وَ تَتَقَلَّبُ عَلَى فُرْشِنَا وَ تَحْضُرُ مَوَائِدَنَا وَ تَأْتِينَا مِنْ كُلِّ نَبَاتٍ فِي زَمَانِهِ رَطْبٍ وَ يَابِسٍ وَ تُقَلِّبُ صِبْيَانَنَا وَ تَمْنَعُ الدَّوَابُ أَنْ تَصِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَ تَأْتِينَا فِي وَقْتِ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ لِتُصَلِّيَهَا مَعَنَا وَ مَا مِنْ يَوْمٍ يَأْتِي عَلَيْنَا وَ لَا لَيْلٍ إِلَّا وَ أَحْبَارُ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ عِنْدَنَا وَ مَا يَحْدُثُ فِيهَا وَ مَا مِنْ مَلِكٍ يَمُوتُ فِي أَرْضِ وَ يَقُومُ غَيْرُهُ إِلَّا وَ تَأْتِينَا بِخَبَرِهِ وَكَيْفَ كان سيرته في الدُّنْيَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 21. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Hussein, from Al Hassan Bin Barra Al Asamma, from Abu Bukeyr, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.), he (the narrator) said, 'I heard him (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "**The Angels tend to descend to us (a.s.) among our (a.s.) luggage, and turn over upon our (a.s.) furnishings, and attend our (a.s.) meals, and they come to us (a.s.) with every vegetation in its time, wet and dry, and turn (with kindness to) our (a.s.) children, and prevent the animals to arrive to us (a.s.), and come to us (a.s.) during the time of every Salat to pray it with us (a.s.).** And there is none from a day nor from a night they come to us (a.s.) except and the news of the people of the earth is with us (a.s.), and what has occurred during it, and there is none from a king dying in the earth and another stands, except and they come to us (a.s.) with his news, and how his ways were in the world."

chapter: Chapter 17: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Angels Enter Their (a.s.) Houses and Circle (Tread) Their (a.s.) Carpets and Come to Them (a.s.) with the News
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 22. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al-Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me: "**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! Verily, the Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend to us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses, and they tread upon our (a.s.) carpets, and bring us (a.s.) the news of everything that occurs in the heavens and the earth.**"'
arabicText: 22- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ قَالَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع يَا أَبَا الْحَجَّاجِ إِنَّ مَلَائِكَةَ اللَّهِ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا فِي مَنَازِلِنَا وَ يَطَئُونَ فُرُشَنَا وَ يَأْتُونَا بِأَخْبَارِ كُلِّ مَا يَحْدُثُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 22. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al-Hajjaj who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me:
thaqalaynMatn: "**O Abu Al-Hajjaj! Verily, the Angels of Allah (a.z.w.j.) descend to us (a.s.) in our (a.s.) houses, and they tread upon our (a.s.) carpets, and bring us (a.s.) the news of everything that occurs in the heavens and the earth.**"'

chapter: Rare from Chapter 17
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying: "**Verily, the Angels descend upon us (a.s.) every day and night, bringing us (a.s.) the greetings of peace from our (a.s.) Lord (a.z.w.j.).**"'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْنَا كُلَّ يَوْمٍ وَ لَيْلَةٍ تَأْتِينَا بِالسَّلَامِ مِنْ رَبِّنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour, from Abu Baseer who said: 'I heard Abu Abdullah (a.s.) saying:
thaqalaynMatn: "**Verily, the Angels descend upon us (a.s.) every day and night, bringing us (a.s.) the greetings of peace from our (a.s.) Lord (a.z.w.j.).**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Hasan, from Musa bin Bakr, from a man, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said: '**The day of Sunday is for the Jinn, they do not appear to anyone during it apart from us (a.s.).**'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ بَكْرٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ : يَوْمُ الْأَحَدِ لِلْجِنَّ لَيْسَ تَظْهَرُ فِيهِ لِأَحَدٍ غَيْرِنَا.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ali Bin Hasan, from Musa bin Bakr, from a man, 'From Abu Abdullah (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: '**The day of Sunday is for the Jinn, they do not appear to anyone during it apart from us (a.s.).**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr Al Sayrafi who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) sent me regarding some needs of his (a.s.) at Al-Medina. While I was in a mountain pass of Al-Rawha upon my riding animal when a man gestured towards me with his cloth. I inclined towards him and thought he might be thirsty, and I gave him the cure (water). He said, 'There is no need for me with it'. Then he gave me a letter, its texture was wet (ink).
