When I looked at its seal, it was a seal of Abu Ja'far (a.s.). I said to him, 'When did the owner of the letter pact to you?' He said, 'Just now'. There were things in it instructing me with it. Then he (the narrator said), 'I turned around and there wasn't anyone with me. So, I proceeded to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and met him (a.s.). I said to him (a.s.), 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! A man came to me with your (a.s.) letter and its texture was wet (ink).' He (a.s.) said: '**When there is haste for us (a.s.) with a matter, I (a.s.) send one of them**' - meaning the Jinn. And there is an increase in it by Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, by this chain: '**O Sadeyr! For us (a.s.) there are servants from the Jinn. Whenever we (a.s.) want a quickness, we (a.s.) send them.**'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي الْبِلَادِ عَنْ سَدِيرِ الصَّيْرَي قَالَ : أَوْصَانِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع بِحَوَائِجَ لَهُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ قَالَ فَبَيْنَا أَنَا فِي فَجٍّ الرَّوْحَاءِ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِي إِذَا إِنْسَانٌ يَأْوِي بِثَوْبِهِ قَالَ فَمِلْتُ إِلَيْهِ وَ ظَنَنْتُ أَنَّهُ عَطْشَانٌ فَنَاوَلْتُهُ الْإِدَاوَةَ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَا حَاجَةَ لِي بِمَا ثُمَّ نَاوَلَنِي كِتَاباً طِينُهُ رَطْبٌ قَالَ فَلَمَّا نَظَرْتُ إِلَى خَلْمِهِ إِذَا هُوَ خاتَمُ أبي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقُلْتُ لَهُ مَتَى عَهْدُكَ بِصَاحِبِ الْكِتَابِ قَالَ السَّاعَةَ قَالَ فَإِذَا فِيهِ أَشْيَاءُ يَأْمُرُنِي بِمَا ثُمَّ قَالَ الْتَفَتُ فَإِذَا لَيْسَ عِنْدِي أَحَدٌ قَالَ فَقَدِمَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع فَلَقِيتُهُ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ رَجُلٌ أَتَانِي بِكِتَابِكَ وَ طِيتُهُ رَطْبٌ قَالَ إِذَا عَجِلَ بِنَا أَمْرٌ أَرْسَلْتُ بَعْضَهُمْ يَعْنِي الْجِنَّ وَ زَادَ فِيهِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ يَا سَدِيرُ إِنَّ لَنَا خَدَماً مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَإِذَا أَرَدْنَا السُّرْعَةَ بَعَثْنَاهُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibrahim Bin Abu Al Bilad, from Sadeyr Al Sayrafi who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) sent me regarding some needs of his (a.s.) at Al-Medina. While I was in a mountain pass of Al-Rawha upon my riding animal when a man gestured towards me with his cloth. I inclined towards him and thought he might be thirsty, and I gave him the cure (water). He said, 'There is no need for me with it'. Then he gave me a letter, its texture was wet (ink). When I looked at its seal, it was a seal of Abu Ja'far (a.s.). I said to him, 'When did the owner of the letter pact to you?' He said, 'Just now'. There were things in it instructing me with it. Then he (the narrator said), 'I turned around and there wasn't anyone with me. So, I proceeded to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and met him (a.s.). I said to him (a.s.), 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! A man came to me with your (a.s.) letter and its texture was wet (ink).'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**When there is haste for us (a.s.) with a matter, I (a.s.) send one of them**' - meaning the Jinn. And there is an increase in it by Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, by this chain: '**O Sadeyr! For us (a.s.) there are servants from the Jinn. Whenever we (a.s.) want a quickness, we (a.s.) send them.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 3. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Al Sumali who said, 'I was seeking permission to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.). It was said, 'There is a group with him (a.s.), wait for a while until they come out'. A group came out I disliked and did not recognise them. Then he (a.s.) permitted for me, so I entered to see him (a.s.) and said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! This is the era of the clan of Umayya and their swords are dripping blood'. He (a.s.) said to me: '**O Abu Hamza! They are a delegation of our (a.s.) Shias from the Jinn. They came to ask us (a.s.) about the matters of their Religion.**'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ عَنِ الثَّمَالِي قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَسْتَأْذِنُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقِيلَ إِنَّ عِنْدَهُ قوم قَوْماً أَثْبُتُ قَلِيلًا حَتَّى يَخْرُجُوا فَخَرَجَ قَوْمٌ أَنْكَرْتُهُمْ وَ لَمْ أَعْرِفُهُمْ ثُمَّ أَذِنَ لِي فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ هَذَا زَمَانُ بَنِي أُمَيَّةَ وَ سَيْفُهُمْ يَقْطُرُ دَما فَقَالَ لِي يَا أَبَا حمزة هؤلاء وَقَدُ شِيعَتِنَا مِنَ الْجِنِّ جَاءُوا يَسْأَلُونَنَا عَنْ مَعَالِمِ دِينِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 3. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Al Sumali who said, 'I was seeking permission to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.). It was said, 'There is a group with him (a.s.), wait for a while until they come out'. A group came out I disliked and did not recognise them. Then he (a.s.) permitted for me, so I entered to see him (a.s.) and said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! This is the era of the clan of Umayya and their swords are dripping blood'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said to me: '**O Abu Hamza! They are a delegation of our (a.s.) Shias from the Jinn. They came to ask us (a.s.) about the matters of their Religion.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 4. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Ali Bin al Hakam from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Al Sumali who said, 'I was with Abu Abdullah (a.s.) in what is between Makkah and Al-Medina when he (a.s.) turned towards his (a.s.) left, and there was a black dog. He (a.s.) said: '**What is the matter with you? May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Uglify you! How quick you are.**' And there he was resembling the bird. I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! What is it?' He (a.s.) said: '**This is Asam, 'postman' of the Jinn. Hisham died just now so he is flying out to give the news of his death in every city.**'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ عَنِ الثَّمَالِيِّ قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فِيمَا بَيْنَ مَكَّةَ وَ الْمَدِينَةِ إِذَا الْتَفَتَ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ فَإِذَا كَلْبٌ أَسْوَدُ فَقَالَ مَا لَكَ فَبَحَكَ اللهُ مَا أَشَدَّ مُسَارَعَتَكَ فَإِذَا هُوَ شَبِيءٌ بِالطَّائِرِ فَقُلْتُ مَا هُوَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَقَالَ هَذَا عَثْمٌ بَرِيدُ الْحِنِّ مَاتَ هِشَامُ السَّاعَةَ فَهُوَ يَطِيرُ يَنْعَاهُ فِي كُلِّ بَلْدَةٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 4. It is narrated to me by Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Ali Bin al Hakam from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Al Sumali who said, 'I was with Abu Abdullah (a.s.) in what is between Makkah and Al-Medina when he (a.s.) turned towards his (a.s.) left, and there was a black dog.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**What is the matter with you? May Allah (a.z.w.j.) Uglify you! How quick you are.**' And there he was resembling the bird. I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! What is it?' He (a.s.) said: '**This is Asam, 'postman' of the Jinn. Hisham died just now so he is flying out to give the news of his death in every city.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad, from Ali Bin Hadeed, from Ibn Hazim, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to the door of Abu Ja'far (a.s.) with companions of our in order to enter to see him (a.s.), and there were eight persons as if they were from a father and mother (resembling each other), having clothes of carpets and cloaks, layer upon layer, and their turbans were yellow. They entered and were not withheld until they came out. He (a.s.) said to me: '**O Sa'ad! Did you see them?**' I said, 'Yes, may I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said: '**They are your brethren from the Jinn. They came to us (a.s.) to have our (a.s.) verdicts regarding their Permissible(s) and their Prohibitions, just as you (Shias) come to us (a.s.) and have our (a.s.) verdicts regarding your Permissible(s) and your Prohibitions.**'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدٌ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَدِيدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ سَعْدِ الْإِسْكَافِ قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ بَابَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع مَعَ أَصْحَابِ لَنَا لِتَدْخُلَ عَلَيْهِ فَإِذَا ثَمَانِيَةٌ نَفَرٍ كَأَنَّهُمْ مِنْ أَبِ وَ أُمَّ عَلَيْهِمْ ثِيَابٌ زَرَانِيُّ وَ أَقْبِيَةٌ طَاقَ طَاقَ وَ عَمَائِمُ صُفْرٌ دَخَلُوا فَمَا احْتَبَسُوا حَتَّى خَرَجُوا قَالَ لِي يَا سَعْدُ رَأَيْتَهُمْ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ أُولَئِكَ إِخْوَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ أَتَوْنَا يَسْتَفْتُونَنَا فِي حَلَالِهِمْ وَ حَرَامِهِمْ كَمَا تَأْتُونَا وَ تَسْتَفْتُونَا فِي حَلَالِكُمْ وَ حَرَامِكُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 5. It is narrated to us by Muhammad, from Ali Bin Hadeed, from Ibn Hazim, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to the door of Abu Ja'far (a.s.) with companions of our in order to enter to see him (a.s.), and there were eight persons as if they were from a father and mother (resembling each other), having clothes of carpets and cloaks, layer upon layer, and their turbans were yellow. They entered and were not withheld until they came out.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said to me: '**O Sa'ad! Did you see them?**' I said, 'Yes, may I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)!' He (a.s.) said: '**They are your brethren from the Jinn. They came to us (a.s.) to have our (a.s.) verdicts regarding their Permissible(s) and their Prohibitions, just as you (Shias) come to us (a.s.) and have our (a.s.) verdicts regarding your Permissible(s) and your Prohibitions.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 6. From him, from Ibn Sinan, from Ibn Muskan, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I sought the permission from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and he (a.s.) sent a message to me: '**Do not be hasty for there is a group of your brethren in my (a.s.) presence.**' It was not long before twelve men came out to me resembling the generation of one people (similar looking), having cloaks upon them, layered, and footwear. They greeted and passed by. And I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), 'I did not recognise them, may I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), those who came out, so who are they?' He (a.s.) said: '**They are a people from your brethren from the Jinn.**' I said to him (a.s.), 'And they appear to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) said: '**Yes.**'
arabicText: 6- عَنْهُ عَنِ ابْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ سَعْدِ الْإِسْكَافِ قَالَ: طَلَبْتُ الْإِذْنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَبَعَثَ إلى لا تَعْجَلْ فَإِنَّ عِنْدِي قَوْماً مِنْ إِخْوَانِكُمْ فلم البَتْ أَنْ خَرَجَ عَلَيَّ اثْنَا عَشَرَ رَجُلًا يُشْبِهُونَ الرُّطَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَقْبِيَةٌ طَبَقَيْنِ وَ حِفَافٌ فَسَلَّمُوا وَ مَرُّوا وَ دَخَلْتُ إلى أَبي جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا أَعْرِفُ هؤلاء جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا فَمَنْ هُمْ قَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ قَوْمٌ مِنْ إِخْوَانِكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ قُلْتُ لَهُ وَ يَظْهَرُونَ لَكُمْ قَالَ نَعَمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 6. From him, from Ibn Sinan, from Ibn Muskan, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I sought the permission from Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and he (a.s.) sent a message to me: '**Do not be hasty for there is a group of your brethren in my (a.s.) presence.**' It was not long before twelve men came out to me resembling the generation of one people (similar looking), having cloaks upon them, layered, and footwear. They greeted and passed by. And I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), 'I did not recognise them, may I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), those who came out, so who are they?'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**They are a people from your brethren from the Jinn.**' I said to him (a.s.), 'And they appear to you (a.s.)?' He (a.s.) said: '**Yes.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Abdullah Al Momin, from Yahya Bin Musawer, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) during a time of intense heat. I reached his (a.s.) door and saw riding beasts upon which were their belongings. I heard noises of a group from inside. Then a people came out to me with extreme beauty and perfection. I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! These people who just came out from your (a.s.) presence, I have never seen a people better looking than them!' He (a.s.) said: '**O Sa'ad! Did you see them?**' I said, 'Yes.' He (a.s.) said: '**They are your brothers from the Jinn. They come to us (a.s.) in order to ask us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion.**'
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُؤْمِنِ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ مُسَاوِرٍ عَنْ سَعْدِ الْإِسْكَافِ قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع فِي وَقْتٍ حَارٌ شَدِيدِ الحَرِ فَانْتَهَيْتُ إِلَى بَابِهِ فَإِذَا رَوَاحِلُ عَلَيْهَا رِحَالُهَا وَ سَمِعْتُ أَصْوَاتَ قَوْمٍ مِنْ دَاخِلٍ فَخَرَجَ إِلَيَّ قَوْمٌ فِي غَايَةِ الْجَمَالِ وَ التَّمَامِ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ هَؤُلَاءِ الْقَوْمُ الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِنْ عِنْدِكَ مَا رَأَيْتُ قَوْماً قَطُّ أَحْسَنَ مِنْهُمْ قَالَ يَا سَعْدُ رَأَيْتَهُمْ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ إِخْوَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ يَأْتُونَا لِيَسْأَلُونَا عَنْ مَعَالِمِ دِينِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 7. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Abdullah Al Momin, from Yahya Bin Musawer, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) during a time of intense heat. I reached his (a.s.) door and saw riding beasts upon which were their belongings. I heard noises of a group from inside. Then a people came out to me with extreme beauty and perfection. I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and I said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! These people who just came out from your (a.s.) presence, I have never seen a people better looking than them!'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O Sa'ad! Did you see them?**' I said, 'Yes.' He (a.s.) said: '**They are your brothers from the Jinn. They come to us (a.s.) in order to ask us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 8. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Umar Bin Yazeed, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said: 'While Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was seated when a tall man came to him (s.a.w.a.) as if he was a palm tree. He greeted him (s.a.w.a.), and he (s.a.w.a.) returned the greeting and said: "You resemble the Jinn and their speech, so who are you O servant of Allah (a.z.w.j.)?" He said, "**I am Al-Ham Bin Al-Heem Bin Al-Aqeys Bin Iblees.**" Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "There are only two fathers between you and Iblees?" He said, "Yes, O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)." He (s.a.w.a.) said: "So, how much of time has come to you?" He said: "I have consumed the age of the world except for a little... I was with Musa (a.s.) and he (a.s.) taught me clarification from the Torah and said: 'When you meet Isa (a.s.), then convey the greetings to him (a.s.) from me (a.s.)'. I did meet him (a.s.) and conveyed the greetings to him (a.s.) from Musa (a.s.), and he (a.s.) taught me clarification from the Evangel, and he (a.s.) said: '**If you come across Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), then convey the greetings to him (s.a.w.a.) from me (a.s.).**' So, Isa (a.s.), O Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), conveys the greetings unto you (s.a.w.a.)." When it was the day of Al-Hareer (battle of Siffeen), he came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) during his (a.s.) battle and said to him (a.s.), "O successor (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)! We find in the Books of the Prophets (a.s.) that the short-haired one is the successor (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), best of the people. Uncover your (a.s.) head". He (a.s.) uncovered his (a.s.) helmet from his (a.s.) head and said: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) am that, O Haam!**"'
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ ع قَالَ : بَيْنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص جَالِسٌ إِذْ أَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ طَوِيلٌ كَأَنَّهُ نَخْلَةٌ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامَ وَ قَالَ يُشْبِهُ الْجِنَّ وَكَلَامَهُمْ فَمَنْ أَنْتَ يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ أَنَا الْهَامُ بْنُ الْهِيمِ بْنِ لَا فِيسَ بْنِ إِبْلِيسَ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَا بَيْنَكَ وَ بَيْنَ إِبْلِيسَ إِلَّا أَبَوَيْنِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ قَالَ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَكَمْ أَتَى لَكَ قَالَ أَكَلْتُ عُمُرَ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا أَفَلَهُ ... ثُمَّ كُنتُ مَعَ مُوسَى ع وَ عَلَّمَنِي سِفْراً مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَ قَالَ إِنْ أَدْرَكْتَ عِيسَى فَأَفْرِلَهُ مِنِّي السَّلَامَ فَلَقِيتُهُ وَ أَقْرَأْتُهُ مِنْ مُوسَى السَّلَامَ وَ عَلَّمَنِي سِفْراً مِنَ الْإِنْجِيلِ وَ قَالَ إِنْ أَدْرَكْتَ مُحَمَّداً ص فَأَقْرِلَهُ مِنِّي السَّلَامَ - فَعِيسَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ يَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ السَّلَامَ ... فَلَمَّا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْحَرِيرِ أَتَى أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي حَرْبِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا وَصِيَّ مُحَمَّدٍ إِنَّا وَجَدْنَا فِي كُتُبِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ أَنَّ الْأَصْلَعَ وَصِي مُحَمَّدٍ خَيْرُ النَّاسِ اكْشِفَ رَأْسَكَ فَكَشَفَ عَنْ رَأْسِهِ مِغْفَرَهُ وَ قَالَ أَنَا وَاللَّهِ ذَاكَ يَا هَامُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 8. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Ibrahim Bin Is'haq, from Abdullah Bin Hammad, from Umar Bin Yazeed, from Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who said:
thaqalaynMatn: 'While Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) was seated when a tall man came to him (s.a.w.a.) as if he was a palm tree. He greeted him (s.a.w.a.), and he (s.a.w.a.) returned the greeting and said: "You resemble the Jinn and their speech, so who are you O servant of Allah (a.z.w.j.)?" He said, "**I am Al-Ham Bin Al-Heem Bin Al-Aqeys Bin Iblees.**" Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.) said: "There are only two fathers between you and Iblees?" He said, "Yes, O Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)." He (s.a.w.a.) said: "So, how much of time has come to you?" He said: "I have consumed the age of the world except for a little... I was with Musa (a.s.) and he (a.s.) taught me clarification from the Torah and said: 'When you meet Isa (a.s.), then convey the greetings to him (a.s.) from me (a.s.)'. I did meet him (a.s.) and conveyed the greetings to him (a.s.) from Musa (a.s.), and he (a.s.) taught me clarification from the Evangel, and he (a.s.) said: '**If you come across Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), then convey the greetings to him (s.a.w.a.) from me (a.s.).**' So, Isa (a.s.), O Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), conveys the greetings unto you (s.a.w.a.)." When it was the day of Al-Hareer (battle of Siffeen), he came to Amir Al-Momineen (a.s.) during his (a.s.) battle and said to him (a.s.), "O successor (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.)! We find in the Books of the Prophets (a.s.) that the short-haired one is the successor (a.s.) of Muhammad (s.a.w.a.), best of the people. Uncover your (a.s.) head". He (a.s.) uncovered his (a.s.) helmet from his (a.s.) head and said: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! I (a.s.) am that, O Haam!**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 9. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abu Dawood Al Mustariq, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Seyf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy who said, 'I and Al Mufazzal Bin Umar were in the house of Abu Abdullah (a.s.). He (a.s.) said: '**O maid! To me (a.s.) with such and such bag!**' She brought out the bag and Abu Abdullah (a.s.) raised it to them and said: '**Do you recognise it?**' They said, 'That is it!' He (a.s.) said: '**I (a.s.) was needy to wealth in the middle of the night so I (a.s.) diverted a man from the Jinn from our (a.s.) Shias and he came to me with this bag from your luggage.**'
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَعْرُوفِ عَنْ أَبِي دَاوُدَ الْمُسْتَرِقِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ الْحَضْرَمِي قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَنَا وَ الْمُفَضَّلُ بْنُ عُمَرَ فِي مَنْزِلِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَقَالَ يَا جَارِيَةُ هَلُمِّي إِلَيَّ بِالْكِيسِ الْفُلَانِي فَأَخْرَجَتِ الْكِيسَ فَرَفَعَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع إِلَيْهِمَا وَ قَالَ تَعْرِفُونَهُ قَالَا هُوَ هُوَ قَالَ إِنِّي احْتَجْتُ إِلَى مَالٍ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ فَوَجَّهْتُ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْجِنِّ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا فَأَتَانِي بِهَذَا الْكِيسِ مِنْ رِحَالِكُمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 9. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abu Dawood Al Mustariq, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Seyf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy who said, 'I and Al Mufazzal Bin Umar were in the house of Abu Abdullah (a.s.).
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O maid! To me (a.s.) with such and such bag!**' She brought out the bag and Abu Abdullah (a.s.) raised it to them and said: '**Do you recognise it?**' They said, 'That is it!' He (a.s.) said: '**I (a.s.) was needy to wealth in the middle of the night so I (a.s.) diverted a man from the Jinn from our (a.s.) Shias and he came to me with this bag from your luggage.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 10. It is narrated to us by Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Abdullah, from Ibn Fazzal, from one of our companions, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) wanting the permission to see him (a.s.), and there were saddlebags at the door in rows, and there were voices which had been raised. Then a group came out to us turbaned with turbans, resembling the generation of one people (similar looking). He (the narrator) said, 'I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), O son (a.s.) of Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! Your (a.s.) permission was delayed today, and I saw a group who came out to us turbaned with turbans, and I did not recognise them'. He (a.s.) said: '**O Sa'ad! Those are your brethren from the Jinn, coming to us (a.s.) asking us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion, and regarding the Permissible(s) and their Prohibitions.**'
arabicText: 10- حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ بَعْضٍ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَعْدِ الْإِسْكَافِ قَالَ : أَتَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَ أُرِيدُ الْإِذْنَ عَلَيْهِ فَإِذَا رَوَاحِلُ عَلَى الْبَابِ مَصْفُوفَةٌ وَ إِذَا أَصْوَاتٌ قَدِ ارْتَفَعَتْ فَخَرَجَ عَلَى قَوْمٌ مُعْتَمُونَ بِالْعَمَالِمِ يُشْبِهُونَ الرُّطْ قَالَ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ يَا ابْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أَبْطَأَ إِذْنُكَ الْيَوْمَ وَ قَدْ رَأَيْتُ قَوْماً خَرَجُوا عَلَيَّ مُعْتَمِّينَ بِالْعَمَائِمِ فَأَنْكَرْتُهُمْ فَقَالَ يَا سَعْدُ أُولَئِكَ إِخْوَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ يَأْتُونَا يَسْأَلُونَنَا عَنْ مَعَالِمِ دِينِهِمْ وَ عَنْ حَلَالِهِمْ وَ حَرَامِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 10. It is narrated to us by Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Abdullah, from Ibn Fazzal, from one of our companions, from Sa'ad Al Iskaf who said, 'I came to Abu Ja'far (a.s.) wanting the permission to see him (a.s.), and there were saddlebags at the door in rows, and there were voices which had been raised. Then a group came out to us turbaned with turbans, resembling the generation of one people (similar looking). He (the narrator) said, 'I entered to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and said, 'May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), O son (a.s.) of Rasool-Allah (s.a.w.a.)! Your (a.s.) permission was delayed today, and I saw a group who came out to us turbaned with turbans, and I did not recognise them'.
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: '**O Sa'ad! Those are your brethren from the Jinn, coming to us (a.s.) asking us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion, and regarding the Permissible(s) and their Prohibitions.**'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 11. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ammar Bin Marwan, from Al Munakhal Bin Jameel, from Jabir who said, 'We were at the door of Abu Ja'far (a.s.) when a group of people emerged who were resembling the 'Zutt' (a type of people). I entered to see him (a.s.) and said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! A group of people just emerged who are resembling the Zutt!" He (a.s.) said: "**O Jabir! Those are your brothers from the Jinn, they come to ask us (a.s.) regarding their Permissibles and their Prohibitions.**"'
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنِ الْمُنَخَّلِ بْنِ جَمِيلٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: كُنَّا عَلَى بَابِ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَخَرَجَ عَلَيْنَا قَوْمٌ يُشْبِهُونَ الزُّطَّ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ خَرَجَ عَلَيْنَا قَوْمٌ يُشْبِهُونَ الزُّطَّ قَالَ يَا جَابِرُ أُولَئِكَ إِخْوَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ يَأْتُونَا يَسْأَلُونَنَا عَنْ حَلَالِهِمْ وَ حَرَامِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 11. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ammar Bin Marwan, from Al Munakhal Bin Jameel, from Jabir who said, 'We were at the door of Abu Ja'far (a.s.) when a group of people emerged who were resembling the 'Zutt' (a type of people). I entered to see him (a.s.) and said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! A group of people just emerged who are resembling the Zutt!"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**O Jabir! Those are your brothers from the Jinn, they come to ask us (a.s.) regarding their Permissibles and their Prohibitions.**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 12. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim, from Salih Bin Saeed, from a man from his companions who said, 'I entered to see Abu Abdullah (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! There was a very intense incident (issue) for me." He (a.s.) said: "**Did you come across a black dog or a black wolf?**" I said: "Yes." He (a.s.) said: "**That was the postman of the Jinn, to whom we (a.s.) gave an instruction so he conveyed it to them on your behalf.**"'
arabicText: 12- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ كَانَ لِي أَمْرٌ شَدِيدٌ فَقَالَ أَمَرَّ بِكَ كَلْبٌ أَسْوَدُ أَوْ ذِئْبٌ أَسْوَدُ فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ فَقَالَ ذَلِكَ بَرِيدُ الْجِنِّ أَمَرْنَاهُ فَأَدَّى إِلَيْهِمْ عَنْكَ.
thaqalaynSanad: 12. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Muhammad, from Ibrahim Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Hashim, from Salih Bin Saeed, from a man from his companions who said, 'I entered to see Abu Abdullah (a.s.) and said to him (a.s.), "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! There was a very intense incident (issue) for me."
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**Did you come across a black dog or a black wolf?**" I said: "Yes." He (a.s.) said: "**That was the postman of the Jinn, to whom we (a.s.) gave an instruction so he conveyed it to them on your behalf.**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abu Dawood Al Mustariq, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazaz, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali who said, 'I was seeking permission to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and I heard a noise of a group. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) permitted for me, and I entered and there wasn't anyone in the house. I said, "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! I heard a noise of a group and I don't see anyone!" He (a.s.) said: "**O Abu Hamza! These are your brethren from the Jinn who come to us (a.s.) to ask us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion.**"'
arabicText: 13- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَامِرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ أَبِي دَاوُدَ الْمُسْتَرِقِ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ الْخَزَّازِ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثَّمَالِيِّ قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَسْتَأْذِنُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ سَمِعْتُ حِسَّ قَوْمٍ فَأَذِنَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع فَدَخَلْتُ وَ لَيْسَ فِي الْبَيْتِ أَحَدٌ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ سَمِعْتُ حِسَّ قَوْمٍ وَ لَا أَرَى أَحَداً فَقَالَ يَا أَبَا حَمْزَةَ هَؤُلَاءِ إِخْوَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ يَأْتُونَا لِيَسْأَلُونَا عَنْ مَعَالِمِ دِينِهِمْ.
thaqalaynSanad: 13. It is narrated to us by Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ibn Marouf, from Abu Dawood Al Mustariq, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazaz, from Abu Hamza Al Sumali who said, 'I was seeking permission to see Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and I heard a noise of a group. Abu Ja'far (a.s.) permitted for me, and I entered and there wasn't anyone in the house. I said, "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! I heard a noise of a group and I don't see anyone!"
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**O Abu Hamza! These are your brethren from the Jinn who come to us (a.s.) to ask us (a.s.) regarding the matters of their Religion.**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Abu Hamza who said, 'I was with Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and he (a.s.) was travelling between Makkah and Al-Medina when he (a.s.) turned and there was a dog to his (a.s.) left. So he (a.s.) said: "**What is the matter with you, may Allah (a.z.w.j.) Uglify you! How hasty you are!**" So he resembled a bird. I said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! What is it?" He (a.s.) said: "**It is the postman of the Jinn. Hisham died right now, so he is flying out giving the news of his death.**"'
arabicText: 14- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ هُوَ يَسِيرُ بَيْنَ مَكَّةَ وَ الْمَدِينَةِ فَالْتَفَتَ فَإِذَا كَلْبٌ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ فَقَالَ مَا لَكَ قَبَّحَكَ اللَّهُ مَا أَعْجَلَكَ فَشَبَّهَ بِالطَّائِرِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ مَا هَذَا قَالَ بَرِيدُ الْجِنِّ مَاتَ هِشَامٌ السَّاعَةَ فَهُوَ يَطِيرُ يَنْعَاهُ.
thaqalaynSanad: 14. It is narrated to us by Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Abu Hamza who said, 'I was with Abu Ja'far (a.s.) and he (a.s.) was travelling between Makkah and Al-Medina when he (a.s.) turned and there was a dog to his (a.s.) left.
thaqalaynMatn: So he (a.s.) said: "**What is the matter with you, may Allah (a.z.w.j.) Uglify you! How hasty you are!**" So he resembled a bird. I said: "May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.)! What is it?" He (a.s.) said: "**It is the postman of the Jinn. Hisham died right now, so he is flying out giving the news of his death.**"'

chapter: Chapter 18: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), and That the Jinn Come to Them (a.s.) Asking Them (a.s.) About the Teachings of Their Religion and They (a.s.) Send Them Regarding Their (a.s.) Needs and Recognise Them
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 15. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Musa Bin Sa'dan, from Abdullah Bin Al Qasim, from Ammar Bin Yasir, who said, 'I was in front of Abu Abdullah (a.s.) when he (a.s.) turned towards his (a.s.) right and there was a man who said, 'I seek refuge with Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His (a.z.w.j.) Messenger (s.a.w.a.)!' He (a.s.) said: "**As for Allah (a.z.w.j.), yes, but as for the Messenger (s.a.w.a.), no. I (a.s.) ask you by the One (a.z.w.j.) who if you did not obey Him (a.z.w.j.), He (a.z.w.j.) will Punish you! What are you?**" I looked but did not see anyone. I heard a voice: "We are from the Jinn of the valleys. We have come to Islam (surrendered), so we (desire to) ask you (a.s.) regarding our Religion." He (a.s.) said: "**Ask.**" So they asked him (a.s.) regarding what they needed.'
arabicText: 15- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ سَعْدَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ يَاسِرٍ قَالَ: بَيْنَا أَنَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع إِذِ الْتَفَتَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ فَإِذَا رَجُلٌ يَقُولُ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَ بِرَسُولِهِ قَالَ أَمَّا بِاللَّهِ فَنَعَمْ وَ أَمَّا بِرَسُولِهِ فَلَا أَسْأَلُكَ بِالَّذِي إِنْ لَمْ تُطِعْهُ عَذَّبَكَ مَنْ أَنْتَ قَالَ فَنَظَرْتُ فَلَمْ أَرَ أَحَداً وَ سَمِعْتُ صَوْتَاً نَحْنُ مِنْ جِنِّ الْأَوْدِيَةِ أَسْلَمْنَا فَنَسْأَلُكَ عَنْ دِينِنَا قَالَ سَلُوا فَسَأَلُوهُ عَمَّا احْتَاجُوا إِلَيْهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 15. It is narrated to us by Muhammad Bin Al Hussein, from Musa Bin Sa'dan, from Abdullah Bin Al Qasim, from Ammar Bin Yasir, who said, 'I was in front of Abu Abdullah (a.s.) when he (a.s.) turned towards his (a.s.) right and there was a man who said, 'I seek refuge with Allah (a.z.w.j.) and His (a.z.w.j.) Messenger (s.a.w.a.)!'
thaqalaynMatn: He (a.s.) said: "**As for Allah (a.z.w.j.), yes, but as for the Messenger (s.a.w.a.), no. I (a.s.) ask you by the One (a.z.w.j.) who if you did not obey Him (a.z.w.j.), He (a.z.w.j.) will Punish you! What are you?**" I looked but did not see anyone. I heard a voice: "We are from the Jinn of the valleys. We have come to Islam (surrendered), so we (desire to) ask you (a.s.) regarding our Religion." He (a.s.) said: "**Ask.**" So they asked him (a.s.) regarding what they needed.'

chapter: Chapter 19: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) are Treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the Sky and the Earth Upon His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Ahwazy, from Ibn Asbat, from his father, from Sowrat Bin Kuleyb who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) are treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in His (a.z.w.j.) skies and His (a.z.w.j.) earth, neither upon gold nor upon silver, only upon His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge.**"
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْأَهْوَازِي عَنِ ابْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ سَوْرَةَ بْنِ كُلَيْبٍ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا خَزَانُ اللَّهِ فِي سَمَائِهِ وَ أَرْضِهِ لَا عَلَى ذهَبٍ وَ لَا عَلَى فِضَّةٍ إِلَّا عَلَى عِلْمِهِ.
thaqalaynSanad: 1. It is narrated to us by Ahmad, from Al Ahwazy, from Ibn Asbat, from his father, from Sowrat Bin Kuleyb who said, 'Abu Ja'far (a.s.) said to me:
thaqalaynMatn: "**By Allah (a.z.w.j.)! We (a.s.) are treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in His (a.z.w.j.) skies and His (a.z.w.j.) earth, neither upon gold nor upon silver, only upon His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge.**"

chapter: Chapter 19: Regarding the Imams (a.s.), They (a.s.) are Treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the Sky and the Earth Upon His (a.z.w.j.) Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
englishText: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Abu Abdullah Al Barqy, from Khalaf Bin Hammad, from Zareeh Al Muhariby, from Al Sumaly, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said: "**From us (a.s.) are treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the earth and His (a.z.w.j.) treasurers in the sky. We (a.s.) aren't treasurers upon gold nor silver.**"
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِي عَنْ خَلَفِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ ذَرِيحِ الْمُحَارِنِي عَنِ الثَّمَالِي عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: إِنَّ مِنَّا لَخَزَنَةَ اللَّهِ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَ خَزَنَتَهُ فِي السَّمَاءِ لَسْنَا بِحُزَانٍ عَلَى ذَهَبٍ وَ لَا فِضَّةٍ.
thaqalaynSanad: 2. It is narrated to us by Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Abu Abdullah Al Barqy, from Khalaf Bin Hammad, from Zareeh Al Muhariby, from Al Sumaly, 'From Abu Ja'far (a.s.) having said:
thaqalaynMatn: "**From us (a.s.) are treasurers of Allah (a.z.w.j.) in the earth and His (a.z.w.j.) treasurers in the sky. We (a.s.) aren't treasurers upon gold nor silver.**"
